heading: C1|W2|S1 – Attack the Defender: Directional 1v1 → 2v2 Transition (part 1)
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 8. U12 Training Sessions > U12 Session Structure > C1|W2|S1 – Attack the Defender: Directional 1v1 → 2v2 Transition
content:
(10–12 players | 70 min | U12 | Croatian U10–11 foundation)
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1U12 | Cycle 1 – Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 2 – Attack the Defender – Directional 1v1 → 2v2
Session Outcomes
Players learn to attack defenders with intent, recognise space behind or beside them, and combine directionally into 2v2 situations.
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup & DescriptionPairs dribbling in 20 × 20 m grid. On whistle, Player A drives directly at B, performs a feint and accelerates past. Rotate roles. Add competition – 1 point per clean beat.Coaching Focus• Attack at speed and commit defender
• Feint early, change pace and direction
• “Go through them, not around them” mindset
Technical / Positional (15–20 mins)
Setup & Description3 channels (12 × 15 m). Attacker (A) starts with ball, defender (D) 5 m ahead. On signal, A drives forward to attack D in a straight 1v1. If A beats D and dribbles over end line → point. After 2–3 rounds, progress to 2v2 in same space (support player joins when A passes halfway).
Coaching Focus• Direct dribble – positive first touch
• Recognise defender’s stance – outside or inside space
• Attack defender’s front foot and break line
• Add support timing in 2v2
Tactical / Main (20–25 mins)
Setup & Description30 × 25 m divided into thirds (defensive / middle / attacking). 3v3 + 2 floaters (wide or behind goals). Teams attack directionally toward mini-goals. Play starts with ball to wide floater, first attacker drives 1v1; teammate joins to create 2v2. Rotate after 3–4 attacks.Coaching Focus• Create 1v1 – recognise isolated defender• If second attacker arrives → 2v2 combination (wall pass, overlap, set–play)• Attack with tempo – defender must react, not dictate• Belco cue: “Drive to draw – combine to break.”
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup & Description6v6 on 40 × 30 m pitch, mini-goals both ends. Encourage quick 1v1 drives when isolated, reward 2 points for goals scored from 1v1 or 2v2 actions.Coaching Focus• Recognise when to attack direct• Maintain compact support underneath• Observe transitions – regain → drive forward
Coaching Information
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Build confidence to attack defenders and recognise direction to goal.
Technical Detail | • Strong first touch forward • Feint and burst • Change pace • Combine after beating defender
Coaching Messages | “Attack the defender’s feet – make them move.” / “Go forward with intent.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Slow approach → push tempo
Praise Language | “Love that drive – you made them react!” / “Perfect timing – drew and played.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery in duels
Reflection Question | “When is the best time to combine instead of staying 1v1?”
Footer:Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
PART 2 – EXTENDED COACH PACK VERSION
Session Header
Cycle 1 – Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 2 – Attack the Defender – Directional 1v1 → 2v2
Session Overview
Objective: Develop players’ ability to attack defenders at speed, commit pressure, and make forward decisions — individually or in pairs.Development Logic: Draws from Croatian U10–11 principle of learning football through the ball — repetition through duels, fast tempo, realistic direction. Builds directly from prior sessions (1v1 mastery, shield & turn). Adds decision on when to drive alone vs combine.Belco Connection: Encourages bravery (attack mentality) and intelligence (timing of support) — building toward Tempo Control and Overload principles.
Practice 1 – Intro / Activation: “Speed Feints”
Setup:• Area: 20 × 20 m grid• Organisation: pairs with one ball• Equipment: cones and mini-goals for direction markersDescription:Players face each other; A drives at B and performs a feint before sprinting past. Rotate after each attempt. Count points for successful beats.Coaching Detail:• Positive first touch toward defender• Feint early – before contact• Explosive change of pace after move• Keep ball close in final touchCommon Mistakes & Fixes:• Dribbling too slow → shorten steps before move• Move too close → execute 1–2 m before defenderProgression:Add mini-goals 5 m behind B – A must dribble through for score.
Practice 2 – Technical / Positional: “Directional 1v1 → 2v2”
Setup:• Area: 3 lanes of 12 × 15 m• Players: 1 attacker + 1 defender per lane• Equipment: cones, small gates on end linesDescription:Attacker drives at defender from halfway; attempts to beat and cross line. Add second attacker and defender after first wave to create 2v2. Focus on supporting run timing.Coaching Detail:• First touch forward and direct• Attack defender’s front foot• Combine (set–play / wall pass) once second player arrives• Recognise trigger: teammate joins → combineCommon Mistakes & Fixes:• Drifting sideways → keep goal direction• Passing early → draw firstProgression:Convert to 2v2 live with scoring mini-goals.
Practice 3 – Main Game / Tactical Application: “3v3 + 2 Floaters – Attack to Combine”

heading: C1|W2|S1 – Attack the Defender: Directional 1v1 → 2v2 Transition (part 2)
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 8. U12 Training Sessions > U12 Session Structure > C1|W2|S1 – Attack the Defender: Directional 1v1 → 2v2 Transition
content:
Setup:• Area: 30 × 25 m, divided into thirds• Teams: 3v3 inside + 2 floaters (wide or behind goals)• Goals: 1 m mini-goals on each endDescription:Ball starts from floater → first attacker drives 1v1. If teammate joins, becomes 2v2 to goal. Rotate teams quickly for intensity.Tactical Purpose:Encourage direct attack, drawing defenders before combining to create overloads. Reinforces “Drive–Draw–Play” rhythm.Coaching Detail:• Create isolation (space and timing)• Attack defender – commit before release• Combine only after pressure drawn• Support run must arrive forward, not flatVariations / Adjustments:• Touch limit for floaters (2-touch)• Add third support player for 3v2 transitionsBelco Cue:
“Drive to draw – combine to break.”
Practice 4 – End Game / Transfer: “Small-Sided Drive Game”
Setup:• 6v6 on 40 × 30 m field, two mini-goals each end• Normal game, bonus points for goals from 1v1 or 2v2 duelsDescription:Encourage players to recognise isolation moments and attack decisively.Observation Focus:• Direct dribbling into space• Combination timing once pressure drawn• Compactness behind ball on loss
Summary Notes
Key Takeaways:Attackers must drive forward to commit; once the defender reacts, combine to exploit.Behaviours to Reward:Positive body shape to goal | Bravery in duels | Timing of support run.Session Progression Logic:1v1 mastery → 2v2 decision → collective overload creation.

heading: C1|W2|S2 – Press Resistance Dribbling: Control Under Pressure
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 8. U12 Training Sessions > U12 Session Structure > C1|W2|S2 – Press Resistance Dribbling: Control Under Pressure
content:
(10–12 players | 70 min | U12 | Croatian U10–11 foundation)
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2U12 | Cycle 1 – Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 3 – Press Resistance Dribbling: Control Under Pressure
Session Outcomes
Players learn to stay calm under pressure, use tight control to protect the ball, and escape using body shape and quick touches.
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup & Description4 players per grid (15 × 15 m). Two players act as pressers; two keep possession in a restricted square (3 × 3 m). Work 20 s on / 20 s off, rotate.Coaching Focus• Stay calm under pressure – soft touches• Use body to shield and turn away• Scan early – prepare escape route
Technical / Positional (15–20 mins)
Setup & DescriptionTwo 8 × 10 m boxes side by side. In each, 3 attackers vs 1 defender (rondo style). Limit 2 touches. Focus on first touch direction to avoid pressure. Switch pressers every minute.Coaching Focus• First touch away from pressure• Use sole or inside foot to control tempo• Scan before receiving – see pressure
Tactical / Main (20–25 mins)
Setup & Description20 × 25 m pitch split in two halves. Play 4v4 + 2 neutrals (one each side). Teams keep possession 5 passes = 1 point; extra point if press is broken by dribble past opponent. Rotate neutrals every 3 min.Coaching Focus• Recognise pressure direction – play opposite• Invite press then escape through carry or pass• Support compact – give outlet under pressure• Belco cue: “Be calm to break.”
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup & Description6v6 on 40 × 30 m. Add rule: team earns bonus point if they beat an opponent’s press and play forward within 5 seconds.Coaching Focus• Observe calmness in first touch• Encourage escape runs and angles• Reward composed decision after press is broken
Coaching Information
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Improve players’ control under pressure and confidence to dribble or pass out of tight areas.
Technical Detail | • First touch direction • Body shape side-on • Use sole/inside for control • Calm tempo to invite press
Coaching Messages | “Be calm to break.” / “Use pressure to open space.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Panicking → slow down first touch
Praise Language | “Excellent first touch – you bought time.” / “Perfect escape – you used their press.”
Behaviours to Reward | Composure under contact
Reflection Question | “What helps you decide whether to dribble or pass out of pressure?”
Footer:Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
PART 2 – EXTENDED COACH PACK VERSION
Session Header
Cycle 1 – Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 3 – Press Resistance Dribbling: Control Under Pressure
Session Overview
Objective: Teach players to retain possession when pressed by opponents through body position, soft touch, and decision-making.Development Logic: Croatian U10–11 emphasises ball control under pressure – repetition in tight spaces, low centre of gravity, and decision rhythm. Links to previous sessions on shield & turn and 1v1 duels.Belco Connection: Trains bravery to receive under pressure and intelligence to decide when to carry vs play out – foundation for Tempo Control and Compactness.
Practice 1 – Intro / Activation: “Box Escape”
Setup:• Area: 15 × 15 m grid, 4 players per grid• Organisation: 2 pressers, 2 keepers of possession• Equipment: cones, 4 ballsDescription:2 attackers keep the ball inside a 3 × 3 m square while pressers apply passive pressure for 20 s, then active. Rotate roles every minute.Coaching Detail:• Stay calm, protect with body and turn away• Use sole to change direction under contact• Play off first touch if space appears• Encourage verbal support between playersCommon Mistakes & Fixes:• Panicking touch → “soft touch buys time”• Turning into pressure → scan firstProgression:Add escape gate – dribble through when space opens for bonus point.
Practice 2 – Technical / Positional: “3v1 Escape Rondos”
Setup:• Area: 8 × 10 m boxes (2–3 grids)• Players: 3 attackers + 1 defender each• Equipment: cones, ballsDescription:Classic rondo with pressing intensity. Attackers must use controlled first touch and shoulder checks to play away from pressure. Change defender each minute.Coaching Detail:• Body shape to see two sides• First touch toward space• Pass with calm tempo – don’t rush• Use defender’s press to open laneCommon Mistakes & Fixes:• Hard first touch → “caress the ball”• Flat body shape → side-on to receiveProgression:Allow dribble out of box after 5 passes – links to press escape.
Practice 3 – Main Game / Tactical Application: “4v4 + 2 Neutrals – Beat the Press”
Setup:• Area: 20 × 25 m split in two halves• Players: 4v4 inside + 2 wide neutrals• Goals: none – possession score systemDescription:Teams keep possession 5 passes = 1 pt; dribbling past an opponent under pressure = extra pt. Rotate neutrals every 3 min.Tactical Purpose:Teach players to invite pressure and escape using body position or quick dribble – building composure and decision under stress.Coaching Detail:• First touch sets angle to see field• Recognise press direction → escape opposite• Compact support – stay within 8 m• After escape – play forward immediatelyVariations / Adjustments:• Add 2 target goals for forward pass reward• Limit neutrals to 2 touch to increase speedBelco Cue:
“Stay calm to escape – let them chase shadows.”
Practice 4 – End Game / Transfer: “6v6 Press Breaker”
Setup:• 40 × 30 m field with 2 mini-goals each end• Normal 6v6 rules, bonus pt for goals from build-ups starting under press.Description:Observe players using press resistance skills in flow.Observation Focus:• Body shape and calm first touch• Using defender’s momentum to turn• Compact support for short pass out of press
Summary Notes
Key Takeaways: Be calm under pressure, use first touch to escape, support close and compact.Behaviours to Reward: Composure, awareness, tempo control after escape.Session Progression Logic: From tight technical duels → possession control → realistic press situations.

heading: C1|W3|S1 – Drive to Commit: Creating Advantage Through Pressure (part 1)
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 8. U12 Training Sessions > U12 Session Structure > C1|W3|S1 – Drive to Commit: Creating Advantage Through Pressure
content:
(U12 | 10–12 players | 70 minutes | Croatian U10–11 foundation | Belco identity applied)
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2U12 | Cycle 1 – Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 3 – Drive to Commit: Creating Advantage Through Pressure
Session Outcomes
Players learn to drive forward with intent, commit defenders to create space for teammates, and recognise the moment to release or continue.
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup & DescriptionPairs dribbling inside 20 × 20 m grid. One player acts as “lead driver,” other shadows 1 m behind. On whistle, leader accelerates, feints, or stops suddenly — partner mirrors movements. Swap every 45 s.Coaching Focus• Drive forward with purpose• Control tempo – change pace suddenly• Use body to disguise intention
Technical / Positional (15–20 mins)
Setup & Description3 lanes (15 × 12 m). Each attacker drives directly at passive defender, aiming to make them commit before passing to next line. Add support player to create 2v1 once defender steps.Coaching Focus• Drive until defender commits• Pass only when defender engaged• Recognise free man – “draw and play”
Tactical / Main (20–25 mins)
Setup & Description25 × 20 m pitch split into two halves. 3v3 + 2 neutrals (one wide, one behind goal). Attackers score by passing to behind-goal neutral or dribbling through gate after drawing a defender.Coaching Focus• Drive to commit opponent – fix before releasing• Play into or beyond the committed defender• Support quickly under the ball for rebound or bounce• Belco cue: “Fix one, free one.”
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup & Description6v6 on 40 × 30 m. Normal play with rule: team earns double points if goal comes after a drive that draws pressure before pass or finish.Coaching Focus• Recognise when to carry to attract pressure• Observe body language of defender• Reward bravery to commit opponent
Coaching Information
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Improve ability to drive forward, attract defenders, and exploit created space.
Technical Detail | • First touch forward • Controlled tempo • Draw before release • Body shape side-on • Awareness of next action
Coaching Messages | “Fix before you free.” / “Drive with purpose, not panic.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Passing too early → encourage one more touch
Praise Language | “Perfect timing – you drew him and freed your mate.” / “That’s brave driving – made them commit.”
Behaviours to Reward | Positive forward carries
Reflection Question | “When should you keep driving instead of releasing?”
Footer:Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
PART 2 – EXTENDED COACH PACK VERSION
Session Header
Cycle 1 – Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 3 – Drive to Commit: Creating Advantage Through Pressure
Session Overview
Objective: Teach players to commit defenders through controlled forward carries, forcing decisions and creating space for teammates.Development Logic: Croatian U10–11 methodology prioritises carrying under pressure to create superiority – repetition through movement, timing, and visual awareness. Builds on “Press Resistance Dribbling” and introduces decision-making for “Drive–Draw–Play.”Belco Connection: Links bravery (drive), compactness (support under ball), and intelligence (timing of release) – directly aligned with Belco’s Overload & Tempo Control identity.
Practice 1 – Intro / Activation: “Mirror Drive”
Setup:• Area: 20 × 20 m grid• Organisation: pairs with one ball each• Equipment: cones for boundariesDescription:Leader drives, feints, stops, or accelerates; partner mirrors 1 m behind. After each round, switch leader/follower. Add ball to mirror phase: both dribble, matching tempo and changes of direction.Coaching Detail:• Vary tempo – slow → fast → stop• Use strong body lean when driving• Eyes up, control with laces or inside• Change rhythm to unbalance defenderCommon Mistakes & Fixes:• Head down → encourage scanning• Constant speed → demand tempo variationProgression:Convert to 1v1 chase – leader breaks through gate, chaser tries to tag.
Practice 2 – Technical / Positional: “Drive–Draw–Play Lanes”
Setup:• Area: 3 lanes (15 × 12 m)• Organisation: 1 attacker, 1 defender, 1 target player• Equipment: cones and mini-goalsDescription:Attacker drives directly at defender. Defender must stay in lane. Once defender commits, attacker passes to target player behind them or dribbles through gate. Rotate every 60 s.Coaching Detail:• Drive with purpose – eyes up• Use defender’s movement as cue to release• If defender delays – keep driving• Support player time run to arrive on releaseCommon Mistakes & Fixes:• Pass too early → drive one more step• Turn sideways → face forward longerProgression:Add second attacker – 2v1 once defender steps, promoting draw and combination play.
Practice 3 – Main Game / Tactical Application: “3v3 + 2 Neutrals – Fix & Free”

heading: C1|W3|S1 – Drive to Commit: Creating Advantage Through Pressure (part 2)
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 8. U12 Training Sessions > U12 Session Structure > C1|W3|S1 – Drive to Commit: Creating Advantage Through Pressure
content:
Setup:• Area: 25 × 20 m (divided into two halves)• Players: 3v3 + 2 neutrals (wide and deep)• Goals: 1 m mini-gates at each endDescription:Teams attack directionally. Player drives at defender; if defender steps, attacker releases pass to teammate or neutral to break line. Play restarts from neutral. Rotate every 4 min.Tactical Purpose:Reinforce concept of creating advantage through pressure — drive to commit a defender, freeing another teammate. Builds decision-making and timing under realistic tempo.Coaching Detail:• Attack front foot – make defender decide• Release immediately after commitment• Support underneath for bounce or recovery• Stay compact – ready to press if lostVariations / Adjustments:• Add third neutral inside for 3v3 + 3 overloads• One-touch finish rule for increased tempoBelco Cue:
“Fix one, free one.”
Practice 4 – End Game / Transfer: “Free Drive Game”
Setup:• Area: 40 × 30 m• Format: 6v6, 2 mini-goals each end• Scoring: bonus pt if a goal follows a forward drive that draws defender before releaseDescription:Normal game play; focus on recognising driving triggers and releasing at correct time.Observation Focus:• Controlled forward carries to attract pressure• Timing of pass when defender steps• Compact reaction to lost possession
Summary Notes
Key Takeaways: Drive with intent to attract pressure; commit before you play; create advantage through decision and timing.Behaviours to Reward: Forward drives | Calm awareness | Bravery to commit under pressure.Session Progression Logic: From repetition of drive mechanics → recognising commitment cue → applying in live play.
Footer:Belconnen United FC – The Belco Way: Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: C1|W3|S2 – Mini Tournament: Duels & Transitions
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 8. U12 Training Sessions > U12 Session Structure > C1|W3|S2 – Mini Tournament: Duels & Transitions
content:
PART 1 – COACH-LITE SESSION PLAN (PRINT VERSION)
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2U12 | Cycle 1 – Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 3 – Mini Tournament: Duels & Transitions
Session Outcomes
Players compete in repeated 1v1 → 2v2 duels and fast transitions, applying dribbling, shielding and turning skills under match pressure.
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup & Description4 × 4 m mini-grids, one ball per pair. Players play 1v1 for 15 s then rest 15 s. Score by dribbling over any side. Quick reset and new round.Coaching Focus• Explosive start and reaction to turnover• Stay low – protect on contact• Recover immediately when losing the ball
Technical / Positional (15–20 mins)
Setup & DescriptionTwo 12 × 10 m zones side-by-side. Play 2v2 continuous: winners stay, challengers rotate. After each duel, the ball immediately transitions—losers defend next wave.Coaching Focus• Positive first touch to attack• React instantly when possession changes• Compact support – 1 sec reaction rule
Tactical / Main (20–25 mins)
Setup & DescriptionThree 25 × 20 m mini-pitches. Format: 3v3 with 2 mini-goals per end. Round-robin mini-tournament; 3–4 min games, 1 min changeover. Winner stays, rotate others.Coaching Focus• Win your duel – attack first• Transition fast – nearest player presses• Play forward within 3 sec of regain• Belco cue: “Win it → Go now.”
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup & DescriptionFinal 6v6 tournament: two teams on, one resting. Score = goal + bonus pt for winning individual duel leading to goal.Coaching Focus• Observe intensity & reaction after loss• Encourage brave 1v1 decisions• Reward tempo control after regain
Coaching Information
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Apply dribbling, shielding and turning skills in live duels and quick transition moments.
Technical Detail | • Explosive first touch • Protect with body • Quick reaction on turnover • Tempo change after regain
Coaching Messages | “Win your duel – change the game.” / “Lose it – react fast.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Ball exposed → use arm bar
Praise Language | “Brave duel – you won it clean.” / “Love that instant press.”
Behaviours to Reward | Duel bravery
Reflection Question | “What happens if we hesitate after losing the ball?”
Footer:Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
PART 2 – EXTENDED COACH PACK VERSION
Session Header
Cycle 1 – Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 3 – Mini Tournament: Duels & Transitions
Session Overview
Objective: Consolidate Cycle 1 learning through competitive duels and fast transitions, emphasising reaction speed and control under pressure.Development Logic: Croatian U10–11 method prioritises learning football through the ball – repetition of 1v1 skills, coordination, and quick recovery behaviours. This session tests those fundamentals in realistic, competitive flow.Belco Connection: Reinforces bravery in duels, compactness after turnover, and intelligent tempo shifts – core identity of Belco football.
Practice 1 – Intro / Activation: “1v1 Quick Fire Duels”
Setup:• Area: 4 × 4 m grids (4–6 setups)• Organisation: pairs, one ball each• Equipment: cones, spare ballsDescription:Players compete for 15 s duels; score by dribbling out of grid. Reset quickly. Work-rest 1:1.Coaching Detail:• Fast feet – attack first touch• Protect & pivot when trapped• Immediate reaction after loss – press back• Encourage energy and competitionCommon Mistakes & Fixes:• Ball too far → tighten touch• No re-press → count “1-sec” ruleProgression:Add directional gate for breakout dribble finish.
Practice 2 – Technical / Positional: “Continuous 2v2 Duels”
Setup:• Two zones 12 × 10 m each• Players: 4 per zone (2v2) + 1 feeder• Equipment: cones, balls, mini-goalsDescription:Feeder plays in ball; 2v2 live for 30 s. Winner stays on; losers immediately defend next wave. Creates continuous transition feeling.Coaching Detail:• Positive first touch after regain• Compact defensive reaction on loss• Communication – who presses first• Tempo change after winCommon Mistakes & Fixes:• Drifting apart → stay close for support• Slow press → trigger with nearest playerProgression:Score only after winning duel + 3 passes – adds patience after chaos.
Practice 3 – Main Game / Tactical Application: “3v3 Mini-Tournament”
Setup:• Three 25 × 20 m mini-pitches• Format: 3v3, 2 mini-goals per end• Scoring: win = 3 pts, draw = 1 ptDescription:Round-robin mini-tournament. Games 3–4 min, 1 min rest/rotation. Each team plays multiple matches. Coach manages tempo and feedback between rounds.Tactical Purpose:Embed dribbling, shielding, and press-reaction habits in competitive flow. Encourage duel focus and fast collective response to transition.Coaching Detail:• Attack duels – brave 1v1 entries• Nearest player presses immediately on loss• Compact triangle support on possession• Transition rule: score within 5 sec = bonus pointVariations / Adjustments:• 1-touch finish only – forces speed of play• Double goals for transition scoresBelco Cue:
“Win it – go now!”
Practice 4 – End Game / Transfer: “6v6 Belco Cup”
Setup:• 40 × 30 m field, 3 teams of 6 rotating (6v6 on, one rest)• Mini-goals each end, 5 min gamesDescription:Play 6v6 matches scoring normally + bonus for goals following duel win or 5 sec transition goal. Track points across tournament.Observation Focus:• Players recognising duel moments• Immediate transition reaction• Tempo control after winning ball
Summary Notes
Key Takeaways: Control under pressure, react fast in transition, and compete to win your duel.Behaviours to Reward: Bravery in 1v1s | Instant reaction on turnover | Compact team shape behind ball.Session Progression Logic: 1v1 duels → continuous 2v2 transitions → tournament competition → transfer game pressure.

heading: 9. U12 Correction & Feedback Language
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 9. U12 Correction & Feedback Language
content:
Purpose
To make feedback consistent and constructive.
In Croatia, correction is viewed as education through rhythm, not interruption.
Coaches correct only when players can immediately re-perform the action — never with long lectures.
U12 Correction Philosophy (Croatian Model)
Type of Feedback | When Used | Croatian Approach | Belco Example Language
Instant (“on the run”) | During flow. Simple motor or awareness correction. | Keep tempo; correct with one word. | “Body!” “Scan!” “Touch!”
Freeze & Recreate | When team spacing or understanding breaks. | Stop briefly, reset shape, replay at correct tempo. | “Hold — look where we are.” “Now play it again.”
Guided Question | To prompt self-correction. | Ask, don’t tell. | “What did you see?” “Could you open earlier?”
Reinforce Success | When idea is right but execution fails. | Praise the behaviour, not just the outcome. | “Right idea — better weight next time.”
Collective Reminder | After pattern breaks repeatedly. | Address group, not individual. | “Team, our spacing is too close — fix it together.”
Croatian Correction Principles
1️⃣ Correct actions, not personalities. – Always coach behaviour.2️⃣ Be specific and short. – One correction per stoppage.3️⃣ Replay immediately. – Learning happens in rhythm.4️⃣ Language = learning. – Use the same cue words you train with.
Belco Common Coaching Language (U12 Level)
Action Type | Correction Cue | Positive Reinforcement
First Touch | “Open body!” “Face forward!” | “Good — you saw it before it came.”
Passing | “Find your mate!” “Play through, not over.” | “Nice — straight line to middle man.”
Dribbling | “Drive at him!” “Protect it!” | “Yes — brave 1v1!”
Support Play | “Stay connected!” “Angle!” | “Good — you helped the open ball.”
Defending | “Close gap!” “Don’t dive!” | “You delayed well — now win it.”
Transition | “React!” “Win it back!” | “Love that — fast five seconds.”
Effort / Mentality | “Stay brave!” “Next action!” | “Excellent — no fear, that’s Belco.”
Tone & Delivery
- Before correction: calm body, clear tone, specific focus.
- During correction: 1–2 words maximum — keep tempo.
- After correction: replay the scenario, then praise the behaviour change.
“Short, sharp, show — then let the game continue.”
That’s the Croatian coaching rhythm: precise, confident, always moving forward.

heading: 10. U12 Game Day Guidance
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 10. U12 Game Day Guidance
content:
Purpose
The match is another training environment — not assessment, not pressure.
Croatians use matchday to reinforce the week’s theme, never to chase a result.
Game Day Objectives
Element | Guidance
Game Intent | Apply the training theme. If the week’s focus was “Dribbling & Ball Control”, the match objective is “Can we own the ball and attack 1v1?”
Coach Voice | Use only the week’s key language cues. Limit sideline talk to one or two repeated triggers — “Be brave!”, “Back–Mid–Front!”, “Win it back!”
Player Guidance | Encourage independence: let them find rhythm. Give simple team prompts — “Can we build?” or “Who’s free?”
Substitution Focus | Rotate evenly. Each player must experience all moments — build-up, defence, transition. Learning > result.
Half-Time Reflection | Ask two questions:
 | 1️⃣ “Did we use what we trained?”
 | 2️⃣ “When did we do it best?”
Coach Behaviour | Calm and positive. React to actions, not scoreline. Praise effort, bravery, decision-making.
Croatian Matchday Principles
1️⃣ “Same words, same game.” – never introduce new commands.
2️⃣ “Observe the idea, not the mistake.” – coach what you see repeatedly.
3️⃣ “Emotion belongs to players; rhythm belongs to the coach.” – your tone sets calm.
Example Belco Match Dialogue
“Keep it, find your mate.”
“Good — you saw the open side.”
“Win it back fast — then play.”
That’s how the Croatians sound — short, calm, and purposeful.

heading: U12 Coach Matchday Standards
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 10. U12 Game Day Guidance > U12 Coach Matchday Standards
content:
Purpose
The coach sets the tone for the match environment.Professional detail builds credibility.Players mirror what they see — composure, preparation, and precision.
Coach Matchday Standards
APPEARANCE
Element | Standard
Belconnen Attire | Official Belconnen polo or approved top. Unified, consistent look across staff.
Footwear | Soccer footwear during warm-up and match. We look like football people, not spectators.
Sunglasses | Remove when speaking to players. Eye contact is essential for message absorption and connection.
Presentation | Neat, professional, deliberate. Our unified appearance signals control and precision.
GEAR
Item | Expectation
Balls | Pumped appropriately before arrival at pitch.
Bibs | Organised and accessible.
Cones | Blue and white matchday cones set and ready.
PREPAREDNESS
Element | Expectation
Substitution Plan | Rotation structure pre-planned. Know your intent before kick-off.
Team Talk | Clear theme linked to weekly focus. No improvisation.
Arrival Time | Minimum 45–60 minutes before kick-off. Field checked and organised.
Sideline Behaviour
Element | Standard
Coach Voice | Calm, clear, rhythmic.
Emotional Control | No visible frustration or emotional reactions.
Referee Interaction | Respectful at all times. Model behaviour.
Opposition Interaction | Professional greeting pre and post match.
Player Interaction | Eye contact, short cues, consistent language from training.
Parent Interaction | Friendly but brief. No tactical debate during game.
Belco Matchday Identity
1️⃣ Calm voice, clear cues.2️⃣ Detail before emotion.3️⃣ Teach relationships, not results.4️⃣ Represent the program with pride.

heading: U12 Pre-Match Warm-Up
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 10. U12 Game Day Guidance > U12 Pre-Match Warm-Up
content:
PRE-MATCH WARM-UP (20-25 MINUTES TOTAL)
Objective: Build rhythm, calm confidence, and connection with the ball — not adrenaline.
At U12–13, the pre-match warm-up is about comfort, confidence, and readiness to play, not structure or tactics.
Its purpose is to help players feel good with the ball, settle emotionally, and arrive at kick-off relaxed and curious.
Warm-ups at this age should:
- include the ball early
- feel playful and familiar
- progress naturally from free movement into simple game actions
- keep coach voice low and positive
Coaches should guide, not control. Use short prompts or questions if needed, but avoid instruction, correction, or pressure. If players are smiling, moving freely, and engaged with the ball, the warm-up is doing its job.
Block | Duration | Focus | Example Activity | Coach Cues / Tone
1️⃣ Movement Prep | 4-5 mins | Coordination, mobility, balance. | Dynamic movements in small grid (skips, lunges, side steps, accelerations). | “Light feet.” “Find rhythm early.” Calm tone.
2️⃣ Ball Familiarity | 4-5 mins | Touch quality and feel for the ball. | 1 ball each: 1v0 touches, feints, turns, 1v1 shadow duels. | “Enjoy the ball.” “Be brave.” “Every touch clean.”
3️⃣ Partner Passing & Movement | 4-5 mins | Technique under rhythm. | Passing pairs, 2-touch, follow movement. Add bounce-pass variation. | “Pass, move, get it back.” “Keep tempo.”
4️⃣ Rondo or Small-Group Possession | 5 mins | Decision + awareness. | 4v2 or 5v2 rondo, vary shape. Focus on scanning and tempo. | “See before you pass.” “Play through, not around.”
5️⃣ Directional Game or Finishing | 5 mins | Confidence and rhythm to goal. | 4v4 to small goals or shooting on move. | “Attack with intent.” “Play forward.” “Enjoy it.”
Detail:
- The last 2–3 minutes are quiet — players refocus and hydrate.
- No lines, no yelling, no chaos.
- The coach’s calm body language sets the match tone.
“Our energy comes from control, not noise.”

heading: U12 Pre-Match Talk
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 10. U12 Game Day Guidance > U12 Pre-Match Talk
content:
PRE-MATCH TALK (less than 4 MINUTES)
Purpose
Anchor the team emotionally and mentally.
Remind them of the week’s theme, give a clear picture of what matters, and build bravery through simplicity.
Structure
Step | Coach Focus | Example Language (Belco tone, Croatian clarity)
1️⃣ Calm & Gather | Lower your voice, draw attention through stillness. | “Eyes on me. Take a breath. We start calm, we stay brave.”
2️⃣ Connect to Training Theme | 1 clear idea that links week to match. | “We worked on keeping the ball and helping the man on it — same today.”
3️⃣ 2–3 Action Reminders | These are your ‘anchors’. | “Win your 1v1s.” “Look before you pass.” “If we lose it — react fast.”
4️⃣ Behaviour Cue | What kind of team do we want to be? | “Play brave, stay together, help the ball.”
5️⃣ Finish Softly but Strong | Leave a calm, focused silence. | “Enjoy the game. Play through, not around.”
Principles for Pre-Match
1️⃣ “Simple, calm, repeatable.”
2️⃣ “Coach behaviour teaches more than words.”
3️⃣ “The pre-match talk starts tempo control.”
Coach tone: quiet, deliberate, short sentences.
No hype — the rhythm of your voice = rhythm of their play.

heading: U12 Half-Time Talk
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 10. U12 Game Day Guidance > U12 Half-Time Talk
content:
HALF-TIME TALK (2–3 MINUTES)
Purpose
Reflect, correct one thing, and reset.No tactical lectures, no emotional outbursts — just re-centre the team on the training theme.
Structure
Step | Coach Focus | Example Language (Belco tone, Croatian clarity)
1️⃣ Calm the Group | Sit, water, short silence. | “Take a breath. Let’s slow it down for a second.”
2️⃣ One Positive (Linked to Theme) | Reinforce behaviour you want repeated. | “We’ve stayed brave and kept trying to play through.”
3️⃣ One Correction (Only One) | Make it simple and actionable. | “When we lose it, we must react faster — no waiting.”
4️⃣ Ask a Question (Ownership) | Simple awareness cue. | “What helped us when we won it back fast?”
5️⃣ Finish with Clarity & Calm | Reset tone for second half. | “Calm start, same idea — brave with the ball, help each other.”
Half-Time Coaching Rules
Rule | Meaning for Coach
1️⃣ One theme, one correction. | Keep clarity. Don’t overload.
2️⃣ Start with calm, finish with clarity. | Emotional regulation = learning retention.
3️⃣ Make it about the behaviour, not the result. | Encourage bravery and rhythm over winning.
4️⃣ Leave silence after you speak. | Let the message settle — don’t fill the air.
Example Complete U12 Half-Time Talk
“Good first half. We kept the ball and stayed brave.
We just need to react faster when we lose it — help each other.
Same idea: calm when we start, brave with the ball, find your friends.
Enjoy the second half.”
Coach Behaviour Summary
Aspect | Croatian Standard for U12s
Voice | Low, calm, rhythmic. Avoid hype.
Body Language | Still, open posture. Crouch to player level.
Focus | Simplicity and repetition of the week’s theme.
Timing | Never longer than 3 minutes.
Message Consistency | Always link back to training — one voice, one theme, every week.
Key Takeaway:
“At U12, the coach teaches tempo with their voice.
The match talk is not about energy — it’s about rhythm and security.”

heading: U12 Pre-Match Routine - Timeline
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 10. U12 Game Day Guidance > U12 Pre-Match Routine - Timeline
content:
Reference: All timings work backwards from KO (Kick-Off)Context: No changerooms · players arrive changed
Time (from KO) | Moment | Coach Focus | Coach Behaviour | Key Reminder
KO–50 | Arrival | Settle & connect | Greet players · light chat · no tactics | Human first
KO–40 | Free Ball Time | Comfort & confidence | Unstructured ball play · fun | Ball removes nerves
KO–30 | Brief Gather | Simple focus | One idea · question-led | One picture only
KO–25 | Warm-Up | Activate through play | Ball-based · minimal voice | Warm up by playing
KO–10 | Final Reminder | Cue recall | One short cue · positive tone | Reminder, not instruction
KO–3 | Game Prep | Emotional calm | Encourage · smile · no talk | Let them play
KO | Kick-Off | Observe & support | Coach less · watch more | Game teaches
⚠️ COACH WARNINGS (U12–13)
Warning Sign | What It Means
Long speeches | Overloading young players
Tactical detail | Age-inappropriate
Constant instructions | Players stop thinking
Emotional intensity | Confidence drops
REMINDER
At U12–13, matchday should feel like training — just with an opponent.

heading: U12 Pre-Match Routine - Detail
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 10. U12 Game Day Guidance > U12 Pre-Match Routine - Detail
content:
🇭🇷 BELCO MATCHDAY BEHAVIOUR STANDARD (U12–13)
EXPANDED VERSION – COACH PACK
PURPOSE (U12–13)
Matchday at this age is about:
- emotional comfort
- confidence with the ball
- curiosity and decision-making
- enjoyment of competition
It is not about:
- results
- systems
- match management
- discipline through control
Croatian methodology treats matches at this age as learning environments, not performance tests.
MATCHDAY FLOW EXPLAINED
🕒 KO–50 | Arrival
Intent: Settle players emotionally
Coach behaviour
- Greet every player
- Casual conversation
- No football information
WhyYoung players cannot receive football detail until they feel safe and settled.
🕒 KO–40 | Free Ball Time
Intent: Remove nerves through the ball
Coach behaviour
- Allow juggling, 1v1s, small games
- No organisation
- No correction unless unsafe
WhyCroatian programs use the ball as the primary emotional regulator.
🕒 KO–30 | Brief Group Gather (2–3 minutes)
Intent: Create a simple focus
Coach behaviour
- One/two ideas only
- Often phrased as a question
Examples
- “When we can face forward, what do we do?”
- “How do we help the player on the ball?”
- “What do we do when we lose it?”
WhyQuestions activate thinking without pressure.
🕒 KO–25 | Warm-Up (Ball-Based)
Intent: Activate body and brain together
Coach behaviour
- Ball involved immediately
- Small-sided, playful activities
- Minimal instruction
WhyWarm-up should look like football, not drills.
🕒 KO–10 | Final Reminder
Intent: Recall a familiar cue
Coach behaviour
- One short reminder
- Calm, positive tone
Examples
- “Can we help the player on the ball?”
- “Remember our spacing.”
- “Be brave.”
WhyThis is a reminder, not instruction.
🕒 KO–3 | Game Prep
Intent: Emotional calm
Coach behaviour
- Encourage
- Smile
- No last instructions
WhySilence signals trust.
🕒 KO | Kick-Off
Intent: Let the game teach
Coach behaviour
- Observe more than instruct
- Intervene only for:
- safety
- emotional support
- very brief correction
Croatian principle
Too much coach voice = less player learning.
COACH BEHAVIOUR STANDARDS (U12–13)
Do
- Be calm and positive
- Use simple language
- Let players try and fail
- Reinforce effort, not outcome
Avoid
- Sideline coaching
- Tactical explanations
- Emotional reactions to mistakes
- Overuse of stoppages
“If you coach a lot on matchday at U12–13, you are stealing learning moments.”

heading: U12 Game Framework
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 10. U12 Game Day Guidance > U12 Game Framework
content:
Game Framework – (9-a-Side)
- Base Shape: 1-3-3-2
- Key Roles:
- Back 3: Build calmly, stay compact when we lose it.
- Middle 3: Create triangles, find the pivot, control tempo.
- Front 2: Stretch vertically, link and finish.
- Team Instructions:
- Build through thirds, not over.
- Press together — nearest three go.
- Stay compact in transition.
- Tempo: calm → quick → calm.

heading: U12 Individual Competencies
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 11. U12 Phase Outcomes > U12 Individual Competencies
content:
Theme: Mastery of the ball and bravery in individual actions.
By the end of this phase, the player can:
- Control and manipulate the ball with both feet in all directions.
- Receive and play with correct body orientation toward the field.
- Change direction and speed with control while protecting the ball.
- Use feints and body movements to unbalance an opponent.
- Combine 1–2 touches under light pressure with accuracy.
- Win or delay 1v1 situations through timing and body shape.
- Strike the ball cleanly (both placement and drive).
- Make quick decisions to pass, dribble, or shoot with intent.
- Show courage and enjoyment in possession, not fear of error.
- React immediately when possession is lost (start recovery instinct).
“At this age, we measure courage with the ball — not just skill.”

heading: U12 Group Competencies
headingPath: COACH PACK 1 (U12) – TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT PHASE > 11. U12 Phase Outcomes > U12 Group Competencies
content:
Collective Habits
Objective: Build small-group awareness — start playing “with others,” not just near them.
Team Competencies:
- Stay connected (distance between players no more than 10–12m).
- Move with the ball — follow play as a group.
- Support the man on the ball from both sides (2v1 mindset).
- Transition quickly between attack and defence (reaction first).
- Communicate basic cues: “turn,” “man on,” “help.”
- Celebrate recovery and collective defending.
- Create triangle shapes naturally around the ball.
- Keep width in possession, compactness without it.
- Develop the habit of defending forward.
- Begin using goalkeeper as part of play (not just a saver).
“The team idea begins with distance and rhythm.”
Session Outcomes
Players gain confidence to attack 1v1 | Beat opponents | Keep possession under pressure.
Activation/Intro (8-10mins)
Setup & Description | Coaching Focus | 
4 cones per player in one line. Mirror Moves – pairs face each other; one leads feints, the other mirrors. Swap every 30 s. Players have balls, move sideways along the line, left and right. If you don’t have enough cones use 2 or 3, and ask the players to stop after 1m | • Stay balanced, light on feet • Eyes up – read the body | 
Technical/Positional (15-20mins)
Setup & Description | Coaching Focus | 
10x10m area 1v1 lanes (8 × 15 m). Player A attacks small gate vs defender B. Quick rotation. Can have players in pairs and they take turns defending. Split across two areas. | • First touch forward • Feint then explode • Protect the ball | 
Tactical/Main (20-25mins)
Setup & Description | Coaching Focus | 
30 × 25 m. Field divided in thirds. 4v4 + 4 neutrals Score by dribbling through mini-goals. Encourage isolated 1v1s. 2 neutrals on the sides at halfway, and 2 at the end of the field between mini-goals. | • Recognise 1v1 moments • Be brave to face forward • Use body disguise | 
Transfer/End Game (10-15mins)
Setup & Description | Coaching Focus | 
40 x 30 m, goals at each end. 6v6 free game – remove zones every 5 min to increase freedom. Look for: • Facing forward on first touch. • Using feint to break line. • | • Observe use of feints • Encourage confidence and creativity | 
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Improve confidence in 1v1 situations and ability to beat defenders with controlled body movement and quick changes of direction.
Technical Detail | • Forward touch to attack space • Low centre of gravity • Explosive cut after feint • Protect ball with arm and body angle.
Coaching Messages | “Face them – don’t hide.” “Beat the player, then protect the ball.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | • Too close to defender → create space first. • No explosion → shorter touch then burst. • Eyes down → head up before move.
Praise Language | “Nice disguise – froze the defender.” “Great burst – took space with purpose.”
Behaviours to Reward | • Facing forward under pressure • Using feints with intent • Immediate reaction after losing the ball.
Reflection Question | “What makes a defender move first – your touch or your eyes?”

heading: U12 sessions
headingPath: U12 sessions
content:
U12 – CYCLE 1Dribbling & Ball Control (4 Weeks)
Week 1• Session 1 – Owning the Ball: 1v1 Mastery & Body Feints• Session 2 – Shield & Turn
Week 2• Session 1 – Attack the Defender: Directional 1v1• Session 2 – Progress to 2v2: Press-Resistance Dribbling
Week 3• Session 1 – Drive to Commit• Session 2 – Finishing the Action: Beat & Accelerate
Week 4• Session 1 – Mini Tournament: Duels & Transitions• Session 2 – Free Play Challenge: Express the 1v1

heading: U12 Cycle 1, week 1, session 1
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 1, week 1, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 1 | Owning the Ball: 1v1 Mastery & Body Feints
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident keeping the ball under control and taking on opponents using body feints.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside and outside touches. Direction changes happen naturally as players avoid each other. On a cue, players add a clear body feint (shoulder drop or hip turn) before pushing the ball away and continuing. Rhythm stays continuous, building comfort and confidence on the ball before moving into opposition.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and smooth rhythm
- Freedom to explore touches and feints
- Head up while moving
- Prepare players to attack space confidently
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 1v1s across multiple small channels
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Players play repeated 1v1s. Attacker starts with the ball and attempts to dribble past the defender to score in an end gate. Defender applies light pressure. After each attempt, players reset quickly and switch roles. Plenty of space encourages success and confidence.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to face the defender
- Using body feints to unbalance opponents
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce space slightly or add a time limit to score
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 2v2 (split into multiple grids if needed)
- Organisation: One ball per grid, rotate pairs
Description
Free 2v2 games where players score by dribbling through gates or into end zones. Players are encouraged to isolate opponents and attack 1v1 whenever possible. Repetition comes from continuous play and frequent restarts.
Coaching Focus
- Quality of first touch forward
- Body control and balance when feinting
- Willingness to engage defenders
Progression
- Add a rule: at least one dribble attempt before scoring
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals scored after a successful dribble or body feint count double. This encourages players to apply 1v1 skills naturally within the game without forcing structure.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising 1v1 moments
- Carrying the ball with purpose
- Simple decisions: dribble or pass
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players confidently take on opponents and protect the ball under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery in possession
- Enjoyment and expression on the ball
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to own the ball and attack defenders
Technical Detail | Ball control, body feints, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Own the ball.” / “Be brave.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Rushing past the defender → encourage feint first
Praise Language | “Great feint.” / “Love the confidence.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, effort, engagement
Reflection Question | “What feint helped you beat your opponent?”
Session Design Note | Space and repetition early build confidence in 1v1 play
Language Note | Light Belco terms: brave, engage, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 1, week 1, session 2
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 1, week 1, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 1 | Shield & Turn
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident protecting the ball from a defender and turning away under pressure.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using slow, close touches. On a cue, players stop the ball, place their body between the ball and an imagined defender, then turn away using the sole or inside of the foot and continue moving. Rhythm flows between move → protect → turn → move, without stopping.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and balance
- Using the body to protect the ball
- Smooth turns away from pressure
- Prepare players to feel comfortable under contact
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 1v1 in multiple small channels
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Attacker starts with the ball and a defender applies light pressure from behind or the side. The attacker’s task is to shield the ball, then turn away and dribble through an end gate to score. After each turn, players reset quickly and switch roles.
Coaching Focus
- Strong body position between ball and defender
- Patience before turning
- Playing with confidence and intent
Progression
- Reduce space or allow the defender to apply slightly more pressure
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 2v2 (split into multiple grids if needed)
- Organisation: One ball per grid
Description
Free 2v2 games. Players score by dribbling through gates or into end zones. Players are encouraged to receive under pressure, shield the ball, and turn away before attacking space. Repetition comes from continuous play and frequent ball contacts.
Coaching Focus
- First touch into a protected position
- Balance and strength when shielding
- Willingness to turn rather than panic
Progression
- Add a rule: at least one shield or turn before scoring
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals scored after a clear shield-and-turn action count double. This rewards players for protecting the ball and turning under pressure within realistic game play.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising pressure from behind
- Protecting before playing
- Simple decisions: turn, carry, or pass
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally protect the ball and turn away when pressured.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to hold the ball
- Confidence under contact
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to shield and turn under pressure
Technical Detail | Body position, balance, turning technique
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Protect first.” / “Turn away.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Turning too early → encourage stronger shielding
Praise Language | “Great protection.” / “Nice turn.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, effort, persistence
Reflection Question | “What helped you keep the ball?”
Session Design Note | Space and repetition build confidence under contact
Language Note | Light Belco terms: protect, brave, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 1, week 2, session 1
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 1, week 2, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 2 | Attack the Defender: Directional 1v1 → 2v2
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident driving directly at a defender and choosing how to beat them before pressure arrives.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player leads with the ball using feints, pauses, and direction changes. The partner mirrors movement. On a cue, the ball carrier attacks forward for 3–4 touches after a feint, then resets. Roles swap regularly. Rhythm builds feint → drive → reset, preparing players to attack defenders with intent.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and disguise
- Clear forward intent after the feint
- Rhythm without stopping play
- Prepare players to commit defenders
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m with two end gates
- Players: 1v1s in multiple channels
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Attacker starts with the ball and must drive at the defender to score through either end gate. Defender applies light pressure. If the attacker hesitates or goes sideways, play resets. Quick role switches keep repetition high.
Coaching Focus
- Facing the defender
- Attacking with speed and confidence
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce space or add a time limit to score
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 2v2 (start as 1v1, second player joins after first action)
- Organisation: One ball per grid
Description
Play starts as a directional 1v1. After the first attacking action (dribble, feint, or shot), a second attacker and defender enter, turning the game into 2v2. This rewards direct attacking first, then simple cooperation. Continuous play and fast restarts create repetition.
Coaching Focus
- First touch forward
- Commitment to beat the defender
- Awareness of support once the second player arrives
Progression
- Limit touches after the 2v2 begins
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals scored after a clear 1v1 attack (dribble or feint leading to penetration) count double. This encourages players to take responsibility in attacking moments before combining.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising 1v1 opportunities
- Driving forward with purpose
- Simple decisions: beat or combine
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players confidently attack defenders and then connect with teammates when needed.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery in possession
- Confidence to engage opponents
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to attack defenders directly
Technical Detail | First touch forward, body feints, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Attack them.” / “Go forward.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Hesitation → encourage earlier forward touch
Praise Language | “Great attack.” / “Love the intent.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, engagement, persistence
Reflection Question | “What helped you beat the defender?”
Session Design Note | Direction and space support confident attacking decisions
Language Note | Light Belco terms: brave, engage, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 1, week 2, session 2
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 1, week 2, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 2 | Press Resistance Dribbling
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident keeping the ball under pressure by protecting it, changing direction, and escaping the press.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players carry the ball freely using turns, stops, and changes of direction. On a cue, a nearby player applies light shadow pressure (no tackling). The ball carrier protects the ball, turns away, and accelerates into space for 3–4 touches before flowing back into free movement. Rhythm stays high and continuous.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact under light pressure
- Using body and ball to protect
- Smooth escape into space
- Prepare players to feel comfortable when pressed
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 1v1s in multiple small grids
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Attacker starts with the ball. Defender presses actively but fairly. The attacker scores by dribbling out of the grid under control. Defender scores by winning the ball. Short rounds with quick switches keep repetition high.
Coaching Focus
- Staying calm under pressure
- Using turns and body position
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce grid size to increase pressure
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 2v2 (split into multiple grids if needed)
- Organisation: One ball per grid
Description
Free 2v2 games. Players score by dribbling out of pressure and into an end zone. Defenders press together. Attackers are encouraged to shield, turn, and escape before combining. Continuous play and frequent ball contact create repetition.
Coaching Focus
- First touch into protected space
- Balance and strength when pressed
- Confidence to turn rather than panic
Progression
- Add a rule: at least one dribble escape before scoring
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the scorer escapes pressure with a dribble or turn before the finish. This rewards press resistance within realistic game play.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising pressure moments
- Protecting the ball before playing
- Simple decisions: escape, carry, or pass
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players stay calm, protect the ball, and dribble out of pressure naturally.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery under pressure
- Confidence to keep the ball
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident dribbling under pressure
Technical Detail | Body position, turning, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Stay calm.” / “Protect then escape.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Panicking → remind players to use body and turn
Praise Language | “Great escape.” / “Nice composure.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, persistence, calmness
Reflection Question | “What helped you get away from pressure?”
Session Design Note | Light pressure early builds confidence before realism
Language Note | Light Belco terms: brave, protect, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 1, week 3, session 1
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 1, week 3, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 3 | Drive to Commit
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident driving forward with the ball to commit a defender and create an advantage.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using controlled touches. On a cue, players push the ball forward with intent for 3–4 touches, then slow and turn back into space. Rhythm alternates between calm control and purposeful forward drives. No stops—continuous flow.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and rhythm
- Clear change from slow to drive
- Head up while carrying
- Prepare players to attack space with intent
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m with central start cone and two end gates
- Players: 1v1s in multiple channels
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Attacker starts at the central cone and must drive directly at the defender before choosing which gate to attack. Defender applies light pressure. If the attacker avoids the defender too early, play resets. Quick switches maintain repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Driving straight at the defender
- Forcing the defender to react
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce distance between attacker and defender
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 2v2 (start as 1v1, second attacker joins once defender is committed)
- Organisation: One ball per grid
Description
Play starts as a 1v1. When the attacker drives and commits the defender, a second attacker joins to create a 2v2. Attackers score in end zones. This links individual drive with simple cooperation. Continuous play builds repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the drive
- Recognising when the defender is committed
- Simple decisions after committing the defender
Progression
- Limit touches once the second attacker enters
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the attack includes a clear drive that commits a defender before the final action. Encourage natural application, not forced dribbling.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising space to drive
- Attacking defenders with purpose
- Simple decisions: drive, pass, or shoot
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally drive at defenders to create advantages.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery in possession
- Confidence to engage opponents
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to drive and commit defenders
Technical Detail | First touch forward, carrying speed, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Drive at them.” / “Make them choose.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Dribbling sideways → encourage forward drive first
Praise Language | “Great commitment.” / “Nice positive carry.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, intent, engagement
Reflection Question | “What happened when you drove forward?”
Session Design Note | Direction and space support confident attacking actions
Language Note | Light Belco terms: brave, engage, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 1 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Dribbling & Ball Control | Week 3 | Mini Tournament: Duels & Transitions
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident winning duels, protecting the ball, and reacting quickly when possession changes.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with close touches. On a cue, players stop the ball, use their body to protect it, then turn away and accelerate for 3–4 touches before flowing back into free movement. Rhythm alternates protect → turn → go, preparing players for repeated duels and quick transitions.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and balance
- Comfort under light contact
- Smooth escape after protection
- Prepare players for duel-based games
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: Two 15x12m mini pitches
- Players: 2v2 games
- Equipment: Mini goals, balls
Description
Short 2v2 games. When a goal is scored or the ball goes out, the other team restarts immediately. Emphasis is on reacting quickly after winning or losing the ball. Rotate opponents every 2–3 minutes.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction after transitions
- Competing in duels
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Award a bonus point for scoring within 5 seconds of a regain
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: Three small-sided pitches (15x12m)
- Players: 2v2 or 3v3
- Organisation: Round-robin mini tournament
Description
Games run continuously. Players score normally. Coaches observe duels and transitions: how players protect the ball, turn away, and react when possession changes. Quick rotations keep intensity high and repetition constant.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery in 1v1 duels
- Protecting the ball under pressure
- Fast reaction after win or loss
Progression
- Reduce pitch size slightly to increase duel frequency
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m (or multiple small pitches)
- Players: Tournament format (3v3 / 4v4)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Mini tournament continues with normal rules. Goals scored after winning a duel or quick transition count double. Play flows with minimal stoppages and quick restarts.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising transition moments
- Carrying the ball with purpose
- Simple decisions: protect, drive, or pass
Progression
- Remove double-goal rule and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
One final free game. No conditions. Observe whether players naturally compete in duels and react quickly during transitions.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence and bravery
- Enjoyment and competitive spirit
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident in duels and transition moments
Technical Detail | Shielding, turning, acceleration
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it and go.” / “Protect then play.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Switching off after loss → prompt quick reaction
Praise Language | “Great duel.” / “Nice reaction.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, effort, persistence
Reflection Question | “What did you do straight after winning the ball?”
Session Design Note | Tournament format increases repetition of duels and transitions
Language Note | Light Belco terms: brave, react, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Passing & Receiving | Week 1 | Short Passing Triangles
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident passing and receiving in small triangles to keep the ball moving.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player moves with the ball using small feints and changes of direction. The partner mirrors movement without the ball. On a cue, the ball carrier passes to the partner, who opens their body and takes the ball away with the first touch before roles switch. Flow stays continuous, linking movement, pass, and receive.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and rhythm
- Opening body to receive
- Smooth pass–receive flow
- Prepare players for combination play
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: Groups of 3
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Players form small triangles and pass freely. After each pass, the passer moves to a new corner to keep the triangle alive. Focus is on short passes, clean receiving, and constant movement. No defenders—success through repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Quality of short passes
- Movement after passing
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a passive defender who shadows passes
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v1 or 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Three attackers keep possession using triangle shapes against light pressure. Players are encouraged to create good angles, pass short, and receive on the move. Repetition comes from quick restarts and frequent role rotation.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to receive
- Passing weight and accuracy
- Awareness of support angles
Progression
- Reduce space or add a second defender
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the attack includes three short passes before scoring. This encourages players to connect using short combinations rather than forcing play.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising short passing options
- Supporting the ball
- Simple decisions: pass and move
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural combinations
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally form triangles and combine with short passes.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to keep the ball
- Team connection and movement
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident using short passing triangles
Technical Detail | Passing accuracy, first touch, body shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Pass and move.” / “Help the ball.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Standing after pass → encourage immediate movement
Praise Language | “Nice combination.” / “Good movement.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, cooperation, effort
Reflection Question | “How did moving after your pass help?”
Session Design Note | Early low pressure supports clean repetition
Language Note | Light Belco terms: connect, intelligent, compact
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Passing & Receiving | Week 1 | Support Angles: Open Body Shape
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident moving to support the ball with an open body shape to play forward or keep possession.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players dribble freely using turns and direction changes. On a cue, players stop the ball, open their body to one side, take the next touch forward, and continue moving. Later, add a passive defender approaching from the front, encouraging players to open up and move away from pressure. Flow stays continuous and rhythmic.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and coordination
- Opening body before the next action
- Smooth transition from control to movement
- Prepare players to receive side-on
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: Groups of 3
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three players pass freely in a triangle. The receiving player must move off the cone to receive side-on, opening their body to see both teammates. After each pass, players rotate positions to maintain angles. No defenders—focus on clean movement and shape.
Coaching Focus
- Creating clear support angles
- Receiving side-on
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a passive defender to block one passing lane
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v1 or 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Attackers keep possession under light pressure. Supporting players are encouraged to adjust angles early, receiving with an open body to play forward or away from pressure. Repetition comes from continuous play and frequent defender rotation.
Coaching Focus
- Movement before receiving
- Body shape to play multiple options
- Confidence to reposition to help the ball
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure on body shape
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the build-up includes a clear open-body receive that helps the team play forward or keep possession. Encourage natural application, not forced movement.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to support
- Opening body to see options
- Simple decisions: play forward or recycle
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally move to support with good angles and open body shape.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to move off the ball
- Calm receiving under pressure
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident supporting with open body shape
Technical Detail | Body shape, first touch, scanning
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Open up.” / “Help the ball.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Receiving square → cue side-on body position
Praise Language | “Great angle.” / “Nice open touch.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, movement, cooperation
Reflection Question | “What did opening your body help you see?”
Session Design Note | Low pressure early supports clean receiving habits
Language Note | Light Belco terms: support, open, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Passing & Receiving | Week 2 | Passing Under Pressure: 4v2 Rondos
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident keeping the ball with short passes when defenders apply pressure.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with close touches and direction changes. On a cue, players pause the ball, lift their head, then pass to a nearby player and receive it back before continuing. Rhythm builds calm control → look → pass → receive, preparing players for pressure situations.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and rhythm
- Head up before passing
- Clean first touch
- Prepare players for quick passing decisions
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 18x18m square
- Players: 4 attackers vs 1 defender
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Attackers keep possession using short passes. Defender applies light pressure. Focus is on quick movement after passing and staying available. Rotate defender every 60–90 seconds to keep intensity high.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of pressure
- Supporting the ball early
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender to move into 4v2
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 15x15m squares
- Players: 4v2 in each grid
- Organisation: Multiple grids if needed
Description
Classic 4v2 rondos. Attackers aim to keep the ball using short, sharp passes. Defenders work to win possession. When defenders win the ball, they switch with the players who lost it. Repetition comes through quick turnovers and constant involvement.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to receive under pressure
- Passing speed and accuracy
- Movement after passing to re-offer
Progression
- Limit attackers to two touches to increase speed
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the build-up includes three or more short passes under pressure before scoring. Encourage natural application of rondo habits into the game.
Coaching Focus
- Keeping possession under pressure
- Supporting angles around the ball
- Simple decisions: pass, move, or protect
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players stay calm and use short passing when pressed.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence on the ball
- Team connection under pressure
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident passing under pressure
Technical Detail | First touch, body shape, passing accuracy
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Keep it moving.” / “Help the ball.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Standing still → remind players to move after passing
Praise Language | “Nice calm pass.” / “Good support.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, cooperation, composure
Reflection Question | “What helped us keep the ball under pressure?”
Session Design Note | Rondos build comfort and confidence with pressure
Language Note | Light Belco terms: connect, compact, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
