content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Passing & Receiving | Week 2 | Receive to Play Forward
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident receiving the ball and playing forward with their first or second action.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with close touches and turns. On a cue, players stop the ball, open their body, and turn forward into space with the next touch. Rhythm flows between control → open → play forward. No pressure early, just repetition and feel for forward movement.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and balance
- Opening body before moving
- Smooth turns into space
- Prepare players to face forward after receiving
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m split into two zones
- Players: 3v1
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. Players are encouraged to receive side-on and, when possible, play a forward pass into the opposite zone. Defender applies light pressure. Rotate defender frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape on receiving
- Awareness of forward options
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender to create 3v2
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: End zone marked ahead of attackers
Description
Attackers attempt to progress into the end zone. Players are encouraged to receive and play forward early—either with a pass or a forward carry. If forward play is blocked, they recycle briefly and try again. Repetition comes from quick restarts and role rotation.
Coaching Focus
- First touch out of feet
- Body shape to see forward
- Confidence to play positive passes
Progression
- Limit attackers to two touches to speed decisions
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the build-up includes a clear receive-to-play-forward action. Encourage natural application, not forced forward passes.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising forward moments
- Playing forward with purpose
- Simple decisions: forward or recycle
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally receive side-on and look to play forward.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence on the ball
- Calm decision-making
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident receiving to play forward
Technical Detail | Body shape, first touch, scanning
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Open up.” / “Can you play forward?”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Receiving square → cue side-on body shape
Praise Language | “Great forward touch.” / “Nice positive play.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, awareness, intent
Reflection Question | “What helped you play forward there?”
Session Design Note | Light pressure early supports confident forward decisions
Language Note | Light Belco terms: open, forward, intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 2, week 3, session 1
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 2, week 3, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Passing & Receiving | Week 3 | Build from the GK: 3v3 + Targets
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident building play from the goalkeeper using short passes and support options.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player leads with the ball using small feints, turns, and direction changes. The partner mirrors movement. On a cue, the ball carrier passes to the partner, who opens their body and takes the first touch forward before continuing movement. Roles swap regularly. Flow links movement → pass → open receive, preparing players to build from the back.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and rhythm
- Opening body before receiving
- Clean pass–receive flow
- Prepare players to play out calmly
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: GK + 2 outfield players vs 1 defender
- Equipment: Balls, cones, mini goals or target gates
Description
GK starts each action with the ball. The GK and two outfield players combine to play out to a target gate under light pressure from one defender. If the defender wins it, play resets quickly with the GK. Rotate defender often.
Coaching Focus
- Calm first pass from the GK
- Supporting angles to help the ball
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender to increase pressure
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v3 + GK + 2 targets (wide or high)
- Organisation: One ball, rotate roles
Description
Play starts with the GK. The building team aims to connect 2–3 passes and find a target player. Targets play one-touch back into the game. Defenders press realistically but fairly. Repetition comes from frequent restarts with the GK.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to receive from the GK
- Movement to create passing angles
- Confidence to keep the ball under light pressure
Progression
- Limit targets to one touch to speed decisions
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5 + GK
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. All play starts from the GK. Goals count double if the team builds with short passes from the GK before scoring. Encourage natural decisions—no forcing play short.
Coaching Focus
- Supporting the GK
- Playing out when it’s on
- Simple decisions: build short or go longer if needed
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams with GK
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally offer support and confidence when the GK has the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Calm build-up behaviour
- Team connection from the back
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident building play from the GK
Technical Detail | Passing accuracy, body shape, first touch
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Help the keeper.” / “Be an option.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Hiding from the ball → encourage early movement
Praise Language | “Great support.” / “Nice build.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, bravery, cooperation
Reflection Question | “How did you help the GK today?”
Session Design Note | Light pressure supports confidence playing out
Language Note | Light Belco terms: build, support, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 2, week 3, session 2
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 2, week 3, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Passing & Receiving | Week 3 | Back → Mid → Front Game
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident connecting passes from back to midfield to front using simple support and movement.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players dribble freely with turns and direction changes. On a cue, players drop back 2–3 steps, receive again, then drive forward into space. Later, add a passive defender stepping in front, encouraging players to go back before going forward. Flow stays continuous and purposeful.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with confidence
- Comfort using the back option
- Smooth transition from back to forward movement
- Prepare players for connecting through lines
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m divided into three horizontal zones (back–mid–front)
- Players: 3v1 in each grid
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. To score a point, the ball must travel back → mid → front through all three zones. Players move between zones to support. Defender applies light pressure. Rotate defender regularly.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of teammates in different zones
- Movement to offer support
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender to create 3v2
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m divided into three zones
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Attackers aim to progress the ball from the back zone to the front zone using simple passes through midfield. Players are encouraged to move the ball, then move themselves to support the next pass. Repetition comes from quick restarts and role rotation.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to receive facing forward
- Timing of movement into the next zone
- Confidence to play through midfield
Progression
- Limit attackers to two touches in the middle zone
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m with three horizontal zones
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the build-up includes a clear back → mid → front connection before scoring. Encourage natural play—no forced passing patterns.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to connect through lines
- Supporting ahead and behind the ball
- Simple decisions: play forward, recycle, or switch
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | 1v1 Defending & Regain | Week 1 | First Defender Habits: Delay & Control
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident being the first defender — slowing the attacker and staying in control.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup & Description
- Each player with a ball in a 20x20m area.
- Free movement using inside/outside touches, sole pulls, and direction changes.
- On coach call, players slow the ball, stop it, then change direction and accelerate.
- Emphasise rhythm changes: fast → slow → fast.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and coordination
- Control before speed
- Build rhythm before defensive work🔹 Belco Cue: “Feel the ball, set the rhythm.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs (1 attacker, 1 defender)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 end lines
Description
- Attacker dribbles freely inside the area.
- Defender’s task is delay only — stay in front, slow the attacker, no tackling.
- After 20–30 seconds, swap roles.
- Focus is on movement, distance, and body shape.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence engaging the ball carrier
- Awareness of distance and angle
- Staying balanced and patient
Progression
- Attacker aims to dribble over an end line.
- Defender scores by forcing the attacker to stop or turn.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 15x20m channels
- Players: 1v1 in each channel
- Equipment: Balls, cones, mini goals
Description
- Attacker starts with the ball and attacks the goal.
- Defender closes space quickly, then slows and controls the attacker.
- Defender can tackle only after delaying for 2 seconds.
- Rotate pairs regularly to keep intensity high.
Coaching Focus
- Quick approach, then controlled deceleration
- Side-on body shape
- Willingness to engage without diving in
Progression
- Remove the 2-second delay rule.
- Reduce channel width to increase pressure.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m with two goals
- Players: 4v4 (no keepers)
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game with quick restarts.
- Defenders earn 1 point for a successful delay (forcing a pass backwards or sideways).
- Goals still count as normal for attackers.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to step in as first defender
- Slowing the attack before trying to win the ball
- Simple decisions: delay, angle, then tackle
Progression
- Remove the scoring for delay and let the game flow freely.
- Reduce space slightly.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal intervention.
- Let players apply first defender habits naturally.
Coaching Focus
- Observe patience and control when defending
- Look for defenders slowing play before tackling
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Delay and control the attacker as first defender
Technical Detail | Body shape, distance, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Slow them down.” / “Stay in control.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Diving in → remind patience; too far away → cue close but balanced
Praise Language | “Great delay.” / “Good control.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, patience, engagement
Reflection Question | “How did you slow the attacker?”
Session Design Note | Space and rules reduce pressure early to build confidence
Language Note | Light Belco vocabulary supports recognition

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | 1v1 Defending & Regain | Week 1 | Reaction to Loss
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident reacting quickly after losing the ball and trying to regain control.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup & Description
- Players work in pairs with one ball in a 15x15m area.
- One player shields the ball using body position and sole rolls.
- On coach call, partner applies light pressure; ball carrier must protect, turn, and move away.
- Swap roles every 30–45 seconds to maintain repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Balance and strength while protecting the ball
- Awareness of opponent proximity
- Calm control before losing possession🔹 Belco Cue: “Protect first, then play.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs (1v1)
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
- Attacker dribbles freely inside the area.
- On coach call, attacker deliberately leaves the ball.
- Defender immediately reacts to win the loose ball.
- Swap roles every repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Quick reaction after loss
- Positive movement toward the ball
- Willingness to re-engage immediately
Progression
- Defender can poke the ball away instead of waiting for coach call.
- Reduce space slightly.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 15x20m channels
- Players: 1v1 in each channel
- Equipment: Balls, mini goals
Description
- Attacker dribbles toward the mini goal.
- Defender tries to win the ball.
- If attacker loses possession, they must react immediately and attempt to regain before defender scores.
- Rotate pairs frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction after losing the ball
- Body shape and speed to close space
- Persistence without fouling
Progression
- Limit defender to 5 seconds to score.
- Reduce channel width.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m with two goals
- Players: 4v4
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game with quick restarts.
- If a team loses possession, nearest player must press immediately for 3 seconds.
- No stoppages — flow is prioritised.
Coaching Focus
- Recognition of ball loss moments
- Quick pressure to disrupt the opponent
- Simple decisions: press or recover space
Progression
- Remove the 3-second rule and let behaviours emerge naturally.
- Reduce space slightly.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal coach intervention.
- Allow players to self-organise reactions after losing the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Observe reaction speed and intent
- Look for players chasing back or pressing instinctively
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | React quickly and positively after losing the ball
Technical Detail | Acceleration, body shape, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “React now.” / “Win it back.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Stopping after loss → cue immediate reaction; diving in → encourage control
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” / “Love the effort.”
Behaviours to Reward | Reaction, persistence, bravery
Reflection Question | “What did you do straight after losing the ball?”
Session Design Note | Rules briefly highlight reactions, then removed for realism
Language Note | Light Belco vocabulary supports recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | 1v1 Defending & Regain | Week 2 | 2v2 Defending: Cover & Support
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident defending with a teammate — one applies pressure while the other provides cover.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: C – Mirror Feints
Setup & Description
- Players work in pairs, each with a ball, inside a 15x15m area.
- One player leads with body feints, direction changes, and stops; partner mirrors the movement.
- On coach call, both players accelerate 3–4m, then slow and reset.
- Swap leader every 30–45 seconds.
Coaching Focus
- Coordination and body control
- Reading movement and reacting quickly
- Gradually build tempo toward defensive work🔹 Belco Cue: “Show, fake, explode.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: Groups of 4 (2 attackers vs 2 defenders)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, end lines
Description
- Two attackers dribble and pass freely.
- Defenders work together to slow play and protect space (no tackling).
- One defender presses the ball, the other drops slightly to cover.
- Rotate roles every 60–90 seconds.
Coaching Focus
- Communication between defenders
- Distance and angle of cover
- Staying compact and patient
Progression
- Allow tackling after 3 seconds of delay.
- Reduce space slightly.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x25m channels
- Players: 2v2 in each channel
- Equipment: Balls, mini goals
Description
- Attackers try to score in the mini goal.
- Defenders score by winning the ball or forcing attackers to retreat.
- Emphasis on first defender pressure and second defender cover.
- Rotate pairs regularly to maintain intensity.
Coaching Focus
- First defender: slow and control
- Second defender: angle, distance, readiness to step in
- Defending as a pair, not two individuals
Progression
- Add a time limit for attackers to score.
- Narrow the channel to increase pressure.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m with two goals
- Players: 4v4 (no keepers)
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Coach highlights moments of good cover and support but keeps stoppages minimal.
- No added rules — realism is the aim.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to press and when to cover
- Staying connected with teammates
- Simple decisions: pressure, cover, or recover
Progression
- Reduce space slightly to demand quicker decisions.
- Add scoring bonus for regains in the attacking half.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal intervention.
- Let defensive partnerships form naturally.
Coaching Focus
- Observe communication and spacing between defenders
- Look for natural cover movements behind the press
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Defend effectively with a teammate using cover and support
Technical Detail | Distance, angle, body shape, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “One presses, one covers.” / “Stay connected.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Both press → remind cover role; too flat → cue depth
Praise Language | “Great cover.” / “Good teamwork.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, patience, teamwork
Reflection Question | “How did your teammate help you defend?”
Session Design Note | Space and delayed pressure support learning before realism
Language Note | Light Belco vocabulary supports recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | 1v1 Defending & Regain | Week 2 | Closed Ball Press
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident pressing a closed ball quickly and regaining control with balance.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup & Description
- Players work individually with a ball in a 20x15m area.
- Free dribbling with changes of speed and direction.
- On coach call, players stop the ball dead, step away, then re-attack their own ball aggressively for 2–3 touches.
- Progress to pairs: one player rolls the ball away lightly, partner reacts to close and touch it first.
Coaching Focus
- Sharp reaction and acceleration
- Positive movement toward the ball
- Build intensity before opposition🔹 Belco Cue: “Go now—win it.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs (1 attacker, 1 defender)
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
- Attacker dribbles freely.
- On coach call, attacker stops the ball (closed ball).
- Defender immediately presses to touch or win the ball.
- Swap roles every 20–30 seconds to keep tempo high.
Coaching Focus
- Quick recognition of a closed ball
- Positive first step toward the ball
- Control and balance when pressing
Progression
- Attacker chooses when to stop the ball (no coach call).
- Reduce space slightly.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 15x20m channels
- Players: 1v1 in each channel
- Equipment: Balls, mini goals
Description
- Attacker dribbles toward the mini goal.
- When the attacker takes a heavy touch or stops the ball, defender presses to win possession.
- Defender scores by winning the ball and dribbling over the start line.
- Rotate pairs frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the press
- Body shape: low, balanced, side-on
- Commitment without diving in
Progression
- Defender must press within 2 seconds of the closed ball.
- Narrow the channel.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m with two goals
- Players: 4v4
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Bonus point for a team that regains possession immediately after a closed ball.
- Keep the game moving; minimal stoppages.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising pressing triggers
- Collective reaction to a closed ball
- Simple decisions: press, support, or recover
Progression
- Remove bonus scoring and allow behaviours to emerge naturally.
- Reduce space slightly.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal intervention.
- Observe natural pressing responses when the ball stops.
Coaching Focus
- Observe speed and intent of the press
- Look for players attacking the ball together
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Press quickly and regain when the ball is closed
Technical Detail | Acceleration, balance, tackling timing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Go now.” / “Attack the ball.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Late press → cue first step; over-commit → remind balance
Praise Language | “Great press.” / “Love the reaction.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, reaction speed, intent
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to press?”
Session Design Note | Clear triggers used early, then removed for realism
Language Note | Light Belco vocabulary supports recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | 1v1 Defending & Regain | Week 3 | Regain & Counter: Win It Back Together
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident winning the ball together and attacking quickly after the regain.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup & Description
- Each player with a ball in a 20x20m area.
- Free movement using inside/outside touches, sole pulls, and turns.
- On coach call, players stop the ball, scan, then explode 3–4m into space.
- Repeat rhythm changes: slow → stop → fast.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and coordination
- Control before acceleration
- Prepare players for quick transitions🔹 Belco Cue: “Feel the ball, set the rhythm.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: Groups of 4 (2 attackers vs 2 defenders)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 end lines
Description
- Attackers keep the ball with short passes or dribbles.
- Defenders work together to regain the ball.
- On regain, defenders immediately counter by dribbling over an end line together.
- Rotate roles every 60–90 seconds.
Coaching Focus
- Quick reaction after the regain
- Nearest player presses, partner supports
- Positive movement forward after winning the ball
Progression
- Limit attackers to two touches.
- Reduce space slightly.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x25m channels
- Players: 2v2 in each channel
- Equipment: Balls, mini goals
Description
- Attackers try to score in the mini goal.
- Defenders aim to regain the ball.
- Once defenders win it, they counter immediately to the opposite end line or mini goal.
- Rotate pairs regularly to keep intensity high.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the press and support
- First touch forward after the regain
- Decision to drive or pass during the counter
Progression
- Add a 5-second window to counter after the regain.
- Narrow the channel.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m with two goals
- Players: 4v4
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- If a team regains possession, they have 5 seconds to attack and try to score for a bonus goal.
- Keep stoppages minimal to encourage flow.
Coaching Focus
- Collective reaction to regaining the ball
- Supporting the ball carrier quickly
- Simple decisions: drive forward, pass, or protect
Progression
- Remove the 5-second bonus and let behaviours emerge naturally.
- Reduce space slightly.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal intervention.
- Observe how teams react together after winning the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Observe speed and intent after regains
- Look for players countering as a pair or unit
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Regain the ball together and counter quickly
Technical Detail | First touch forward, acceleration, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it—go.” / “Together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Admiring the regain → cue immediate forward action; isolated counter → remind support
Praise Language | “Great regain.” / “Love the counter.”
Behaviours to Reward | Teamwork, reaction speed, bravery
Reflection Question | “What did you do right after winning the ball?”
Session Design Note | Clear counter moments early, then removed for realism
Language Note | Light Belco vocabulary supports recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | 1v1 Defending & Regain | Week 3 | Transition Reaction Game
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident reacting instantly in transition — defending immediately after loss and attacking quickly after regain.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup & Description
- Players in pairs with one ball in a 15x15m area.
- One player shields and moves the ball using sole rolls and turns.
- On coach call, partner applies light pressure; ball carrier turns away and dribbles 3–4m.
- Swap roles every 30–45 seconds.
Coaching Focus
- Balance and body control
- Awareness of opponent proximity
- Calm movement before transition moments🔹 Belco Cue: “Protect first, then play.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Groups of 3 (2v1)
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
- Two attackers pass and dribble against one defender.
- On any turnover, roles instantly switch — the player who lost the ball becomes the defender.
- Play continues without stoppage for 30–45 seconds before rotating roles.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction to loss or regain
- Positive first movement
- Engagement without hesitation
Progression
- Reduce space slightly.
- Add a time challenge: regain within 3 seconds.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x25m channels
- Players: 2v2 in each channel
- Equipment: Balls, mini goals
Description
- Normal 2v2 game toward a mini goal.
- When possession changes, the team that regains has 5 seconds to attack and score.
- If the counter fails, play continues normally.
- Rotate teams frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Speed of reaction after regain
- First touch forward
- Simple decisions: drive, pass, or protect
Progression
- Reduce counter window to 3 seconds.
- Narrow the channel.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m with two goals
- Players: 4v4
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game with quick restarts.
- Every turnover is live — no pauses.
- Coach reinforces transition moments verbally without stopping play.
Coaching Focus
- Collective reaction in transition
- Supporting the ball carrier quickly
- Compactness when defending after loss
Progression
- Add a scoring bonus for goals scored within 6 seconds of a regain.
- Reduce space slightly to increase speed of decisions.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal intervention.
- Allow players to manage transitions naturally.
Coaching Focus
- Observe reaction speed and intent
- Look for players switching roles instantly
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | React instantly in transition moments
Technical Detail | First step, acceleration, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “React first.” / “Next action.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Switching off → cue immediate movement; forcing play → encourage simple action
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” / “Love the response.”
Behaviours to Reward | Reaction speed, engagement, teamwork
Reflection Question | “What did you do straight after the ball changed?”
Session Design Note | Clear transition rules early, removed later for realism
Language Note | Belco vocabulary used lightly to support recognition

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | Finishing & Shooting | Week 1 | Finishing from Dribble – Attack with Intent
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident driving at defenders and finishing with intent after a dribble.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup & Description
- Each player with a ball in a 20x20m area.
- Players move freely using inside/outside touches, sole pulls, and direction changes.
- Coach calls changes of rhythm (slow → fast → stop → explode).
- Add a short “burst” dribble on command to simulate attacking space.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and freedom of movement
- Encourage creativity without correction overload
- Gradually build tempo toward game play🔹 Belco Cue: “Feel the ball, set the rhythm.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: Split into two groups of 5–6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals per grid
Description
- Players dribble freely inside the area.
- On coach’s call, players attack the nearest mini goal and finish from a dribble.
- After shooting, retrieve ball and rejoin dribbling.
- Focus is on quick recognition and positive movement toward goal.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to move with the ball
- Awareness of space and open goals
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add passive defenders who apply light pressure from the side or behind.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: Two 15x20m channels
- Players: Pairs (1 attacker vs 1 defender)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, mini goals
Description
- Attacker starts with the ball and drives toward goal.
- Defender applies controlled pressure (initially delayed).
- Attacker aims to beat the defender and finish.
- Rotate roles every 2–3 repetitions to maintain intensity.
Coaching Focus
- Quality of first touch forward
- Body control and balance while dribbling
- Willingness to engage opponents and attack space
Progression
- Defender starts closer or becomes fully active.
- Limit attacker to 4–5 seconds to finish.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m, two goals
- Players: 4v4 + 2 floaters (numerical support for attackers)
- Equipment: Balls, goals, bibs
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Goals count double if scored after a dribble past an opponent.
- Floaters play with the team in possession to support confidence and repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising 1v1 moments
- Carrying the ball with purpose toward goal
- Simple decisions: go, protect, or pass
Progression
- Remove one floater to balance numbers.
- Limit touches in attacking half to encourage decisive action.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal football rules.
- Minimal coach intervention.
- Encourage players to express themselves and attack when opportunities appear.
Coaching Focus
- Observe confidence and bravery on the ball
- Look for spontaneous dribble-to-finish actions
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Attack with the ball and finish confidently after a dribble
Technical Detail | First touch forward, balance when striking, ball control at speed
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Attack with intent.” / “Be brave and go.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Hesitation → encourage earlier decision; heavy touch → remind softer first touch
Praise Language | “Great bravery.” / “Love the intent.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, engagement, quick reaction, persistence
Reflection Question | “When did you decide to go at goal?”
Session Design Note | Early activities include space and light pressure to support success
Language Note | Use Belco terms lightly to build recognition and confidence
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | Finishing & Shooting | Week 1 | 1-Touch Finishes
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident finishing first time when the ball arrives in front of goal.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup & Description
- Players work in pairs with one ball in a 15x15m area.
- One player shields the ball, using body position and sole rolls.
- On coach call, player turns away from partner and dribbles 3–4m before stopping the ball.
- Switch roles regularly to maintain repetition and balance.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact under light contact
- Balance and timing when turning
- Confidence protecting the ball before releasing🔹 Belco Cue: “Protect first, then play.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 2 groups of 5–6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals per grid
Description
- Players pass and move freely inside the area.
- After a short pass, the receiver finishes first time into a mini goal.
- Players rotate positions quickly to maintain rhythm and repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Quick body shape adjustment
- Confidence striking without extra touches
- Playing with positive intent
Progression
- Reduce space between pass and shot.
- Introduce light pressure from behind the shooter.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x25m, channel facing a goal
- Players: Groups of 3 (server, finisher, recover defender)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, goals
Description
- Server passes into the path of the finisher.
- Finisher must strike first time.
- Recover defender applies delayed pressure from the side.
- Rotate roles every 3–4 repetitions.
Coaching Focus
- Quality of body shape before receiving
- Clean contact and balance when striking
- Willingness to shoot without hesitation
Progression
- Defender becomes fully active.
- Vary the angle and speed of the pass.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m with two goals
- Players: 4v4 + 2 neutral wide players
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Goals count double if scored first time.
- Wide neutrals provide frequent passing opportunities to encourage quick finishes.
Coaching Focus
- Anticipating passes in front of goal
- Sharp movement to create finishing angles
- Simple decisions: shoot first time or move the ball on
Progression
- Remove one neutral player.
- Limit attackers to two touches in the final third.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal stoppages.
- Encourage players to recognise moments to finish early.
Coaching Focus
- Observe confidence striking first time
- Look for spontaneous finishing actions
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Finish confidently with one touch
Technical Detail | Body shape, balance, clean contact
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Be ready early.” / “Trust the first touch.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Extra touch → remind early body shape; rushed shot → encourage calm balance
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” / “Sharp finish.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, anticipation, quick reaction
Reflection Question | “When did you know you could shoot first time?”
Session Design Note | Numerical support used to increase repetition and confidence
Language Note | Light use of Belco terms to support recognition

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | Finishing & Shooting | Week 2 | Combination to Finish – Wall Pass Release
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident using a wall pass to break pressure and finish quickly.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: C – Mirror Feints
Setup & Description
- Players work in pairs, each with a ball, inside a 15x15m area.
- One player leads with body feints and direction changes; partner mirrors the movements with the ball.
- On coach’s call, both players explode forward 3–5m and stop the ball.
- Swap leader/follower every 30–45 seconds to maintain rhythm and focus.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and coordinated body movement
- Encourage disguise before acceleration
- Build tempo toward game play🔹 Belco Cue: “Show, fake, explode.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: Groups of 3–4
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals per grid
Description
- One player dribbles toward a teammate, plays a short wall pass, then moves to receive and finish into a mini goal.
- Rotate roles quickly to create repetition.
- Focus is on timing the run after the pass.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to combine and move
- Awareness of teammate position
- Playing with energy and intent after the pass
Progression
- Add a passive defender who shadows the dribbler.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 20x25m channel toward goal
- Players: Groups of 3 (dribbler, wall player, defender)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, goals
Description
- Dribbler drives forward and plays a wall pass with the support player.
- After the return pass, dribbler finishes on goal.
- Defender applies delayed pressure from behind or the side.
- Rotate roles every 3–4 repetitions.
Coaching Focus
- Quality and weight of the wall pass
- Timing of movement after the pass
- Willingness to finish quickly
Progression
- Defender becomes fully active.
- Reduce space between pass and shot.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 35x25m with two goals
- Players: 4v4 + 2 neutral support players
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Goals count double if scored after a wall pass combination.
- Neutral players support the team in possession to encourage combinations.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising moments to combine
- Quick movement after passing
- Simple decisions: combine or drive to finish
Progression
- Remove one neutral player.
- Limit touches for the wall player to one touch.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal intervention.
- Encourage players to combine naturally near goal.
Coaching Focus
- Observe confidence using wall passes
- Look for spontaneous combination-to-finish actions
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Combine with a teammate and finish confidently
Technical Detail | Pass weight, movement timing, balance when finishing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Pass and move.” / “Go again after you play.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Standing after pass → remind quick movement; heavy return pass → encourage softer touch
Praise Language | “Great combination.” / “Nice movement.”

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | Finishing & Shooting | Week 2 | Cut-Back Zone
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident driving to the end line and finishing from cut-back passes.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup & Description
- Each player with a ball in a 20x15m area.
- Players dribble freely using changes of speed and direction.
- On coach’s call, players drive forward into space, turn sharply, and accelerate again.
- Progress to light 1v1 shadow pressure where a partner tracks but does not tackle.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with forward intent
- Confidence driving into space
- Gradually connect repetition to opposition🔹 Belco Cue: “Drive before they arrive.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m with a wide channel on each side (cut-back zones)
- Players: Groups of 4–5
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals centrally
Description
- Players dribble toward a wide channel, enter it, and cut the ball back to a teammate arriving centrally to finish.
- After passing, the wide player rotates inside.
- Continuous flow with quick resets and lots of repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to drive wide with the ball
- Awareness of teammates arriving
- Positive intent after entering the zone
Progression
- Add a passive defender inside the box to create light pressure on the finisher.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m toward goal, marked cut-back zone on each side
- Players: Groups of 3 (wide dribbler, finisher, defender)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, goals
Description
- Wide player dribbles into the cut-back zone.
- Defender applies delayed pressure from inside.
- Ball is cut back to the arriving finisher for a shot.
- Rotate roles every 3–4 repetitions.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the cut-back pass
- Movement and body shape of the finisher
- Willingness to arrive and finish first time or with one control
Progression
- Defender becomes fully active.
- Limit the finisher to one or two touches.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m with two goals
- Players: 4v4 + 2 wide neutral players (fixed in cut-back zones)
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Goals count double if scored from a cut-back.
- Wide neutrals encourage repeated wide penetration and finishing actions.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to go wide
- Carrying the ball with purpose
- Simple decisions: drive, cut-back, or finish
Progression
- Remove one wide neutral.
- Allow defenders to enter cut-back zones.
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with normal rules.
- Minimal intervention.
- Encourage natural use of wide areas and cut-backs when available.
Coaching Focus
- Observe confidence attacking wide spaces
- Look for spontaneous cut-back finishes
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Drive wide and finish from cut-back passes
Technical Detail | Ball control at speed, pass timing, finishing balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Get wide and drive.” / “Arrive to score.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Early cross → encourage deeper drive; late arrival → cue earlier movement
Praise Language | “Great run.” / “Perfect cut-back.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, movement, reaction, intent
Reflection Question | “What helped the finish after the cut-back?”
Session Design Note | Space and wide support used early to promote success
Language Note | Light Belco vocabulary supports recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U12 | Finishing & Shooting | Week 3 | Finishing Under Pressure – 2v2 + Keeper
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident finishing while under pressure from opponents and the goalkeeper.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup & Description
- Each player with a ball in a 20x20m area.
- Free movement using inside/outside touches, sole pulls, and turns.
- On coach call, players accelerate 3–4m, stop the ball, then change direction.
- Finish with short bursts toward a cone to simulate attacking space.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and freedom of movement
- Rhythm and balance before acceleration
- Gradually build tempo toward game play🔹 Belco Cue: “Feel the ball, set the rhythm.”
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: Groups of 4 (2v1 rotation)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Two attackers combine against one defender.
- Attackers aim to create space and finish quickly.
- Defender applies controlled pressure; rotate roles every 60–90 seconds.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence carrying the ball under light pressure
- Awareness of teammate position
- Positive intent to finish early
Progression
- Defender becomes more active.
- Reduce space slightly.
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m toward a full-size goal
- Players: 2 attackers vs 2 defenders + goalkeeper
- Equipment: Balls, cones, goal
Description
- Play starts with a pass into the attackers.
- Attackers try to score against two defenders and a keeper.
- Defenders apply realistic pressure but no tackling in first 3 seconds.
- Rotate pairs regularly to maintain intensity.
Coaching Focus
- Quality first touch under pressure
- Body control and balance when shooting
- Willingness to shoot or combine quickly
Progression
- Remove delayed tackle rule.
- Limit attackers to 6–8 seconds to finish.
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m with two goals and keepers
- Players: 4v4 + goalkeepers
- Equipment: Balls, bibs, goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts from the goalkeeper.
- Encourage frequent attacks toward goal.
- No additional conditions — realism is the focus.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising finishing moments under pressure
- Carrying the ball with intent in tight areas
- Simple decisions: shoot, protect, or combine
Progression
- Reduce space slightly.
- Add a shot clock (e.g. 10 seconds to finish).
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: 5v5 or 6v6 including goalkeepers
- Area: 40x30m with goals
Description
- Free game with minimal stoppages.
- Normal rules.
- Let players solve problems under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Observe bravery near goal
- Look for confident finishes despite pressure
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Finish confidently while under pressure
Technical Detail | First touch, balance, shooting accuracy
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Be brave near goal.” / “Finish with intent.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Panic shot → encourage composure; hiding from ball → reward movement
Praise Language | “Great bravery.” / “Strong finish.”
Behaviours to Reward | Courage, engagement, persistence
Reflection Question | “What helped you score under pressure?”
Session Design Note | Pressure increases gradually to support confidence
Language Note | Belco vocabulary used lightly for recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 5, week 1, session 1
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 5, week 1, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Cooperation (2v1–3v2) | Week 1 | Create the Overload: Drive–Draw–Play
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident driving at an opponent to draw pressure and releasing a teammate at the right moment.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones to mark grid
Description
Players move freely inside the grid using inside/outside touches, sole rolls, and gentle direction changes. Rhythm builds gradually—slow to quick—without stopping. On a visual cue, players change direction and accelerate for 3–4 touches, then settle back into rhythm. The block flows directly into the Activation by opening the grid into channels.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and continuous movement
- Freedom to explore touches and rhythm
- Build tempo without stopping play
- Prepare players for driving actions
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
- Players: 2v1 in each lane (rotate roles)
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Each lane starts with a 2v1. The attacker with the ball drives forward toward the defender. The aim is to commit the defender before either continuing past them or slipping the ball to the supporting player to cross the end line. Reset quickly and rotate roles.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to carry the ball forward
- Awareness of defender position
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce lane width slightly to increase pressure
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2 (rotate attackers/defenders)
- Organisation: One ball per group, cones for end zones
Description
Attackers start with the ball and attempt to score by dribbling or passing into the end zone. The ball carrier is encouraged to drive first, forcing a defender to engage, then release to a free teammate. Repetition is created through quick restarts and frequent rotations.
Coaching Focus
- Quality first touch forward
- Body control while changing speed
- Willingness to engage defenders
Progression
- Limit attackers to 5 seconds to finish the action
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5 + 2 neutral floaters (overloads for team in possession)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game flow with neutrals always supporting the team in possession. Goals can be scored normally. Neutrals encourage numerical superiority, helping players recognise when to drive, draw, and release.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising overload moments
- Carrying the ball with purpose
- Simple decisions: go, protect, or pass
Progression
- Remove one neutral to balance numbers
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Normal rules apply. Coach observes how players naturally apply drive–draw–play behaviours.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence and bravery on the ball
- Spontaneous use of overloads
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to drive at defenders and release teammates
Technical Detail | First touch forward, balance, ball control at speed
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Drive with purpose.” / “Draw them in, then play.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Passing too early → encourage one more touch forward
Praise Language | “Brave drive.” / “Great moment to release.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, engagement, persistence
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to pass?”
Session Design Note | Early activities use space and numbers to support success
Language Note | Use Belco terms lightly: overload, brave, compact
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Cooperation (2v1–3v2) | Week 1 | Support Timing
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident arriving at the right moment to support the ball carrier.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely, using sole rolls, inside turns, and shield turns. On a cue, players slow, protect the ball with the body, then burst away into space for 3–4 touches. Rhythm alternates between protect → escape. The grid opens into channels to flow into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with balance and control
- Freedom to feel contact and protect
- Build rhythm without stopping play
- Prepare players for supporting movements
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, split into two channels
- Players: 2 attackers vs 1 defender per channel
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Ball starts with the front attacker. The supporting player begins behind. The ball carrier drives forward; the support player delays, then moves to offer a passing option once the defender commits. Score by crossing the end line with ball or pass.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of defender movement
- Patience before supporting
- Energy and intent in movement
Progression
- Defender can recover after being passed to increase realism
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: End zones marked with cones
Description
Attackers attempt to score in the end zone. Supporting players must adjust their position based on the ball carrier’s movement—hold when the drive is on, arrive when the defender steps. Quick resets create repetition and learning through flow.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of support runs
- Body shape to receive
- Willingness to stay available
Progression
- Reduce space slightly to tighten timing
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (floater for team in possession)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game with one neutral supporting possession. Goals scored normally. Neutral encourages players to recognise when to arrive and when to wait.
Coaching Focus
- Supporting from the right distance
- Arriving at speed into space
- Simple decisions: support now or hold
Progression
- Remove the neutral to balance numbers
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Coach observes natural support timing in live play.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to move off the ball
- Spontaneous support actions
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to support at the right moment
Technical Detail | Body shape, balance, first touch to play forward
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Wait… now go.” / “Arrive, don’t rush.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Supporting too early → encourage patience and scanning
Praise Language | “Perfect timing.” / “Great support run.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, persistence
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to move?”
Session Design Note | Space and numbers used early to support success
Language Note | Light use of Belco terms: support, brave, intelligent

U12 | Cooperation (2v1–3v2) | Week 2 | Bounce & Break Lines
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident using a bounce pass to break past an opponent and continue forward.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)

Setup

Description
One player leads with the ball, using body feints, step-overs, and sharp direction changes. The partner mirrors the movement without a ball. After 30–40 seconds, swap roles. Rhythm builds from slow disguise to quick explode movements. Finish with both players accelerating away after a feint to transition into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with disguise
- Freedom to explore feints and reactions
- Build rhythm and sharp changes of pace
- Prepare players for bounce movements and forward runs
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)

- Players: 2 attackers vs 1 defender per channel
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Ball starts with the front attacker. The second attacker begins slightly behind and to the side. The ball carrier drives toward the defender, plays a bounce pass, then runs beyond to receive back and cross the end line. Rotate roles quickly.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to play and move
- Awareness of defender position
- Energy and intent after the pass
Progression
- Defender can recover after the bounce to increase pressure

- Players: 3v2

Attackers aim to enter the end zone by breaking a line using a bounce pass or quick give-and-go. The first pass attracts pressure; the return pass releases the runner beyond the defender. Quick restarts maintain repetition and flow.

- Timing of the forward run
- Balance and control at speed

Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)

Normal game rules. The neutral helps create bounce opportunities between lines. Goals count normally. Players are encouraged to play, move, and receive beyond pressure.

- Remove the neutral to increase realism

Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals

Free game with minimal conditions. Observe natural use of bounce passes and forward runs.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to combine under pressure
- Spontaneous line-breaking actions
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to bounce and move beyond defenders
Technical Detail | Passing weight, body shape, first touch forward
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Play it and go.” / “Bounce, then break.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Standing after pass → encourage immediate forward run
Praise Language | “Great bounce.” / “Excellent movement.”

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Cooperation (2v1–3v2) | Week 2 | Third-Man Runs: Connect Units
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident playing forward by using a teammate to release a third player into space.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: Split into two groups; one ball per player
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Players start 1v0, carrying the ball at speed across the grid with turns and changes of direction. On a signal, a passive defender steps in, creating light 1v1 pressure. Attackers drive first, then exit the space. The block naturally raises intensity and flows into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
- Strong first touch forward
- Confidence to drive before pressure arrives
- Smooth transition from free movement to opposition
- Prepare players for combination play
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, two small grids
- Players: 3 attackers vs 1 defender per grid
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers combine to move the ball across the grid. The first pass attracts the defender, the second player sets the ball, and the third player runs beyond to receive and cross the end line. Rotate roles quickly to keep flow and repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of teammates’ movement
- Timing of the third-man run
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Defender becomes active immediately after first pass
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x30m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: End zones marked
Description
Attackers aim to reach the end zone using combination play. The ball carrier draws pressure, plays into a teammate, and the third attacker runs beyond the defenders to receive. Success is measured by breaking through with a pass or carry. Quick restarts ensure repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Quality of set pass
- Timing and angle of the third-man run
- Balance and control when receiving at speed
Progression
- Reduce space slightly to increase decision speed
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (supports team in possession)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game flow. The neutral helps connect units and encourages third-man movements. Goals scored normally. Players are encouraged to play through teammates rather than around them.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to run beyond
- Playing forward after combinations
- Simple decisions: set or spin
Progression
- Remove the neutral to increase realism
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Coach observes natural use of third-man runs and connections.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to move off the ball
- Spontaneous combination play
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to use a third teammate to play forward
Technical Detail | Passing accuracy, body shape, first touch into space
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Play, set, go.” / “Move to receive.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Standing after pass → encourage immediate movement
Praise Language | “Great run.” / “Excellent connection.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, bravery, reaction
Reflection Question | “Who helped you get forward?”
Session Design Note | Early space and numbers support success before realism
Language Note | Light Belco terms: connect, brave, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Cooperation (2v1–3v2) | Week 3 | Positional Game: Find the Free Man
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident recognising the free teammate and playing the ball into space away from pressure.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside/outside touches and gentle direction changes. Rhythm builds from slow control to sharper tempo. On a call, players lift their head, change direction, and accelerate for 3–4 touches before settling back into rhythm. Grid opens into zones to transition into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and smooth rhythm
- Head up while controlling the ball
- Build tempo without stopping play
- Prepare players to see space and teammates
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m rectangle
- Players: 3v1 in each grid (rotate defender)
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep the ball away from one defender. The aim is to find the free player by moving the ball quickly and adjusting position after each pass. Defender pressures lightly. Rotate roles frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of pressure and space
- Moving after passing
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender (3v2) if success is high
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, split into two zones
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Attackers aim to transfer the ball from one zone to the other by finding the free player. Defenders work to block passing lines. Repetition comes through quick restarts and rotations. Success is measured by clean passes into the free space.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to see options
- First touch away from pressure
- Willingness to reposition to be free
Progression
- Reduce space between zones to increase difficulty
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (plays for team in possession)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. The neutral helps create a spare player. Teams are encouraged to switch play and find the free man before attacking goal.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising where the free player is
- Quick ball movement
- Simple decisions: play now or move it again
Progression
- Remove the neutral to increase realism
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Observe natural recognition of free players under match pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to keep the ball
- Spontaneous use of width and support
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to find and use the free teammate
Technical Detail | First touch direction, body shape, passing accuracy
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Head up.” / “Who’s free?”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing passes → encourage extra touch or recycle
Praise Language | “Great awareness.” / “Nice find.”
Behaviours to Reward | Scanning, movement, patience

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Cooperation (2v1–3v2) | Week 3 | Mini Game: Cooperate to Score
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident working with a teammate to create and finish scoring chances.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely, using sole rolls, inside/outside turns, and stop–start actions. On a cue, players slow, protect the ball with the body, then spin away into space for 3–4 touches before rejoining free movement. Rhythm alternates between protect → escape, preparing players for contact before combining.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with balance
- Freedom to feel pressure and turn away
- Build rhythm without stopping play
- Prepare players for game contact
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, two mini goals at each end
- Players: 2v1 in each grid
- Equipment: Mini goals, cones, balls
Description
Attackers aim to score in either mini goal. The ball carrier attracts the defender, then combines with the teammate to finish. Defenders rotate regularly. Quick restarts keep tempo high.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to attack defenders
- Awareness of teammate position
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Defender starts closer to increase pressure
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: Two mini goals or end zones
Description
Attackers try to score by cooperating—driving, passing, or combining to unbalance defenders. Repetition comes from short games (60–90 seconds) and frequent role rotation. Success is measured by creating a clear scoring chance, not just the goal.
Coaching Focus
- Quality first touch forward
- Supporting angles and distance
- Willingness to pass or move to score
Progression
- Limit attackers to one pass before a shot attempt
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 4v4 + 2 neutrals (play for team in possession)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Neutrals create constant overloads, encouraging cooperation to score. Goals count normally. Players are encouraged to use teammates to create the finish.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to combine
- Moving after passing
- Simple decisions near goal
Progression
- Remove one neutral to balance numbers
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Observe how players naturally cooperate to score under match pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence and bravery near goal
- Spontaneous combinations and finishes
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to cooperate to create goals
Technical Detail | First touch, balance, passing accuracy near goal
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Use your teammate.” / “Create it together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Shooting too early → encourage one more action
Praise Language | “Great teamwork.” / “Nice combination.”
Behaviours to Reward | Cooperation, bravery, persistence
Reflection Question | “How did you help your teammate score?”
Session Design Note | Overloads used to support success and confidence

heading: U12 Cycle 6, week 1, session 1

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Transition (Win–Play–React) | Week 1 | Immediate Regain: 5-Second Rule
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident reacting immediately after losing the ball to try and win it back.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player moves with the ball using feints, stops, and sharp direction changes. The partner mirrors movement without the ball. After 30–40 seconds, swap roles. Finish each round with a short burst away after a feint. The rhythm builds disguise → reaction → acceleration, linking into transition behaviour.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and sharp reactions
- Freedom to explore feints and movement
- Build rhythm and quick responses
- Prepare players to react instantly
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 3v3 (multiple grids if needed)
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
Free 3v3 possession game. When a team loses the ball, they have 5 seconds to try to win it back. If they succeed, they keep playing. If not, the other team keeps possession without pressure. Coach counts seconds out loud early to help recognition.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction after loss
- Energy and intent to press
- Awareness of nearest opponent
Progression
- Reduce the count to 3 seconds if success is high
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v2 (defenders rotate)
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Four attackers keep possession against two defenders. If defenders win the ball, attackers have 5 seconds to react and regain. If attackers win it back, play continues. If not, defenders switch out. Repetition comes from frequent transitions.
Coaching Focus
- First reaction step forward
- Closing distance quickly
- Working together to press
Progression
- Add a third defender to increase realism
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. When possession is lost, the team has 5 seconds to try and win it back before retreating and defending normally. Goals count as usual. The rule encourages fast transition without stopping play.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising transition moments
- Pressing the ball quickly
- Simple decisions: press or recover
Progression
- Remove the countdown and observe natural reactions
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Coach observes spontaneous reactions after loss of possession.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to react immediately
- Team response, not just individual effort
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to react and press after losing the ball
Technical Detail | First step, balance, body position when pressing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “React now.” / “Closest player goes.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Stopping after loss → prompt immediate movement
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” / “Brilliant effort to win it back.”
Behaviours to Reward | Reaction speed, effort, cooperation
Reflection Question | “What did you do when we lost the ball?”
Session Design Note | Time-based pressure supports learning without overloading
Language Note | Light Belco terms: react, brave, compact
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Transition (Win–Play–React) | Week 1 | Compact Recovery
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident recovering quickly after losing the ball and getting compact with teammates.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players carry the ball freely at speed with turns and changes of direction. On a cue, players stop the ball, leave it, and sprint 5–7 metres back toward a recovery line, then re-collect a ball and continue. Later, add a passive defender to create light 1v1 pressure before the recovery sprint. Flow remains continuous.
Coaching Focus
- Strong movement with and without the ball
- Fast reaction from attack to recovery
- Build rhythm between play → recover → play
- Prepare players for compact movement
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
Free 3v3 game. When a team loses the ball, all three players must recover into a compact shape inside a marked central zone before defending again. Play continues once they recover. Quick restarts keep intensity high.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction after loss
- Moving together, not individually
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce the recovery zone size to demand tighter compactness
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v3
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
The attacking team tries to keep possession. When defenders win the ball, roles switch instantly. The team that loses possession must recover quickly and get compact before pressing. Success is measured by recovering shape quickly, not by winning the ball immediately.
Coaching Focus
- Speed of recovery runs
- Distances between teammates
- Body shape ready to defend
Progression
- Add a scoring target to increase realism after recovery
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. When possession is lost, the team must recover into their half and regain compactness before actively pressing. Coach allows play to flow, intervening minimally.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising recovery moments
- Staying connected as a unit
- Simple decisions: recover first, then press
Progression
- Remove the half restriction and observe natural compact recovery
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally recover quickly and stay compact after losing the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to sprint and recover
- Team shape and connection
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to recover quickly and defend compactly
Technical Detail | Running mechanics, balance, defensive body shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Recover together.” / “Get compact first.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Players chasing alone → remind them to recover as a group
Praise Language | “Great recovery.” / “Nice compact shape.”
Behaviours to Reward | Effort, connection, reaction
Reflection Question | “What helped us defend better after losing the ball?”
Session Design Note | Space and time early allow focus on recovery behaviour
Language Note | Light Belco terms: compact, react, brave
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Transition (Win–Play–React) | Week 2 | First Pass Forward: Counter Moments
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident playing the first pass forward immediately after winning the ball.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with inside/outside touches and gentle direction changes. On a cue, players accelerate for 3–4 touches forward, then settle back into rhythm. Periodically, players stop the ball, scan, and push the first touch forward again. Flow continues straight into the Activation by opening the grid.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and smooth rhythm
- Head up before accelerating
- Build tempo without stopping play
- Prepare players to go forward quickly
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 3v2
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Defenders start with the ball. When attackers win possession, they must play the first pass forward into a target zone or to a teammate ahead. If successful, play continues. If the first pass goes sideways or backward, possession resets. Rotate roles frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate forward thinking
- Awareness of teammates ahead
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce space to speed up decisions
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v3
- Organisation: End zone or mini goals at one end
Description
The defending team attempts to win the ball. Upon regaining, they have one action to play forward—a pass or carry into space. Attackers counter-press lightly to add realism. Repetition comes from frequent transitions and quick restarts.
Coaching Focus
- First touch direction forward
- Passing weight and accuracy
- Confidence to play early
Progression
- Limit regaining team to 3 seconds to play forward
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. When a team wins possession, goals scored within 5 seconds count double to encourage counter moments. Play flows continuously with minimal stoppages.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising counter moments
- Playing forward quickly
- Simple decisions: pass or drive
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe spontaneous first-pass-forward actions after regains.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to play forward
- Team reaction and support runs
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to play forward immediately after regaining
Technical Detail | First touch forward, passing accuracy, scanning
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it—go forward.” / “Play early.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Extra touches after regain → encourage earlier decision
Praise Language | “Great first pass.” / “Nice counter.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, awareness, reaction
Reflection Question | “What helped us go forward quickly?”
Session Design Note | Early space and time support confidence before realism
Language Note | Light Belco terms: counter, brave, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

Players move freely using sole pulls, inside turns, and shielding actions. On a cue, players protect the ball briefly, then release into space with 3–4 quick touches. Rhythm flows between protect → escape → carry, linking directly into transition movement.
