content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Transition (Win–Play–React) | Week 3 | React & Recover: Protect Middle
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident reacting after losing the ball and recovering quickly to protect the middle.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and sharp direction changes. The partner mirrors without the ball. On a cue, roles switch instantly. Finish each round with both players sprinting 5–7 metres toward a central cone, then returning to the ball. Flow builds disguise → reaction → recover.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and quick reactions
- Freedom to explore feints and movement
- Rhythm between play and recovery
- Prepare players for immediate defensive reactions
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m with a marked central zone
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
Free 3v3 game. When a team loses possession, all players must recover into the central zone before defending again. Once inside, they can step out to press. Play continues quickly with minimal stoppages.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction after loss
- Sprinting back to protect the middle
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce size of the central zone to demand tighter compactness
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v3
- Organisation: One ball per group, central channel marked
Description
Attacking team keeps possession. When defenders win the ball, roles switch instantly. The team that loses possession must recover through the middle channel before defending wide. Success is measured by speed and togetherness of recovery, not tackles.
Coaching Focus
- First reaction step toward goal side
- Distances between teammates
- Body shape ready to block central passes
Progression
- Add a 5-second window to recover before defending fully
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. After losing the ball, teams are encouraged to protect the middle first before pressing wide. Coach allows play to flow, reinforcing behaviour with brief cues only.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising danger through the middle
- Recovering goal-side and compact
- Simple decisions: recover or press
Progression
- Remove verbal reminders and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players instinctively recover centrally after turnovers.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to sprint back
- Team connection protecting central space
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to recover quickly and protect the middle
Technical Detail | Running mechanics, balance, defensive body shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Middle first.” / “Recover together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Chasing wide too early → remind players to protect central space
Praise Language | “Great recovery run.” / “Nice protection of the middle.”
Behaviours to Reward | Reaction, effort, connection
Reflection Question | “Where is the danger when we lose the ball?”
Session Design Note | Early space and clear zones support understanding of recovery priorities
Language Note | Light Belco terms: compact, react, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 6, week 3, session 2
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 6, week 3, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Transition (Win–Play–React) | Week 3 | Small-Sided Transition Tournament
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident reacting quickly in transition moments and competing together in fast-changing games.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players carry the ball freely at speed with turns and direction changes. On a cue, players leave their ball and sprint to press a nearby player’s ball for 3–4 seconds, then return to collect a new ball and continue. Light 1v1 contact appears naturally. Flow stays high and competitive, leading directly into games.
Coaching Focus
- Sharp reactions between attack and defence
- High energy and repeated efforts
- Smooth transition from ball work to opposition
- Prepare players mentally for tournament play
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: Two 20x15m mini pitches
- Players: 3v3 games running simultaneously
- Equipment: Mini goals, balls, cones
Description
Short 3v3 games (2–3 minutes). When a team scores or the ball goes out, the other team restarts immediately. Emphasis is on reacting fast to win, lose, and restart. Rotate opponents frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction to changes
- Playing with intensity and intent
- Competing fairly and positively
Progression
- Award bonus point for a goal scored within 5 seconds of regain
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: Three small pitches (20x15m)
- Players: 3v3 or 4v3 depending on numbers
- Organisation: Round-robin tournament
Description
Teams rotate through quick games. Each game emphasises transition: after losing the ball, teams must react immediately; after winning it, they attack quickly. Scores reset often to keep focus on moments, not outcomes.
Coaching Focus
- First reactions after win or loss
- Supporting teammates in chaos
- Willingness to repeat efforts
Progression
- Reduce rest between games to increase fatigue and realism
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: Multiple small-sided pitches
- Players: Tournament format (3v3 / 4v4)
- Equipment: Goals, balls, cones
Description
Tournament continues with minimal rules. Normal football laws apply. Coaches manage rotations and keep games short and intense. Focus is on transition behaviours showing up naturally.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising transition moments
- Team reaction rather than individual chasing
- Simple decisions under pressure
Progression
- Finals round with slightly larger pitch
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
One final free game. No conditions. Observe whether transition habits from the tournament appear in a more realistic game.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence and bravery in transitions
- Collective effort and communication
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to react and compete in transition moments
Technical Detail | First step, balance, movement after regain or loss
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “React fast.” / “Next action.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Switching off after goals → prompt immediate restart focus
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” / “Love the effort.”
Behaviours to Reward | Effort, reaction, teamwork
Reflection Question | “What did you do straight after winning or losing the ball?”
Session Design Note | Tournament format increases repetition of transition moments
Language Note | Light Belco terms: react, brave, compete
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 7, week 1, session 1
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 7, week 1, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Small Group Defending | Week 1 | Compact 3v3 Block: Protect Zone 14
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident defending together to protect the central danger area in front of goal.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside/outside touches and gentle turns. On a cue, players stop the ball, turn their body to face a central cone, then move again. Rhythm alternates between movement and orientation, preparing players to recognise and protect central space. Grid narrows into a central zone to flow into Activation.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and coordination
- Awareness of central space
- Smooth rhythm without stoppages
- Prepare players mentally for defending shape
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m with a marked central zone (Zone 14)
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Free 3v3 game. Teams score by dribbling or passing into end zones, but no goals can be scored through the central zone. Defenders are encouraged to stay compact and block central access, forcing play wide. Quick restarts maintain intensity.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of central danger
- Staying close together
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Allow goals from central zone after 3 passes to increase challenge
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m with a clearly marked central channel
- Players: 3 defenders vs 3 attackers
- Organisation: One ball, rotate roles
Description
Attackers attempt to play through or dribble into the central channel to score. Defenders work as a compact unit, shifting together to block passing lanes and delay attacks. Success for defenders is measured by denying central access, not by tackling.
Coaching Focus
- Distances between defenders
- Body shape to block central lanes
- Communication and shared movement
Progression
- Add a neutral attacker to overload defenders briefly
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Coach highlights Zone 14 verbally but does not stop play. Defending teams are encouraged to stay compact and protect the middle first, then press wide when safe.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising central danger
- Compact team shape
- Simple decisions: hold or step
Progression
- Remove verbal reminders and observe natural defending behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally recover centrally and defend compactly.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to hold shape
- Team connection protecting the middle
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to defend together and protect central space
Technical Detail | Defensive stance, balance, lateral movement
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Protect the middle.” / “Stay connected.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Defenders spreading too far → remind them to stay compact
Praise Language | “Great shape.” / “Nice protection.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, discipline, teamwork
Reflection Question | “Why is the middle dangerous?”
Session Design Note | Clear zones help young players recognise danger areas
Language Note | Light Belco terms: compact, protect, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Small Group Defending | Week 1 | Defend as a Unit
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident defending together by moving, pressing, and recovering as a connected group.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely using sole pulls, inside turns, and stop–start actions. On a cue, players slow, protect the ball briefly with the body, then turn and move away into space. Rhythm alternates between protect → escape. Finish with players leaving the ball and jogging together to a side of the grid before restarting.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with balance
- Comfort under light contact
- Rhythm without stopping play
- Prepare players for collective movement
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
Free 3v3 game. When defending, the team scores a point for staying connected (all three within arm’s length) for 5 seconds without conceding a pass forward. Play flows continuously with quick restarts.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of teammates
- Moving together, not chasing alone
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce space slightly to demand tighter unit movement
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4 attackers vs 3 defenders
- Organisation: One ball, rotate defenders
Description
Attackers try to play through or score in end zones. Defenders work as a unit, shifting side-to-side and stepping together to delay play. Success is measured by forcing attackers backward or wide, not by winning the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Distances between defenders
- Body shape to block forward options
- Communication and shared stepping
Progression
- Add a time limit for attackers to increase pressure
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Coach encourages defenders to press, hold, and recover together. Minimal stoppages; feedback given briefly on the move.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to step or hold
- Staying connected as play shifts
- Simple decisions: press together or drop together
Progression
- Remove all cues and observe natural unit defending
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally defend as a unit under match flow.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to stay compact
- Team connection and effort
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to defend as a connected unit
Technical Detail | Defensive stance, lateral movement, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Together.” / “Move as one.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Players chasing alone → remind them to stay connected
Praise Language | “Great unit.” / “Nice team defending.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, discipline, teamwork
Reflection Question | “How did staying together help us defend?”
Session Design Note | Numbers and space support learning collective defending
Language Note | Light Belco terms: compact, unit, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 7, week 2, session 1

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Small Group Defending | Week 2 | Press Together: Nearest Three Go
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident pressing the ball together with the nearest teammates instead of chasing alone.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player moves with the ball using feints, stop–starts, and sharp changes of direction. The partner mirrors movement without the ball. On a cue, both players sprint toward a nearby cone together before resetting. Roles swap regularly. Flow builds disguise → reaction → joint movement.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and quick reactions
- Freedom to explore movement and deception
- Rhythm between individual action and group reaction
- Prepare players to move together defensively
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
Free 3v3 game. When the defending team steps to press, all three players must go together. If one player presses alone, play is reset. Quick restarts keep energy high.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of teammates
- Moving together toward the ball
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce space to demand faster collective reactions
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4 attackers vs 3 defenders
- Organisation: One ball, rotate defenders
Description
Attackers keep possession and try to break lines. When defenders decide to press, the nearest three defenders must step together, closing space and blocking forward options. Success is measured by forcing attackers backward or sideways.
Coaching Focus
- Distance between pressing players
- Body shape to block passing lanes
- Communication and shared decision-making
Progression
- Add a 5-second press window to increase intensity
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Coach reinforces the idea that when pressing starts, the nearest three go together. Minimal stoppages; cues given briefly during flow.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising pressing triggers
- Collective movement toward the ball
- Simple decisions: press together or drop together
Progression
- Remove verbal cues and observe natural pressing behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players instinctively press as a group rather than individually.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to commit together
- Team connection and effort
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to press together as a unit
Technical Detail | Acceleration, balance, defensive body shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Go together.” / “Nearest three.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | One player pressing alone → remind them to wait and go as a group
Praise Language | “Great group press.” / “Nice teamwork.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, discipline, collective effort
Reflection Question | “What happened when we pressed together?”
Session Design Note | Numbers and space support recognition of collective pressing
Language Note | Light Belco terms: press, compact, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Small Group Defending | Week 2 | Communication & Line Coordination
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident communicating and moving together to keep a clear defensive line.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players carry the ball freely with turns and direction changes. On a cue, players stop the ball, look up, call a teammate’s name, and sprint together to a line before restarting with a new ball. Later, add a passive defender to create light 1v1 pressure before the group movement. Flow stays continuous.
Coaching Focus
- Sharp reactions and awareness
- Calling and responding while moving
- Rhythm between individual action and group movement
- Prepare players for talking and shifting as a line
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
Free 3v3 game. When defending, the team must call “step” or “drop” before moving the line forward or back. If no call is made, play resets. Quick restarts maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Clear, early communication
- Moving together as one line
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce space to increase speed of calls and movement
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4 attackers vs 3 defenders
- Organisation: One ball, rotate defenders
Description
Attackers circulate the ball and try to play through. Defenders work as a coordinated line, shifting left/right and stepping or dropping together based on a clear call. Success is measured by staying connected and blocking forward passes.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of step and drop
- Distances between defenders
- Communication that leads movement
Progression
- Add a second ball after a restart to increase communication demand
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Coach encourages defenders to talk early and move the line together. Minimal stoppages; feedback given briefly on the move.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to step or drop
- Clear calls that guide teammates
- Simple decisions: hold the line or recover
Progression
- Remove verbal reminders and observe natural communication
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally communicate and coordinate their defensive line.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to speak
- Team connection and organisation
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to communicate and move as a defensive line
Technical Detail | Defensive stance, lateral movement, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Talk early.” / “Move together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Silent defending → prompt simple, loud calls
Praise Language | “Great call.” / “Nice line movement.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, leadership, teamwork
Reflection Question | “How did talking help us defend?”
Session Design Note | Clear cues and small numbers support communication learning
Language Note | Light Belco terms: line, compact, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Small Group Defending | Week 3 | Defend Depth: Through-Ball Control
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident defending space behind them by delaying, dropping, and stopping through-balls as a unit.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside/outside touches and turns. On a cue, players push the ball forward 3–4 metres, then turn and recover back toward a starting cone before continuing. Rhythm alternates between forward movement and recovery, linking ball control with depth awareness.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact and coordination
- Awareness of space in front and behind
- Smooth rhythm without stopping play
- Prepare players for recovery defending
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m with a marked end zone behind defenders
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
Free 3v3 game. Attackers score by passing or dribbling into the end zone. Defenders focus on protecting space behind them, dropping early to block through-balls rather than stepping too high. Quick restarts maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of depth behind
- Dropping together, not chasing
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce end zone size to tighten timing
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4 attackers vs 3 defenders
- Organisation: End zone marked behind defenders
Description
Attackers attempt to play through-balls into the end zone. Defenders work as a compact line, adjusting depth to delay play, block passing lanes, and recover together. Success is measured by denying through-balls, not by tackles.
Coaching Focus
- Distance between defenders
- Body shape to see ball and space
- Timing of drop vs step
Progression
- Add a neutral attacker to increase pressure on depth
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Coach reinforces the idea of defending depth first—protecting space behind before stepping to press. Play flows with minimal interruption.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising through-ball danger
- Holding a connected line
- Simple decisions: drop or step
Progression
- Remove verbal cues and observe natural depth control
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally manage depth and recover to stop through-balls.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to drop early
- Team connection protecting space behind
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to defend depth and stop through-balls
Technical Detail | Defensive body shape, turning, recovery runs
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Protect the space.” / “Drop together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Stepping too high → remind players to see space behind
Praise Language | “Great recovery.” / “Nice depth control.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, discipline, teamwork
Reflection Question | “What told you to drop instead of step?”
Session Design Note | Clear depth zones help players recognise danger behind
Language Note | Light Belco terms: depth, compact, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Small Group Defending | Week 3 | Game: Stay Connected
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident staying connected with teammates while defending in game-like situations.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely using sole pulls, inside turns, and stop–start movements. On a cue, players turn away from pressure, then jog together toward a side cone before resuming ball work. Rhythm alternates between individual control and group movement, preparing players to stay connected.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with balance
- Comfort turning under light pressure
- Rhythm between individual action and group movement
- Prepare players for connected defending
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Balls, cones
Description
Free 3v3 game. When defending, all three players must stay within a marked distance (arm’s length) of each other. If the unit stretches too far, play resets. Quick restarts keep energy high.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of teammate positions
- Moving together side-to-side
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce space to demand tighter connections
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4 attackers vs 3 defenders
- Organisation: One ball, rotate defenders
Description
Attackers attempt to keep possession or score in end zones. Defenders work to stay connected, shifting together to block forward passes and delay attacks. Success is measured by denying space and forcing attackers wide or backward.
Coaching Focus
- Distances between defenders
- Body shape to see ball and teammates
- Communication that maintains connection
Progression
- Add a time limit for attackers to increase pressure
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Coach observes and reinforces the idea of staying connected while defending, allowing play to flow with minimal stoppages.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when the unit stretches
- Recovering together
- Simple decisions: hold shape or press together
Progression
- Remove all reminders and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally stay connected in real game flow.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to hold shape
- Team connection and effort
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to stay connected while defending
Technical Detail | Defensive stance, lateral movement, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Stay together.” / “Connected.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Players stretching → remind them to check teammate distance
Praise Language | “Great connection.” / “Nice team shape.”
Behaviours to Reward | Discipline, communication, teamwork
Reflection Question | “How did staying close help us defend?”
Session Design Note | Game-based focus reinforces habits under realism
Language Note | Light Belco terms: connected, compact, intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 8, week 1, session 1
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 8, week 1, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Build to Score | Week 1 | Build Out of Pressure: Open Ball Decisions
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident opening their body to play forward when under light pressure.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player leads with the ball using body feints, shoulder drops, and small direction changes. The partner mirrors without the ball. On a cue, the ball carrier opens their body and pushes the ball forward into space for 3–4 touches. Swap roles regularly. Flow builds disguise → open body → accelerate, leading naturally into build play.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with disguise
- Freedom to explore body movement
- Rhythm from feint to open touch
- Prepare players to receive and play forward
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, split into two zones
- Players: 3v1 in each grid
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. Players are encouraged to receive side-on, opening the body to play forward into the next zone. Defender applies light pressure. Rotate defender frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape when receiving
- Awareness of pressure
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender to increase pressure slightly (3v2)
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: End zone marked ahead of attackers
Description
Attackers build out from one end under pressure. The receiving player is encouraged to open the body and play forward—either by passing or carrying—into the end zone. Repetition comes through quick restarts and rotations.
Coaching Focus
- First touch away from pressure
- Open body position
- Confidence to play forward
Progression
- Reduce space to speed decisions
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (supports build-up)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. The neutral helps create time and space to build. Teams are encouraged to build out of pressure rather than clearing the ball. Goals scored after a forward build count normally.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising pressure moments
- Opening body to play forward
- Simple decisions: play, carry, or recycle
Progression
- Remove the neutral to increase realism
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Observe whether players naturally open their body and play forward under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to receive under pressure
- Spontaneous forward decisions
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to open body and play forward
Technical Detail | Body shape, first touch direction, scanning
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Open up.” / “Play forward if you can.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Receiving square → cue side-on body shape
Praise Language | “Great open touch.” / “Nice decision.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, awareness, intent
Reflection Question | “What helped you play forward?”
Session Design Note | Early numbers and space support confident decision-making
Language Note | Light Belco terms: open body, brave, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U12 Cycle 8, week 1, session 2
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 8, week 1, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Build to Score | Week 1 | Back Support
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident using a supporting teammate behind the ball to keep possession and play forward again.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players carry the ball freely at speed, using turns and direction changes. On a cue, players stop the ball, drop back 3–4 metres, receive again, then drive forward. Later, add a passive defender who approaches from the front, encouraging the ball carrier to pull away and re-engage. Flow stays continuous and dynamic.
Coaching Focus
- Strong first touch away from pressure
- Comfort moving backward to go forward again
- Smooth rhythm between retreat and advance
- Prepare players for back-support decisions
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 3v1
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. If the ball carrier is pressured, they are encouraged to play back to the free player behind, then reposition to receive again. Defender works at light intensity. Rotate defender frequently.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of pressure
- Confidence to use the back option
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender to create more pressure (3v2)
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: One ball, end zone ahead of attackers
Description
Attackers try to build into the end zone. When forward options are blocked, the ball carrier must use back support before building again. Supporting players adjust position behind the ball to stay playable. Quick restarts create repetition.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to receive facing forward
- Movement to create a back option
- Patience and confidence on the ball
Progression
- Limit time on the ball to encourage quicker decisions
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (plays as back support for team in possession)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. The neutral operates behind the ball, offering a constant back-support option. Teams are encouraged to recycle play when under pressure instead of forcing forward passes.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to go back
- Using support to reset and build again
- Simple decisions: forward, back, or carry
Progression

Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside/outside touches and gentle turns. On a cue, players push the ball forward, open their body toward a central cone, then continue moving. Rhythm builds from slow control to quicker tempo. The grid opens into lanes leading into the Activation.
Coaching Focus

- Rhythm without stopping play

Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)

- Players: 3v1 in each grid
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. One attacker operates inside the central square as the pivot. The aim is to find the pivot, who then plays out to a teammate. Defender applies light pressure. Rotate pivot and defender regularly.

- Players: 3v2

Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup

- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Build to Score | Week 2 | Play Forward When You Can
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident recognising moments to play forward quickly instead of taking extra touches.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely using sole pulls, inside turns, and stop–start movements. On a cue, players feel light pressure (imaginary or passive), turn away, then immediately push the ball forward into space for 3–4 touches. Rhythm alternates between protect → turn → go forward. The grid opens forward to transition into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with balance
- Confidence to turn out of pressure
- Rhythm from control to forward action
- Prepare players to see forward options early
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, split into two halves
- Players: 3v1 in each grid
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. If a forward option is open (space or free teammate), players are encouraged to play forward immediately into the opposite half. If not, they keep the ball. Defender applies light pressure. Rotate defender often.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of forward space
- Confidence to pass early
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender to increase pressure (3v2)
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: End zone marked ahead of attackers
Description
Attackers attempt to reach the end zone. When the forward pass or carry is on, players are encouraged to take it early. If forward play is blocked, they recycle briefly and try again. Repetition comes through quick restarts and role rotation.
Coaching Focus
- First touch forward
- Body shape to play early
- Confidence to make positive decisions
Progression
- Limit attackers to 3 seconds on the ball to speed decisions
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals scored within three passes after a forward play count double. This rewards recognising moments to play forward without forcing it.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising forward opportunities
- Playing forward with purpose
- Simple decisions: go now or keep it
Progression
- Remove double-goal rule and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally play forward when the moment appears.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to play forward
- Confidence in decision-making
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to play forward when the option is on
Technical Detail | First touch forward, body shape, passing weight
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “If it’s on—play it.” / “See it early.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Extra touches → encourage earlier decision
Praise Language | “Great positive pass.” / “Nice early play.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, awareness, intent
Reflection Question | “How did you know it was time to go forward?”
Session Design Note | Space and numbers early support confident forward play
Language Note | Light Belco terms: play forward, brave, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Build to Score | Week 3 | Switch of Play: Use Width & Depth
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident switching the ball to space away from pressure to attack with width and depth.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player leads with the ball using body feints and direction changes. The partner mirrors movement without the ball. On a cue, the ball carrier opens their body and pushes the ball diagonally into space, then accelerates. Swap roles regularly. Rhythm builds disguise → open body → change of direction, preparing players for switching actions.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with disguise
- Opening body to see wider space
- Smooth rhythm into acceleration
- Prepare players to change the point of attack
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m, split into three vertical zones (wide–central–wide)
- Players: 3v1
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. Players score a point by switching the ball from one wide zone to the opposite wide zone. Defender applies light pressure. Rotate defender frequently to maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of space away from pressure
- Body shape to play across
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender to increase pressure (3v2)
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: Wide channels marked on both sides
Description
Attackers attempt to progress by using width. When pressure arrives on one side, players are encouraged to switch play through a teammate or carry the ball across to the opposite channel before attacking forward. Repetition comes through quick restarts and rotations.
Coaching Focus
- Timing and weight of switch pass
- Movement to receive in space
- Confidence to move the ball away from pressure
Progression
- Reduce space slightly to increase decision speed
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 45x30m, wide channels marked
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the attack includes a switch of play before finishing. This encourages using width and depth to create space.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when one side is blocked
- Switching early to find space
- Simple decisions: go forward or switch
Progression
- Remove double-goal rule and observe natural switching behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally use width and switch play under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to play away from pressure
- Spontaneous use of wide space
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to switch play using width and depth
Technical Detail | Body shape, passing accuracy, first touch direction
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Change the picture.” / “Use the space.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing play on one side → remind players to scan opposite space
Praise Language | “Great switch.” / “Nice use of width.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, bravery
Reflection Question | “What told you to switch?”
Session Design Note | Clear wide zones support recognition of switching moments
Language Note | Light Belco terms: width, switch, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Build to Score | Week 3 | Final Third Entry: Break Lines
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident breaking defensive lines to enter the final third with control and purpose.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players carry the ball freely with turns and direction changes. On a cue, players accelerate forward through a marked “line” of cones, then slow and turn away before re-entering the grid. Later, add a passive defender stepping in front of the line, encouraging players to shift the ball and break through with a touch or feint. Flow stays continuous and attacking.
Coaching Focus
- Strong first touch forward
- Confidence to attack space
- Rhythm between slow control and quick penetration
- Prepare players for line-breaking actions
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m split into two horizontal zones
- Players: 3v2
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Attackers start in the build zone and aim to break into the final zone by passing or carrying past defenders. Defenders apply light pressure. Once the ball enters the final zone, reset quickly and rotate roles.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of defensive line
- Body shape to play or carry forward
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Reduce space between zones to tighten timing
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: Final-third entry zone marked near goal
Description
Attackers work the ball from deeper areas and attempt to break a line into the final-third zone using a pass, carry, or combination. Repetition comes from quick restarts and frequent rotation. Success is measured by controlled entry, not just speed.
Coaching Focus
- Quality of forward pass or touch
- Timing of movement beyond the line
- Confidence to engage defenders
Progression
- Limit attackers to 2–3 touches after breaking the line
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 45x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the attack includes a clear line-breaking action before entering the final third. This encourages positive play without forcing it.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising line-breaking moments
- Playing forward with purpose
- Simple decisions: pass, carry, or combine
Progression
- Remove double-goal rule and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally look to break lines when entering the final third.
Coaching Focus
- Bravery to attack space
- Confidence in forward decisions
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to break lines and enter the final third
Technical Detail | First touch forward, passing weight, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Break the line.” / “Be positive.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Playing sideways near the line → encourage forward touch or pass
Praise Language | “Great penetration.” / “Nice line break.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery, intent, engagement
Reflection Question | “What helped you get into the final third?”
Session Design Note | Space and numbers early support confident attacking decisions
Language Note | Light Belco terms: break lines, brave, intelligent

Players move freely with the ball using inside/outside touches and gentle turns. While moving, the coach holds up fingers or calls a colour/number behind the players. Players glance away from the ball, call it out, then continue dribbling. Rhythm builds from slow movement to quicker tempo without stopping.

Three attackers keep possession against one defender. Before receiving, players must call the name of a teammate or point to a free space. Defender applies light pressure. Rotate defender often to keep tempo high.

Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2

Attackers try to keep possession and progress into an end zone. Players are encouraged to scan early, receive side-on, and play quickly. Repetition comes through quick restarts and frequent role rotation. Success is measured by clean first actions after receiving.

- Calm decisions under pressure

U12 | Game Intelligence | Week 1 | Spatial Awareness
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident recognising space and adjusting their position to receive or move into better areas.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely using turns, sole pulls, and stop–start actions. On a cue, players protect the ball briefly, then turn away and move into an open space inside the grid before continuing. Rhythm flows between protect → turn → relocate, encouraging awareness of where space is.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with balance

- Rhythm without stopping play
- Prepare players to notice and use space
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 3v1
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. Attackers score a point by receiving the ball in a clearly open space (not next to a teammate). Defender applies light pressure. Rotate defender regularly.

- Awareness of teammate positions
- Moving away from pressure

Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group

Attackers aim to keep the ball and progress into an end zone. Players are encouraged to adjust position before receiving—moving away from teammates and defenders to create space. Repetition comes from quick restarts and role rotation.

- Movement off the ball
- Body shape to receive in space
- Confidence to relocate before asking for the ball

- Limit attackers to two touches to increase speed of movement
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls

Normal game rules. Goals count double if the build-up includes a clear movement into space before receiving. Coach reinforces spacing cues briefly during play.

- Recognising crowded vs open areas
- Spreading out when in possession
- Simple decisions: move or receive

- Remove double-goal rule and observe natural spacing

- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m

- Confidence to move without the ball
- Calm positioning under pressure

Element | Detail

Praise Language | “Great space.” / “Nice movement.”

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 9 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Game Intelligence | Week 2 | Decision Speed: Tempo Changes
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident changing tempo quickly—slowing down or speeding up—based on what they see.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player moves with the ball using feints, pauses, and sharp accelerations. The partner mirrors without a ball. The ball carrier alternates between slow control and sudden bursts of speed. After 30–40 seconds, roles switch. Flow builds slow → fast → slow, preparing players to feel tempo changes.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with disguise
- Freedom to pause and explode
- Rhythm between slow control and quick actions
- Prepare players to vary tempo naturally
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 3v1
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. Players are encouraged to slow the ball when unpressured, then speed up when the defender steps in. The aim is to recognise when to change tempo to keep possession or break pressure. Rotate defender often.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of defender distance
- Confidence to slow the game
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender (3v2) to reduce time and space
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Attackers aim to progress into an end zone. When space is available, players are encouraged to speed up play. When pressure is close, they are encouraged to slow down, protect the ball, or recycle before going again. Repetition comes through quick restarts and role rotation.
Coaching Focus
- First touch that matches the situation
- Body control when changing speed
- Willingness to pause before accelerating
Progression
- Limit attackers to two touches in the final zone to increase speed
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the attack includes a clear tempo change (slow build followed by quick attack). Coach reinforces tempo cues briefly during play.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to slow or speed up
- Playing with control, not rushing
- Simple decisions: pause or go
Progression
- Remove double-goal rule and observe natural tempo variation
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally change tempo based on pressure and space.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to control the game
- Calm decision-making under pressure
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to change tempo based on the situation
Technical Detail | First touch, balance, control at different speeds
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Slow it… now go.” / “Change the speed.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Playing fast all the time → encourage moments of pause
Praise Language | “Great change of pace.” / “Nice control.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, bravery
Reflection Question | “When did speeding up help you?”
Session Design Note | Space and numbers early allow players to feel tempo changes
Language Note | Light Belco terms: tempo, intelligent, brave

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 9 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Game Intelligence | Week 2 | Play Fast or Slow
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident choosing when to play quickly and when to slow the game down.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players dribble freely with turns and direction changes. On a cue, players either slow right down (small touches, head up) or burst forward for 3–4 fast touches. Later, add a passive defender stepping in, forcing the ball carrier to decide whether to slow and protect or accelerate away. Flow remains continuous.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact at different speeds
- Feeling the difference between control and speed
- Smooth transitions between slow and fast
- Prepare players for tempo choices
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 3v1
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. When the defender is far, attackers are encouraged to slow the ball and keep it moving. When the defender presses, players are encouraged to play fast—one or two touches to escape pressure. Rotate defender regularly.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of defender distance
- Confidence to slow play when safe
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender (3v2) to increase pressure and decision speed
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Attackers aim to progress into an end zone. If space is open, players are encouraged to play fast and attack. If space is closed, players must slow down, protect the ball, and recycle before going again. Repetition comes through quick restarts and frequent role rotation.
Coaching Focus
- First touch matching the situation
- Body control when slowing play
- Confidence to wait for the right moment
Progression
- Limit touches in the end zone to encourage quick finishing after fast play
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals count double if the attack shows a clear choice to play fast or slow at the right moment (coach judges by obvious pause or acceleration). Minimal stoppages.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to speed up
- Knowing when to slow the game
- Simple decisions: keep it or go
Progression
- Remove double-goal rule and observe natural tempo decisions
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally vary speed and control based on pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to control tempo
- Calm decisions under pressure
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to choose fast or slow play
Technical Detail | First touch, balance, control at different speeds
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Is it on?” / “Control or go.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Rushing every action → encourage moments of calm
Praise Language | “Great choice.” / “Nice control.”

heading: U12 Cycle 9, week 3, session 1
headingPath: U12 sessions > U12 Cycle 9, week 3, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 9 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Game Intelligence | Week 3 | Managing Possession: Control Rhythm
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident keeping the ball by controlling the rhythm of play instead of forcing actions.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside/outside touches and turns. Rhythm starts slow and controlled. On a cue, players pause the ball briefly, lift their head, then continue at the same calm tempo. Later cues allow short bursts forward before returning to control. Flow remains calm and continuous.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with calm control
- Head up while moving
- Feeling rhythm without rushing
- Prepare players to manage the game with the ball
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 3v1
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. The aim is to keep the ball calmly, using short passes and movement. Players are encouraged to slow the game when unpressured and avoid forcing forward passes. Rotate defender regularly.
Coaching Focus
- Patience on the ball
- Awareness of pressure
- Playing with composure and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender (3v2) to challenge rhythm control
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Attackers aim to keep possession for a set number of passes before progressing into an end zone. Players must control rhythm—recycling the ball when pressure is high and moving it quicker when space opens. Repetition comes from quick resets and role rotation.
Coaching Focus
- First touch that supports control
- Body shape to play multiple options
- Confidence to reset play
Progression
- Reduce space to demand better control under pressure
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Goals only count if the team completes at least three controlled passes in the build-up. This encourages patience and rhythm control without over-coaching.
Coaching Focus
- Managing tempo with the ball
- Avoiding rushed decisions
- Simple choices: keep it or go
Progression
- Remove the pass condition and observe natural rhythm
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally slow the game, keep possession, and choose moments to accelerate.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to control the game
- Calm behaviour under pressure
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to control rhythm in possession
Technical Detail | First touch, body shape, passing accuracy
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Calm on the ball.” / “We control the game.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Rushing forward → encourage recycle and reset
Praise Language | “Nice control.” / “Good patience.”
Behaviours to Reward | Composure, awareness, bravery
Reflection Question | “When did slowing down help us?”
Session Design Note | Low pressure early supports calm decision-making
Language Note | Light Belco terms: control, rhythm, intelligent
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 9 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Game Intelligence | Week 3 | Playmaker Game: Read the Moment
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident reading the moment to decide whether to pass, carry, or slow the game.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with turns, sole pulls, and changes of direction. On a cue, players protect the ball briefly, lift their head, then choose to either turn away slowly or burst forward into space. Rhythm alternates between protect → look → decide, building feel for moment-based decisions.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with balance
- Head up before action
- Rhythm without stopping play
- Prepare players to feel decision moments
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 3v1
- Equipment: Cones, balls
Description
Three attackers keep possession against one defender. One attacker is nominated as the playmaker each round. The playmaker decides when to speed up, slow down, or change direction of play. Rotate playmaker frequently so all players experience the role.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of pressure and space
- Confidence to make choices
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Add a second defender (3v2) to increase challenge
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 3v2
- Organisation: One ball per group
Description
Attackers aim to progress into an end zone. One player acts as the decision leader on each attack, reading the moment to decide whether to play forward, recycle, or carry. Roles rotate regularly. Repetition comes through quick restarts.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning before acting
- Body shape to play multiple options
- Confidence to pause or accelerate
Progression
- Limit decision leader to two touches to sharpen choices
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m, two goals
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Normal game rules. Each team nominates a playmaker who can move freely. Goals count double if the playmaker clearly reads the moment—slowing play, switching, or accelerating at the right time during the build-up.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising moments to control or attack
- Supporting the playmaker’s decisions
- Simple choices: hold, move, or go
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive and observe natural behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Free game with no conditions. Observe whether players naturally read the moment and take responsibility for decisions.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence to take ownership
- Calm decision-making under pressure
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident to read the moment and choose the right action
Technical Detail | Scanning, body shape, first touch
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Read it.” / “What’s the moment?”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Playing on autopilot → encourage head-up pause
Praise Language | “Great decision.” / “Nice read.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, bravery, responsibility
Reflection Question | “What made you choose that action?”
Session Design Note | Leadership roles help players take ownership of decisions
Language Note | Light Belco terms: playmaker, intelligent, brave

Players feel more confident expressing the Belco identity in games — brave on the ball, connected with teammates, and intelligent in decisions.

- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: Pairs, one ball between two
- Equipment: Cones

One player leads with the ball using feints, pauses, turns, and bursts of speed. The partner mirrors movement. On a cue, the ball carrier chooses an action: drive, turn, or slow. Roles swap regularly. The block blends scanning, tempo change, and confidence on the ball before moving straight into games.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with freedom
- Expressive movement and rhythm
- Feeling when to go fast or slow
- Prepare players for mixed game demands

- Players: 3v3 games
- Equipment: Mini goals, balls

Short free 3v3 games. Normal rules. Emphasis is on quick restarts and natural transitions. Players rotate opponents every 2–3 minutes to create variety and energy.

- Immediate engagement
- Playing with energy and intent

- Numbers: Even teams

- Collective effort and intelligence

Element | Detail

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 10 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Mini Tournament / Review | Week 1 | Belco Cues Refresher
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident recognising and using simple Belco cues to guide decisions during games.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x25m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players dribble freely with turns and direction changes. On a cue from the coach (“brave,” “open,” “support”), players respond immediately:
- Brave → drive forward
- Open → open body and change direction
- Support → stop ball, move to find a passing angleLater, add a passive defender to create light pressure. Flow stays quick and playful, reinforcing cue recognition.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with freedom
- Immediate response to simple cues
- Rhythm without stopping play
- Prepare players to recognise cues in games
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: Two 20x15m mini pitches
- Players: 3v3 games
- Equipment: Mini goals, balls
Description
Free 3v3 games. Coach occasionally calls a Belco cue (“compact,” “brave,” “open”). Players adjust behaviour immediately but play continues. Rotate teams every 2–3 minutes.
Coaching Focus
- Listening while playing
- Quick adjustment of behaviour
- Playing with energy and intent
Progression
- Players call the cues themselves after a regain or goal
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: Three small-sided pitches (20x15m)
- Players: 3v3 or 4v4
- Organisation: Round-robin mini games
Description
Each game focuses loosely on one cue:
- Pitch 1: Brave (drive, engage opponents)
- Pitch 2: Compact (stay connected when defending)
- Pitch 3: Intelligent (scan, decide, control tempo)Games rotate every few minutes so players experience all cues.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when a cue fits the moment
- Applying cues naturally, not forcing them
- Repetition through varied games
Progression
- Remove named focus and let players choose cues themselves
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m (or multiple small pitches)
- Players: Tournament format (4v4 / 5v5)
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Mini tournament with normal rules. Coach uses short Belco cues only during play. No stoppages for explanation—players learn by reacting in the moment.
Coaching Focus
- Belco language appearing naturally
- Decision-making under pressure
- Team connection and identity
Progression
- Final game with players responsible for cueing each other
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Even teams
- Area: 45x30m
- Direction: Two goals
Description
Final free game. No coach cues. Observe whether players naturally use Belco language and behaviours without prompting.
Coaching Focus
- Confidence and ownership
- Collective understanding of identity
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident using Belco cues in games
Technical Detail | First touch, scanning, balance, movement
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Be brave.” / “Stay compact.” / “Play smart.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Overthinking cues → remind players cues guide, not stop play
Praise Language | “That’s Belco.” / “Great response to the cue.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, bravery, communication
Reflection Question | “Which cue helped you most today?”
Session Design Note | Review session reinforces shared language through play
Language Note | Belco cues used simply and consistently

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 10 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2— Belco-blue underline —
U12 | Mini Tournament / Review | Week 2 | Coach–Player Reflection
Session Outcomes
Players feel more confident talking about their game, recognising their strengths, and understanding simple next steps.
Ball Mastery Block (5–8 mins – Optional)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m grid
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with turns, sole pulls, and direction changes. Tempo is relaxed. On a cue, players stop the ball, look up, and jog to a cone where a coach asks a simple question (e.g. “What did you enjoy last session?”). Players answer briefly, return to the ball, and continue. Flow stays calm and unpressured.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with low intensity
- Relaxed rhythm and enjoyment
- Prepare players mentally for reflection and conversation
Activation / Intro (8–10 mins)
Setup
- Area: Two 20x15m mini pitches
- Players: 3v3
- Equipment: Mini goals, balls
Description
Free 3v3 games with longer game times than usual. Coach observes quietly. Between games, players rotate teams and are asked one short reflection question before restarting (e.g. “What went well there?”).
Coaching Focus
- Comfortable game rhythm
- Confidence to speak briefly
- Playing with enjoyment and intent
Progression
- Players ask each other one question before restarting
Technical / Skill Practice (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: Three small-sided pitches (20x15m)
- Players: 3v3 or 4v4
- Organisation: Free rotation
Description
Games continue with no conditions. Each pitch has a reflection theme:
- Pitch 1: What am I good at?
- Pitch 2: What helped my team?
- Pitch 3: What do I want to try more?
After each short game, players share one thought with the coach before rotating.
Coaching Focus
- Honest reflection
- Positive language
- Awareness of self and team
Progression
- Players choose which pitch they want to play on based on the question
Game / Main Practice (20–25 mins)
Setup
- Field: 40x30m
- Players: 5v5
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
One longer free game. No conditions. Coach only intervenes at natural breaks to ask simple questions. Focus stays on letting the game breathe and players feel ownership.
Coaching Focus
- Expression without pressure
- Natural application of learning
- Team enjoyment
Progression
- Captain-led teams with players organising themselves
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Numbers: Whole group
- Area: Off the pitch or centre circle
- Direction: Seated circle
Description
Players sit together with the coach. Each player answers one simple reflection question:
- “Something I’m proud of this cycle”
- “Something I want to keep improving”
Keep answers short. End on a positive group message.
Coaching Focus
- Listening and respect
- Confidence to speak
- Ending the cycle positively
Coaching Information (U12)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players confident reflecting on their game
Technical Detail | Awareness, communication, self-evaluation
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “You own your game.” / “Be proud, then improve.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Overthinking answers → remind players short is fine
Praise Language | “Good reflection.” / “That’s honest.”
Behaviours to Reward | Honesty, effort, confidence
Reflection Question | “What are you most proud of?”
Session Design Note | Low pressure and conversation close the cycle
Language Note | Belco identity language used positively
