Equipment: Cones to mark area
Description
Players work in pairs facing each other. One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and direction changes; the partner mirrors without the ball. Roles switch every 45–60 seconds. Flow stays continuous, building rhythm and reactions before moving straight into the Activation game.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and continuous movement
Freedom to explore disguises and changes of pace
Build rhythm and tempo without stopping play
Prepare players physically and mentally for pressing work
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x20m
Players: 5v5 + 2 neutrals (7v5 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with two neutrals always playing with the team on the ball. The out-of-possession team can only send three players to press at any one time. Regain = 1 point; 6 passes = 1 point. Play restarts immediately with the coach feeding a new ball.
Main Rule: Only the nearest three can press — others must stay compact and screen.
Coaching Focus
First presser closes space, next two lock angles
Early support on the press, not late chasing
Rhythm: go together, not one-by-one
Progression
Reduce area to increase pressure
Remove one neutral to balance numbers
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x25m, split into two horizontal zones
Players: 6v6 (one team building, one pressing)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional practice. The building team starts in the back zone and must play into the front zone to score by dribbling or passing through. When possession is lost, the nearest three press immediately for 5 seconds to win it back. If they regain, they can attack straight away.
Key Condition: Only the nearest three can enter the pressing zone during the 5-second window.
Coaching Focus
First movement is forward and aggressive
Second and third presser close passing lanes
Body shape to see both ball and cover
Progression
Reduce the 5-second window to 3 seconds
Allow all players to press if the first regain fails (realism)
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal directional game. On loss of possession, teams earn double goals if they regain within 6 seconds using the nearest three. If the press is broken, play continues normally. Restarts are quick from the coach to maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
Recognise press triggers (bad touch, back to goal, sideline)
Connect short and compact before spreading again
Maintain team rhythm after regain
Progression
Remove the double-goal incentive
Let the game decide when and how to press
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8 (numbers available)
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. No constraints. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players press together without prompting?
Are the nearest three recognised naturally?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players consistently connect as the nearest three to press and regain while staying compact
Technical Detail | Closing speed, support angle, body shape, timing of the first step
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Nearest three go.” / “Arrive together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | One player presses alone → remind: wait half a second, go together
Praise Language | “Good trigger recognition.” / “Great connection on the press.”
Behaviours to Reward | Sprint to press, covering teammate, communication
Reflection Question | “What helped you win it back quickly?”
Session Design Note | Early practices use numerical support to clarify pressing roles
Language Note | Use Belco terms for recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Press & Regain | Week 1 | Reaction to Loss
Session Outcomes
Players react immediately on loss by reconnecting with the nearest support to delay, press, or regain.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players drive with the ball across their lane (1v0), turn, and return. On the coach’s call, the partner steps in to create a brief 1v1. The attacker must protect, change direction, or release the ball quickly, then swap roles. Flow is continuous and sharp, moving straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact with purposeful carrying
Quick transition from repetition to opposition
Build tempo and readiness for loss moments
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When possession is lost, the losing team has 3 seconds to react and touch the ball (press or screen). Success = keep play alive or force a mistake; failure = opponents keep possession freely. Immediate restarts from the coach.
Main Rule: First reaction must be forward or connecting — no retreating.
Coaching Focus
Instant reaction on loss (first step matters)
Nearest player delays; next two connect
Rhythm: react, then decide
Progression
Remove one neutral to increase realism
Reduce reaction window to 2 seconds
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m, two end zones
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional game. Teams score by receiving in the end zone. On loss, the nearest two must delay and connect while the third supports from behind. If the ball is regained within 5 seconds, play continues immediately toward goal.
Key Condition: First action after loss is to slow or block forward play.
Coaching Focus
First defender slows and shows direction
Second defender closes the obvious pass
Third defender supports underneath
Progression
Allow free pressing after 5 seconds
Reduce space between zones
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with a scoring incentive: double goal if a team scores within 8 seconds of regaining possession. If the first reaction fails, teams recover into shape and play on. Restarts are quick to maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
Recognise loss moments early
Decide: delay, press, or recover
Stay compact during the reaction
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let outcomes emerge naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with minimal intervention. Normal football rules.
Coaching Focus
Observe automatic reactions on loss
Look for connected responses without prompts
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players react immediately and collectively when possession is lost
Technical Detail | First step speed, angle of approach, timing of support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “React first.” / “Nearest connects.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Players drop early → cue: delay first, then recover
Praise Language | “Great first reaction.” / “Good connection after loss.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, communication, willingness to support
Reflection Question | “What did you do first when we lost it?”
Session Design Note | Early activities include numerical support to clarify reaction roles
Language Note | Belco terms used for recognition, not explanation

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Press & Regain | Week 2 | Counter-Press on Closed Ball
Session Outcomes
Players recognise a closed ball and counter-press immediately with the nearest support to regain or force a mistake.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: All players, one ball each
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely, combining inside–outside touches, rolls, and direction changes. On a signal, players stop the ball, change direction, and re-accelerate. Rhythm builds through continuous movement and repeated changes, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and smooth rhythm
Freedom to explore tempo changes
Prepare coordination and awareness for pressing cues
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 5v5 + 2 neutrals
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When a player receives with back to play or tight pressure (closed ball), the defending team can counter-press with the nearest three immediately. Regain = 1 point; 6 passes = 1 point. Quick restarts from the coach.
Main Rule: Closed ball = go now.
Coaching Focus
Recognise the closed body shape
First presser arrives tight; others lock angles
Compact distances before pressing
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x25m, central corridor marked
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional practice. Play is live through the central corridor. When the ball enters the corridor and the receiver is closed, the defending team has 4 seconds to counter-press with the nearest three. If they regain, they attack immediately toward goal.
Key Condition: Press is triggered by body shape, not the pass.
Coaching Focus
Read cues: touch, body angle, pressure
Arrive together and compact
Decide quickly: win it or force long
Progression
Reduce counter-press window to 3 seconds
Allow all players to press after the window (realism)
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with incentive: double goal if scored within 7 seconds of a counter-press regain on a closed ball. If the counter-press fails, teams recover into shape and play continues. Fast restarts maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
Spot closed-ball moments early
Support the press from the right distance
Stay compact after regain
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let decisions emerge naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Coach observes application only.
Coaching Focus
Do players counter-press instinctively on closed balls?
Are distances compact without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players consistently counter-press when the opponent receives on a closed ball
Technical Detail | First step speed, pressing angle, distance between pressers
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Closed — go.” / “Arrive together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Late or solo press → cue: wait, then go as three
Praise Language | “Great trigger recognition.” / “Strong compact press.”
Behaviours to Reward | Reading cues, quick support, bravery to step in
Reflection Question | “What told you the ball was closed?”
Session Design Note | Early activities use space and support to clarify pressing triggers
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Press & Regain | Week 2 | Recover Shape Fast
Session Outcomes
Players recover quickly and collectively into a compact shape after the first press is broken.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players work in pairs. One player carries the ball while the partner applies light, passive pressure from different angles. The ball carrier shields, turns away, and accelerates into space. After each turn, roles switch immediately. Flow stays continuous and sharp, leading directly into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact with balance and control
Feel pressure and protect first
Prepare players for contact and recovery actions
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 5v5 + 2 neutrals
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When the defending team presses and the ball is played through or away from pressure, the defending team must recover into a compact shape before pressing again. Coach counts aloud “1–2–3” to set recovery tempo. Regain after recovery = 1 point.
Main Rule: No pressing until shape is recovered.
Coaching Focus
Immediate sprint to recover, not jogging
Compact distances side-to-side and front-to-back
Communication while moving
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space to increase speed of recovery
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m, split into three vertical channels
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional game. Teams attack toward goals. If the pressing team is played through one channel, they must recover into the central channel before re-engaging. A regain after recovery allows immediate attack.
Key Condition: Recovery run first, press second.
Coaching Focus
First reaction is to protect central space
Players recover on angles, not straight lines
Stay connected while moving back
Progression
Remove channel restriction
Allow free pressing after recovery (realism)
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goal if a team scores after regaining possession following a full team recovery. If teams fail to recover quickly, play continues with no bonus. Fast restarts keep intensity high.
Coaching Focus
Recognise when the press is beaten
Recover together, then step again
Maintain compactness while retreating
Progression
Remove scoring incentive
Allow the game to flow freely
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players recover shape instinctively?
Are distances compact without reminders?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players recover quickly into a compact shape after the press is broken
Technical Detail | Recovery runs, body orientation, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Recover first.” / “Get compact.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Players chase from behind → cue: protect space, then step
Praise Language | “Great recovery run.” / “Good compact shape.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, organisation, willingness to sprint back
Reflection Question | “What helped us get compact quickly?”
Session Design Note | Early activities delay pressing to reinforce recovery habits
Language Note | Belco terms used for recognition, not explanation

heading: U13 Cycle 3, week 3, session 1

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Press & Regain | Week 3 | Transition Games: Win, Play, React
Session Outcomes
Players connect actions across transitions — winning the ball, playing with purpose, and reacting immediately to the next moment.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players face each other. One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and direction changes; the partner mirrors without the ball. On a signal, the leader releases the ball and both sprint to collect a new one, switching roles. Flow remains continuous and sharp before moving straight into Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and constant movement
Sharp reactions to visual cues
Build rhythm and readiness for transition moments
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When the defending team regains, they must complete one forward or connecting pass within 3 seconds. If they lose it again, they immediately react to press or delay. Play restarts quickly from the coach.
Main Rule: Win it → play it → react.
Coaching Focus
Immediate awareness after regain
First decision simple and purposeful
Fast reaction if possession is lost again
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce time window to 2 seconds
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m, two mini goals at each end
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, mini goals
Description
Directional transition game. Teams score in the opponent’s mini goals. On a regain, the team has 5 seconds to attack before the opposition can fully recover. If the attack breaks down, players must react immediately to press or recover shape.
Key Condition: Every regain creates a new decision moment.
Coaching Focus
Head up immediately after winning the ball
Support angles to play forward or secure possession
Instant reaction when momentum changes
Progression
Reduce time to attack to 4 seconds
Increase pressure by reducing space
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with transition scoring: double goal if scored within 8 seconds of a regain. If the regain fails to lead to an attack, teams must react quickly to the next defensive moment. Fast restarts maintain tempo and rhythm.
Coaching Focus
Connect actions across transitions
Recognise when to play fast vs secure
Stay compact immediately after loss
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game flow naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with minimal conditions. Normal football rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players link win–play–react naturally?
Are decisions made without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players connect regains, attacking decisions, and defensive reactions
Technical Detail | First touch after regain, support angle, transition speed
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it.” / “Play with purpose.” / “React again.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing play after regain → cue: secure first if needed
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” / “Good decision after the win.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, composure, immediate effort
Reflection Question | “What helped you decide quickly after the regain?”
Session Design Note | Repeated transitions create rhythm and decision learning
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Press & Regain | Week 3 | Collective Pressing Challenge
Session Outcomes
Players press collectively with clear triggers, shared timing, and compact distances to regain or force predictable play.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players drive the ball across the lane (1v0), turn, and return. On the coach’s call, the partner steps in to apply live pressure for a brief 1v1. The ball carrier must protect, escape, or release quickly. Roles switch immediately. Flow stays sharp and competitive before moving straight into Activation.
Coaching Focus
Purposeful carrying and sharp turns
Quick adjustment from no pressure to pressure
Prepare players for competitive pressing actions
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with one neutral supporting the team on the ball. The defending team can only win the ball if at least three players are involved in the press. Solo regains do not count. Successful collective regain = 1 point; 6 passes = 1 point. Immediate restarts from the coach.
Main Rule: Regains only count if they’re collective.
Coaching Focus
Wait for support before stepping in
Clear roles: press, cover, screen
Compact distances between the three
Progression
Remove the neutral
Reduce area size
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m, divided into thirds
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional game. Teams build from one third to the next. Pressing team scores a point by forcing a regain or a backward pass through collective pressure (minimum three involved). If the press is broken, players must recover and reconnect before pressing again.
Key Condition: Press together or don’t press.
Coaching Focus
Trigger recognition (touch, direction, sideline)
First player sets direction, others lock space
Team moves as one unit
Progression
Reduce time allowed to regain
Remove thirds for game realism
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with a collective pressing challenge: double goals if a team regains through a clear collective press (coach judgement) and scores within 10 seconds. If pressing is disconnected, play continues with no bonus. Quick restarts maintain intensity.
Coaching Focus
Collective movement over individual effort
Communication before and during the press
Compact shape behind the press
Progression
Remove double-goal condition
Let pressing decisions emerge naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. No constraints. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players choose to press together?
Is the team compact during and after the press?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players apply collective pressing with shared timing and compactness
Technical Detail | Pressing angles, distance between units, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Together or wait.” / “Press as one.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Solo pressing → cue: connect first, then go
Praise Language | “Great collective press.” / “Good patience before stepping.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, discipline, teamwork
Reflection Question | “What helped us press together successfully?”
Session Design Note | Constraints reward collective solutions over individual effort
Language Note | Belco terms used for recognition, not explanation

heading: U13 Cycle 4, week 1, session 1
headingPath: U13 sessions > U13 Cycle 4, week 1, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Switch of Play & Width | Week 1 | Width Awareness: Stretch the Field
Session Outcomes
Players recognise when to use width to stretch the opposition and create space to play through or switch.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: All players, one ball each
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside–outside touches, rolls, and changes of direction. On a signal, players open their body and push the ball into a new space before continuing. Rhythm builds through constant movement and scanning, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and smooth rhythm
Opening body to see space
Prepare players for awareness and spacing decisions
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m with two wide channels marked
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. Teams score a point by playing into a wide channel and receiving under control. After using one side, the next point must come from the opposite side. Play restarts quickly from the coach.
Main Rule: Use the width before playing inside.
Coaching Focus
Early recognition of free wide space
Support angles to play out, not force through
Patience to circulate and stretch
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce channel width to increase accuracy
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with wide lanes on each side
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goals. Goals count only if the team has used a wide lane in the build-up within the last three passes. Players in wide lanes are free to receive but must play out within two touches.
Key Condition: Width creates the next action.
Coaching Focus
Timing of movement to the wide space
Body shape to receive facing forward
Decide when to play wide vs switch inside
Progression
Allow defenders to enter wide lanes
Reduce touches in wide areas
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team scores after switching play from one side of the field to the other. If play becomes narrow, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
Recognise when one side is crowded
Use width to stretch before penetrating
Maintain connection across the field
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game decide when to use width
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. No constraints. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally use the width?
Is the field stretched in possession?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players use width to stretch opponents and open central space
Technical Detail | First touch toward space, support angle, body orientation
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Make it wide.” / “Stretch them first.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing play inside → cue: go around to go through
Praise Language | “Great width.” / “Good patience to switch.”
Behaviours to Reward | Scanning, composure, willingness to move wide
Reflection Question | “What changed when we used the width?”
Session Design Note | Early constraints exaggerate width to support recognition
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U13 Cycle 4, week 1, session 2
headingPath: U13 sessions > U13 Cycle 4, week 1, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Switch of Play & Width | Week 1 | Open Body to Find Far Side
Session Outcomes
Players receive on an open body shape to see and access the far side of the field.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players work in pairs. One player carries the ball while the partner applies light, angled pressure. The ball carrier protects, opens up, turns away from pressure, and plays into space before resetting. Roles switch continuously with no stoppages, flowing directly into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Feel pressure before turning
Open body to escape and see space
Prepare for receiving under pressure
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m with two wide channels
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. A point is scored when a player receives on an open body and successfully plays to the opposite side. Play continues immediately with quick restarts.
Main Rule: You must open to play away from pressure.
Coaching Focus
Scan before receiving
Open body to see both sides
Play away from pressure, not into it
Progression
Remove one neutral
Limit touches to increase speed of play
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with wide lanes
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goals. Goals only count if the attacking move includes a switch of play initiated by a player receiving on an open body shape. Wide players must receive facing forward or across the field.
Key Condition: Body shape determines options.
Coaching Focus
Side-on receiving position
First touch sets the next action
Timing support to offer the far-side option
Progression
Allow defenders to step into wide lanes
Reduce time on the ball
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if the attack includes a clear switch started by an open-body receive. If players receive square or closed, play continues with no bonus. Fast restarts maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
Recognise pressure and space early
Open up before the ball arrives
Connect the far side quickly
Progression
Remove the scoring incentive
Allow the game to flow freely
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes application only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally open their body?
Is the far side recognised without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players receive open to access far-side options
Technical Detail | Body orientation, first touch direction, scanning
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Open up.” / “See far.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Square receive → cue: half-turn before the ball arrives
Praise Language | “Great open body.” / “Good switch decision.”
Behaviours to Reward | Scanning, composure, awareness of far side
Reflection Question | “What did opening your body allow you to see?”
Session Design Note | Constraints exaggerate body shape to support recognition

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Switch of Play & Width | Week 2 | Build One Way, Finish the Other
Session Outcomes
Players recognise when to build on one side to attract pressure, then switch quickly to finish on the far side.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players face each other. One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and direction changes; the partner mirrors without the ball. On a signal, the leader accelerates away and both players move to find new space, then switch roles. Rhythm stays continuous and sharp, leading straight into Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and constant movement
Sharp changes of direction and pace
Prepare players to draw pressure before escaping
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m with wide channels on both sides
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. Teams must complete three passes on one side before they are allowed to score a point by playing into the opposite wide channel. Play resets quickly from the coach.
Main Rule: Draw pressure one way before playing the other.
Coaching Focus
Patience to build and attract pressure
Awareness of opposite-side space
Timing the switch, not forcing it
Progression
Reduce required passes to two
Remove one neutral to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with wide lanes
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goals. To score, teams must build through one wide lane or half-space, then switch to the opposite side before finishing. Wide players are free to receive but must play forward or inside within two touches.
Key Condition: Build to fix, switch to finish.
Coaching Focus
Support angles to keep the ball on one side
Body shape to play out of pressure
Speed and accuracy of the switch
Progression
Allow defenders to step into wide lanes
Limit touches in central areas
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if the team scores after switching play from one side of the pitch to the other in the build-up. If teams attack down the same side, goals count as normal. Fast restarts keep rhythm high.
Coaching Focus
Recognise when one side is overloaded
Stay connected across the field
Switch with purpose, not just for variety
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Allow the game to flow freely
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally build and switch?
Is the far side used to finish attacks?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players build on one side to attract pressure and finish on the far side
Technical Detail | Support angle, body orientation, weight and timing of switch pass
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Fix them here.” / “Finish over there.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Switching too early → cue: draw them first
Praise Language | “Great patience.” / “Perfect switch to finish.”
Behaviours to Reward | Scanning, composure, willingness to stay wide
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to switch?”
Session Design Note | Constraints exaggerate build-up direction to support recognition
Language Note | Belco terms used for recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Switch of Play & Width | Week 2 | Use the Pivot Switch
Session Outcomes
Players recognise the pivot as a safe connection to switch play and change the point of attack.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players carry the ball across the lane (1v0), turn, and return. On the coach’s call, the partner steps in to apply brief live pressure (1v1). The ball carrier protects, turns out, or plays away quickly, then roles switch. Flow is continuous and sharp, leading straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Purposeful carrying and turning
Protect first, then play out
Prepare players for receiving under pressure and connecting centrally
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m with two wide channels
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (central neutral acts as pivot)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with one neutral fixed centrally (pivot) and two free neutrals. Teams score a point by switching the ball from one wide channel to the other through the pivot. Play resets quickly from the coach.
Main Rule: Switches must go through the pivot.
Coaching Focus
Find the pivot early under pressure
Pivot opens body to play away from pressure
Support angles around the pivot
Progression
Remove one wide neutral
Limit pivot to two touches
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with wide lanes
Players: 6v6 with one designated pivot per team (inside)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goals. The pivot must be used at least once in the build-up before switching play to the opposite side. The pivot plays one- or two-touch, linking pressure to space.
Key Condition: Pivot connects, others move.
Coaching Focus
Pivot’s body shape to see both sides
Timing of movement to offer angles
Speed of circulation through the middle
Progression
Allow defenders to mark the pivot tightly
Remove touch limits for realism
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if the attacking team switches play via a central pivot before scoring. If teams bypass the pivot, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain rhythm.
Coaching Focus
Recognise central pressure
Use the pivot to escape and switch
Stay connected across the width of the field
Progression
Remove scoring incentive
Let the game decide when to use the pivot
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally find the pivot to switch?
Is the far side used after central connection?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players use the pivot to connect and switch play effectively
Technical Detail | Open body shape, first touch direction, pass weight
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Find the pivot.” / “Through the middle.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Playing wide under pressure → cue: reset through the pivot
Praise Language | “Great connection inside.” / “Good switch through the pivot.”
Behaviours to Reward | Scanning, offering central support, composure
Reflection Question | “How did the pivot help us switch?”
Session Design Note | Constraints exaggerate central connection to support recognition
Language Note | Belco terms used for recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Switch of Play & Width | Week 3 | Switch + Penetrate: From Wide to Central
Session Outcomes
Players switch play to create space wide, then recognise the moment to penetrate centrally with speed and purpose.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: All players, one ball each
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside–outside touches, rolls, and changes of direction. On a signal, players push the ball into a new space, open their body, and accelerate forward. Rhythm is continuous, with frequent changes of direction to prepare players for switching and driving inside.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and smooth rhythm
Open body before moving forward
Prepare coordination and tempo for penetration moments
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m with two wide channels
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. Teams score a point by switching play to a wide channel and then playing one penetrative pass or dribble inside within three seconds. Play restarts quickly from the coach.
Main Rule: Switch first, penetrate next.
Coaching Focus
Speed of the switch to unbalance
Early movement inside after the wide receive
Decision: drive, pass, or secure
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce time window to penetrate
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with wide lanes and a central scoring zone
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goals. Goals count only if the attack includes a switch to a wide lane followed by a penetration into the central zone (pass or dribble) before finishing. Wide players are encouraged to receive facing forward.
Key Condition: Width creates the lane to go inside.
Coaching Focus
Timing of central runs after the switch
Body shape to attack inside
Choosing the right penetration option
Progression
Allow defenders to press aggressively in wide lanes
Reduce space in the central zone
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if the team scores after switching play wide and then penetrating centrally within the same phase. If teams stay wide without penetration, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain rhythm.
Coaching Focus
Recognise when the centre opens after the switch
Speed and intent to attack inside
Maintain connection around the ball
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Allow the game to flow naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes application only.
Coaching Focus
Do players combine width and penetration naturally?
Is the centre attacked at the right moment?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players use width to open space and penetrate centrally with intent
Technical Detail | First touch forward, body orientation, timing of central movement
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Wide first.” / “Attack inside.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Staying wide too long → cue: switch, then go
Praise Language | “Great switch and drive.” / “Good timing into the middle.”
Behaviours to Reward | Scanning, bravery to attack space, connection
Reflection Question | “What told you the centre was open?”
Session Design Note | Constraints exaggerate switch–penetrate timing for recognition
Language Note | Belco terms used for recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Switch of Play & Width | Week 3 | Full-Pitch Decision Game
Session Outcomes
Players recognise when to stay compact or stretch the field, making independent decisions to switch, penetrate, or secure possession.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players work in pairs. One player carries the ball while the partner applies light pressure from different angles. The ball carrier protects, turns out, and accelerates into space before resetting. Roles switch continuously with no stoppages, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Balance and protection under contact
Turning into space with awareness
Prepare players for decision-making under pressure
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 35x30m
Players: 5v5 + 2 neutrals
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. Teams score a point by either switching play or penetrating centrally — the choice is theirs. Consecutive points must be scored using a different solution (can’t repeat the same option twice). Immediate restarts keep tempo high.
Main Rule: Choose the best solution — not the same one.
Coaching Focus
Scanning to assess space and pressure
Decision-making based on opposition shape
Smooth rhythm between actions
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space to increase realism
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m (near full width), goals at each end
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game with minimal constraints. Teams can score in any way, but goals count double if the attack shows a clear decision to either:
Switch play to exploit width, or
Penetrate centrally when space opens
Coach judgement defines clarity of decision, not execution quality.
Key Condition: Read the picture, then act.
Coaching Focus
Recognition of space vs congestion
Body shape to keep multiple options alive
Tempo control: speed up or slow down
Progression
Remove double-goal condition
Let the game run fully free
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: Full-width half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with one simple condition: coach does not guide decisions. Players must manage width, switches, and penetration independently. Restarts are quick to maintain rhythm.
Coaching Focus
Independent decision-making
Use of width without instruction
Staying connected while stretched
Progression
No progression — realism is the goal
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: Match numbers available
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game. Normal football rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players solve problems without prompts?
Is width used naturally within team shape?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players independently choose when to switch, penetrate, or secure
Technical Detail | Scanning, body orientation, tempo control
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Read it.” / “Choose the best option.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing a switch → cue: what does the picture show?
Praise Language | “Good decision.” / “Great awareness.”
Behaviours to Reward | Scanning, patience, brave choices
Reflection Question | “Why did you choose that option?”
Session Design Note | Constraints are reduced to test true understanding
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Finishing Under Pressure | Week 1 | Finishing from Build-Up
Session Outcomes
Players connect the build-up to finishing, recognising when and how to arrive in scoring positions under pressure.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players face each other. One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and direction changes; the partner mirrors without the ball. On a signal, the leader accelerates away and finishes with a controlled strike into a mini target or gate, then roles switch. Flow is continuous, building rhythm before moving into Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and disguise
Sharp change from build-up movement to finishing action
Prepare players mentally for end-product moments
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, two mini goals
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. To score, the team must complete a short build-up (minimum 3 passes) before finishing into either mini goal. If possession is lost, immediate reaction to regain and continue play. Quick restarts from the coach.
Main Rule: Build first, then finish.
Coaching Focus
Patience and connection before the shot
Movement to support underneath the ball
Quality decision: shoot or reset
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce touches to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a central finishing zone
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. Teams build toward goal. Finishes must come from inside the central zone after a combination (wall pass, third-man, or carry). If the shot is blocked or missed, play continues immediately with transition.
Key Condition: Build to arrive — don’t rush the finish.
Coaching Focus
Timing of runs into the finishing zone
Body shape to shoot under pressure
Support around the ball after the shot
Progression
Add a recovering defender into the zone
Limit time inside the zone
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if the team scores following a clear build-up phase (coach judgement) rather than a direct or rushed shot. If teams shoot early, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
Recognise when the attack is ready to finish
Composure under pressure in front of goal
Stay connected after the shot (rebound awareness)
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Allow the game to flow freely
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes application only.
Coaching Focus
Do players connect build-up to finishing naturally?
Is patience shown before shooting?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players finish attacks that come from connected build-up play
Technical Detail | First touch to set shot, body balance, shot selection
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Build it.” / “Arrive to finish.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Rushed shots → cue: one more connection
Praise Language | “Great patience.” / “Good arrival to finish.”
Behaviours to Reward | Composure, timing of runs, awareness of teammates
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to finish?”
Session Design Note | Early constraints slow play to support quality finishing moments
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Finishing Under Pressure | Week 1 | Arrive Late in the Box
Session Outcomes
Players recognise when to arrive late into the box to finish with composure under pressure.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones, mini goals or gates
Description
Players drive with the ball across the lane (1v0), turn, and pass to their partner. On the return pass, the receiver arrives late to finish into a mini goal or gate. On the coach’s call, the partner applies light pressure to create a brief 1v1 before the finish. Roles switch continuously with no stoppages.
Coaching Focus
Timing the forward movement, not rushing
Arriving onto the ball with balance
Prepare players for finishing after movement
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, two mini goals
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. To score, a player must pass first, then arrive late to finish into a mini goal. Standing in the goal zone waiting is not allowed. Immediate restarts keep rhythm high.
Main Rule: Pass, then arrive.
Coaching Focus
Delay run until space opens
Awareness of timing and angle of arrival
Composure on the finish
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space around the goals
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a marked box area
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. Teams build toward goal. Goals only count if the scorer arrives into the box after the pass is played (no early runs). Defenders recover to apply pressure from behind or the side. Play continues on rebounds and blocks.
Key Condition: Timing beats speed.
Coaching Focus
Reading when the ball carrier can play forward
Body shape to finish first time or with one touch
Supporting rebounds and second balls
Progression
Add a recovering defender starting goal-side
Limit touches inside the box
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if scored by a late-arriving runner into the box. If players enter the box early and wait, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
Patience in the build-up
Late movement to lose defenders
Finishing with control under pressure
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Allow the game to flow freely
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes application only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally delay and arrive late?
Is timing improving without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players time late runs into the box to finish under pressure
Technical Detail | Timing of run, body balance, finishing technique
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Wait… now go.” / “Arrive to score.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Arriving too early → cue: hold the edge, attack the space
Praise Language | “Perfect timing.” / “Great late run.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, awareness, composure
Reflection Question | “What told you it was the right moment to arrive?”
Session Design Note | Constraints delay runs to highlight timing over speed
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Finishing Under Pressure | Week 2 | Combination to Finish
Session Outcomes
Players combine with teammates near goal to create finishing chances under pressure.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: All players, one ball each
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely using inside–outside touches, rolls, and directional changes. On a signal, players play a short wall pass with a nearby teammate and accelerate into a new space before continuing. Rhythm stays continuous and fluid, leading directly into Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and smooth rhythm
Awareness of nearby teammates
Prepare players for quick combinations before finishing
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, two mini goals
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. To score, the attacking team must use a combination (wall pass or third-player) before finishing into a mini goal. If possession is lost, immediate reaction to press and continue play. Quick restarts maintain tempo.
Main Rule: Combine before you finish.
Coaching Focus
Support close to the ball
Timing and angle of combination runs
Decide quickly: play back, through, or around
Progression
Remove one neutral
Limit touches to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a marked finishing zone
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goal. Goals count only if the finish comes after a clear combination action (wall pass, third-man run, or overlap) inside or just outside the finishing zone. Defenders recover to apply live pressure.
Key Condition: Combine to create the finish, not force it.
Coaching Focus
Body shape to play first time
Timing of supporting runs near the ball
Composure to finish after combination
Progression
Add a recovering defender starting close to goal
Reduce space in the finishing zone

Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if scored following a clear combination in the final third. If players shoot without support or combination, goals count as normal. Fast restarts keep rhythm high.
Coaching Focus
Recognise when combination is on
Stay connected around the ball
Finish with control under pressure
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Allow the game to flow freely
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally combine near goal?
Is finishing linked to connection rather than individual shots?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players finish chances created through combination play
Technical Detail | Weight of pass, first touch, finishing technique
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Connect to finish.” / “One sets, one scores.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Shooting early → cue: find the extra pass
Praise Language | “Great combination.” / “Good patience to finish.”
Behaviours to Reward | Support runs, awareness, composure

Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Finishing Under Pressure | Week 2 | Cut-Back & Second Line
Session Outcomes
Players recognise cut-back moments and arrive from the second line to finish with control under pressure.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players work in pairs. One player carries the ball while the partner applies light pressure from behind or the side. The ball carrier protects, turns away, and plays into space before resetting. Roles rotate continuously with no stoppages, building rhythm before moving into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Balance and protection under contact
Turning away from pressure
Prepare players for receiving and finishing under pressure
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m with end zones near goals
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, two mini goals
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. To score, the attacking team must play the ball into an end zone and then cut it back to a teammate arriving from outside the zone to finish. Players cannot wait inside the end zone. Immediate restarts keep tempo high.
Main Rule: Drive, cut back, arrive.
Coaching Focus
Timing of the second-line run
Awareness of space at the top of the box
Composed finish from movement
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space in the end zones
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with wide channels and a marked cut-back zone
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goal. Attacks must include a wide penetration followed by a cut-back into the central area for a finish by a second-line runner. Defenders recover to apply live pressure. Rebounds and blocks stay live.
Key Condition: First line goes past — second line finishes.
Coaching Focus
Decision to cut back vs shoot
Timing and angle of arrival from deep
Body shape to finish first time or with control
Progression
Add a recovering defender starting centrally
Limit touches for the finisher
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if scored from a cut-back finished by a second-line runner. If players crowd the box early, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain rhythm and realism.
Coaching Focus
Patience in the box
Awareness of second-line space
Finishing under defensive pressure
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Allow the game to flow freely
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally arrive from the second line?

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Finishing Under Pressure | Week 3 | Decision Finishing: Calm in the Box
Session Outcomes
Players stay composed in the box, choosing the right finish under pressure rather than rushing the action.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players face each other. One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and small direction changes; the partner mirrors without the ball. On a signal, the leader breaks away, creates a short shooting angle, and plays a controlled finish into a gate or mini goal. Roles switch immediately. Flow stays continuous and calm.
Coaching Focus
Disguise before action
Smooth tempo, not rushed touches
Prepare players for calm actions near goal
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, two mini goals
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. To score, the attacker must receive in a finishing area and choose one of three options: shoot, pass, or protect and reset. A rushed shot under pressure cancels the goal. Immediate restarts from the coach.
Main Rule: Pause, then decide.
Coaching Focus
Head up before finishing
Recognise pressure and support
Choose the best option, not the fastest
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space around the goals
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a marked box
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. Teams build toward goal. When the ball enters the box, the attacker must make a clear decision finish: shoot, slip a teammate, or protect the ball. Defenders recover to apply live pressure. Goals only count if the decision matches the situation (coach judgement).
Key Condition: Right decision beats fast decision.
Coaching Focus
Body shape to see goal and teammates
Composure with defender closing
Balance and control before striking
Progression
Add a second recovering defender
Limit touches for the finisher
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if the scorer shows clear composure (extra touch, pass, or delayed finish) under pressure in the box. Rushed finishes count as normal goals only. Fast restarts keep realism.
Coaching Focus
Calm behaviour under pressure
Awareness of teammates in the box
Confidence to delay the finish
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game flow freely
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players stay calm in the box naturally?
Are finishing decisions improving without prompts?

Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players make composed finishing decisions under pressure
Technical Detail | First touch control, body balance, shot selection
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Stay calm.” / “Choose the finish.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Snatched shots → cue: buy half a second
Praise Language | “Great composure.” / “Good decision in the box.”
Behaviours to Reward | Calmness, awareness, confidence
Reflection Question | “What helped you stay calm there?”
Session Design Note | Constraints reward decision quality over speed
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Finishing Under Pressure | Week 3 | Make It Count Tournament
Session Outcomes
Players finish chances with composure and intent in competitive, game-real situations where every action matters.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones, mini goals or gates
Description
Players drive with the ball across the lane (1v0), turn, and return. On the coach’s call, the partner steps in to apply live pressure (1v1). The attacker must protect, escape, or finish quickly. Roles switch immediately. Flow is continuous, competitive, and sharp before moving into Activation.
Coaching Focus
Purposeful carry into finishing action
Quick adjustment from no pressure to pressure
Prepare players for competitive scoring moments
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 3v3 + 3 neutrals (rotate teams)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, two mini goals
Description
Tournament-style possession game. Each round lasts 2–3 minutes. Teams score by finishing into mini goals after any type of build-up. Missed chances result in immediate transition for the opponent. Keep score across rounds.
Main Rule: Every chance counts.
Coaching Focus
Sharp focus in front of goal
Quick reaction after shots (rebound or recover)
Decision-making under time and score pressure
Progression
Reduce space to increase pressure
Limit touches near goals
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with goals
Players: 4 teams of 3 (two games running side by side)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Small-sided finishing games played simultaneously. Winners move up, losers move down (ladder format). Goals only count if the finish is controlled (no rushed first-touch shots unless clearly on). Games are short and intense.
Key Condition: Quality over quantity.
Coaching Focus
Composure before striking
Choosing the right finish under pressure
Staying mentally sharp between games
Progression
Reduce game time to raise stakes
Add a rule: goals must be scored within 6 seconds of entering the final third
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Tournament match with scoring values:
1 goal = normal finish
2 goals = composed finish (extra touch, pass, or clear decision)
Scoreboard is visible. Fast restarts maintain intensity and realism.
Coaching Focus
Calm behaviour under pressure
Smart decisions in the box
Emotional control when chances are missed
Progression
Remove scoring values
Let match pressure decide behaviour
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: Match numbers available
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game. Tournament final. Normal rules. No coach input.
Coaching Focus
Do players take responsibility in finishing moments?
Is composure shown without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players finish chances with composure in competitive game conditions
Technical Detail | First touch control, shot selection, balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Make it count.” / “Be calm, be brave.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Rushing under pressure → cue: trust your first touch
Praise Language | “Great composure.” / “You owned that moment.”
Behaviours to Reward | Confidence, calmness, accountability
Reflection Question | “What helped you score under pressure today?”
Session Design Note | Competitive formats increase realism and emotional pressure
Language Note | Belco terms used for recognition, not explanation

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Transition Moments | Week 1 | Fast Reaction Game: Win → Play
Session Outcomes
Players react instantly after regaining possession and connect the first action forward with tempo and purpose.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: All players, one ball each
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely using inside–outside touches, rolls, and directional changes. On a signal, players push the ball into a new space, open their body, and accelerate for 3–4 touches before resetting. Rhythm stays continuous and sharp, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
High ball contact and smooth rhythm
Open body before accelerating
Prepare players for immediate action after winning the ball
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When the defending team regains, they must complete one forward or breaking pass within 3 seconds to score a point. If they fail, play continues but no point is awarded. Immediate restarts from the coach.
Main Rule: Win it → play it forward.
Coaching Focus
First action after regain is positive
Head up immediately
Support angles appear quickly around the ball
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce the time window to 2 seconds
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with two mini goals at each end
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, mini goals
Description
Directional transition game. Teams score in the opponent’s mini goals. On regain, the team has 5 seconds to attack before the opposition can recover fully. If the first pass goes backward, the time window closes and play continues normally.
Key Condition: First pass sets the transition.
Coaching Focus
Body shape to play forward on the regain
Timing of support runs ahead of the ball
Decide quickly: play, carry, or secure
Progression
Reduce attack window to 4 seconds
Increase pressure by reducing space
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if scored within 8 seconds of regaining possession. If the transition slows, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain tempo and realism.
Coaching Focus
Recognise transition moments early
Speed of play without rushing
Stay connected after the first forward action
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game decide tempo naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players play forward instinctively after regains?
Is support immediate without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players connect regains to quick, purposeful forward play
Technical Detail | First touch direction, body orientation, pass choice
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it.” / “Play forward.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Stopping on regain → cue: first look forward
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” / “Good first action.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, urgency, support runs
Reflection Question | “What did you see right after we won it?”
Session Design Note | Time windows exaggerate transition speed for clarity
Language Note | Belco terms used for recognition, not explanation

U13 | Transition Moments | Week 1 | Play → Press
Session Outcomes
Players react immediately after losing possession, connecting the nearest players to press and delay before the opponent can play forward.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
One player carries the ball while the partner applies light, angled pressure. The ball carrier turns away, protects, and releases the ball. On release, roles switch instantly. Rhythm is continuous, preparing players for contact, balance, and immediate reaction after actions.
Coaching Focus
Balance and strength through the turn
Awareness of pressure
Prepare for quick reaction after playing the ball
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When a team loses possession, they must touch the ball within 3 seconds to score a point (press or block pass). If they fail, the opposition keeps possession freely. Immediate restarts from the coach.
Main Rule: Play it → lose it → press it.
Coaching Focus
Instant reaction after the pass
Nearest player presses, others connect
Forward mentality on loss
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce reaction window to 2 seconds
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with two horizontal zones
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional practice. Teams build forward. When possession is lost, the nearest three must press immediately for 5 seconds to delay or regain. If the press is successful, play continues forward. If broken, the team must recover shape and defend.
Key Condition: First reaction after playing is defensive.
Coaching Focus
Sprint reaction after the pass
Compact distances between pressers
Body shape to block forward options
Progression
Reduce pressing window to 4 seconds
Allow free pressing after the window (realism)
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team regains possession within 6 seconds of losing it and scores in the same phase. If the press fails, play continues normally. Fast restarts maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
Recognition of loss moments
Collective press rather than chasing
Staying compact behind the press
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let pressing decisions emerge naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players react automatically after losing the ball?
Is pressing connected and purposeful?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players press immediately and collectively after losing possession
Technical Detail | First step speed, pressing angle, distance between players
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Play it — press it.” / “React now.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Standing after pass → cue: move as soon as you play
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” / “Good connected press.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, communication, willingness to defend
Reflection Question | “What did you do first after we lost it?”
Session Design Note | Time constraints exaggerate reaction speed for clarity
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

Session Outcomes

Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players face each other. One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and short direction changes; the partner mirrors without the ball. On a signal, the leader breaks away with the ball and plays a firm forward pass to a target cone or teammate before resetting. Roles switch immediately. Flow stays sharp and continuous, leading into Activation.
Coaching Focus
Sharp body movement and disguise
Quick shift from regain-type action to forward play
Prepare players for first-pass intent
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When the defending team regains, they score one point by completing a forward pass within 3 seconds. If the first pass goes backward, no point is awarded, but play continues. Immediate restarts maintain tempo.
Main Rule: First look forward.
Coaching Focus
Head up immediately on regain
Forward support appears early
Speed of decision, not rushed execution
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce time window to 2 seconds
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with two wide counter channels
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goal. On regain, the team must play the first pass forward into a teammate running into a channel or central space. The counter continues live until a shot, loss, or reset. If the first pass is sideways or backward, defenders are released to recover immediately.
Key Condition: First pass defines the counter.
Coaching Focus
Body shape to play forward under pressure
Timing of the first forward run
Support underneath in case the counter slows
Progression
Add a recovering defender starting goal-side
Reduce space in channels
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if scored from a counter-attack where the first pass after regain goes forward. If teams delay or reset first, goals count as normal. Fast restarts keep realism and rhythm high.
Coaching Focus
Recognise counter-attack cues
Speed of support ahead of the ball
Balance between speed and control
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let counter decisions emerge naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally look forward first on regains?
Is the counter-attack structured rather than rushed?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players play the first pass forward to start counter-attacks
Technical Detail | Body orientation, pass weight, first touch direction
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “First look forward.” / “Break the line.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Playing safe backward → cue: scan before you win it
Praise Language | “Great first pass.” / “Good counter decision.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, forward runs, bravery to play early
Reflection Question | “What helped you see the forward pass?”
Session Design Note | Time constraints exaggerate counter moments for clarity
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation
