content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Transition Moments | Week 2 | Transition Control: When to Go, When to Secure
Session Outcomes
Players recognise transition moments and choose correctly whether to attack quickly or secure possession to keep control.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players drive with the ball across the lane (1v0), turn, and return. On the coach’s call, the partner steps in to apply brief live pressure (1v1). The ball carrier must decide to push past, protect, or release the ball, then roles switch immediately. Flow is continuous and sharp, leading into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Sharp decision-making under changing pressure
Protect first, then play if needed
Prepare players for “go or secure” choices
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When the defending team regains, they must either:
Play forward within 3 seconds (go), or
Secure the ball with two safe passes (secure)
Correct decision = 1 point (coach judgement). Immediate restarts maintain rhythm.
Main Rule: Read the picture before you decide.
Coaching Focus
Quick scan after regain
Recognise numbers ahead vs pressure around
Support options appear quickly
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce time window for decision
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with two counter channels and a central safe zone
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. On regain, teams choose:
Go: play forward into a channel and attack within 5 seconds
Secure: play into the central safe zone and keep possession
If the wrong option is chosen (e.g. go with no support), defenders are released immediately. Play stays live.
Key Condition: Decision first, speed second.
Coaching Focus
Body shape to see both options
Communication to signal intent
Tempo control after the first action
Progression
Add a recovering defender starting closer to goal
Reduce size of safe zone
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team makes a clear correct decision (go or secure) in the transition phase and scores in the same possession. Wrong decisions remove the bonus but play continues. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
Independent decision-making in transitions
Balance between speed and control
Staying connected regardless of choice
Progression
Remove scoring incentive
Let game rhythm decide decisions
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players choose to go or secure without prompts?
Is tempo managed intelligently?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players choose correctly between attacking quickly or securing possession
Technical Detail | Scanning, body orientation, pass selection
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “What’s the picture?” / “Go or secure.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing counters → cue: secure first if outnumbered
Praise Language | “Good decision.” / “Smart control.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, communication, composure
Reflection Question | “Why did you choose to go or secure there?”
Session Design Note | Constraints exaggerate decision clarity in transitions
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

U13 | Transition Moments | Week 3 | Manage Transition: Balance Attack & Defence
Session Outcomes
Players balance attacking intent with defensive security during transition moments, recognising when to commit numbers forward and when to protect space.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: All players, one ball each
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using varied touches and direction changes. On a signal, players accelerate for 3–4 touches, then decelerate and protect the ball before moving again. Rhythm alternates between speed and control, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Smooth rhythm changes
Awareness of speed vs control
Prepare players for balance decisions
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. When the defending team regains, they choose to:
Attack quickly with two players, or
Secure possession while two players hold behind the ball
Correct balance (attack + cover) = 1 point (coach judgement). Immediate restarts maintain tempo.
Main Rule: Go with some, protect with others.
Coaching Focus
Recognition of numbers around the ball
Communication of roles
Balance between forward intent and cover
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space to increase transition pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a marked defensive cover zone
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. On regain, teams may attack, but at least two players must stay connected behind the ball in the cover zone. If possession is lost again and no cover is present, the opposition attacks freely. Play remains live.
Key Condition: Attack with balance, not all-in.
Coaching Focus
Identifying who goes and who holds
Body orientation to see both ball and space
Staying connected as play moves forward
Progression
Reduce the size of the cover zone
Allow delayed recovery runs for realism
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team scores while clearly maintaining defensive balance (cover players positioned behind the ball). If teams over-commit and get countered, goals count normally. Fast restarts keep realism.
Coaching Focus
Decision-making in open transitions
Balance between attacking numbers and protection
Compactness behind the ball
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game punish or reward decisions naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players manage transitions without prompts?
Is balance visible between attack and defence?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players balance attacking intent with defensive cover in transitions
Technical Detail | Body orientation, recovery positioning, role clarity
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Who goes? Who holds?” / “Attack with balance.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Everyone goes forward → cue: protect first
Praise Language | “Great balance.” / “Good decision to hold.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, communication, discipline
Reflection Question | “What made you decide to go or hold?”
Session Design Note | Constraints highlight role balance over pure speed
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

U13 | Transition Moments | Week 3 | Tempo Shift Challenge
Session Outcomes
Players recognise when to shift tempo after transitions — speeding up to exploit space or slowing down to regain control.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
One player carries the ball while the partner applies light, angled pressure. The ball carrier turns away, accelerates for 3–4 touches, then slows to protect and reset. Roles switch immediately. Rhythm alternates between speed and control, flowing straight into Activation.
Coaching Focus
Feel pressure before changing tempo
Accelerate, then decelerate with balance
Prepare players for tempo decisions in transition
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. On regain, teams must choose one action:
Fast: play forward within 3 seconds
Control: complete two secure passes before attacking
Correct tempo choice = 1 point (coach judgement). Immediate restarts keep rhythm high.
Main Rule: Change tempo on purpose.
Coaching Focus
Quick scan after regain
Recognise space vs pressure
Support adjusts to the chosen tempo
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space to increase decision speed
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a central control zone
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. On regain, teams decide to:
Accelerate: attack directly within 5 seconds, or
Stabilise: play through the control zone before attacking
If tempo choice doesn’t match the picture (e.g. forcing speed under pressure), defenders are released immediately. Play remains live.
Key Condition: Tempo shift creates advantage.
Coaching Focus
Body shape to see options early
Communication to signal tempo
Smooth transitions between fast and calm
Progression
Reduce size of control zone
Add a recovering defender starting closer to goal
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description

U13 | Compactness & Balance | Week 1 | Line Distances: Stay Connected
Session Outcomes
Players maintain compact line distances so the team stays connected when attacking, defending, and transitioning.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: All players, one ball each
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using varied touches and direction changes. On a signal, players slow, scan, and adjust their position relative to nearby teammates before accelerating again. Rhythm alternates between movement and adjustment, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Awareness of teammates while on the ball
Rhythm changes without losing control
Prepare players for spacing and connection decisions
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. The defending team scores a point by forcing play long or disconnected. The attacking team scores by keeping all players within two passing distances of each other while completing passes. Immediate restarts keep tempo high.
Main Rule: Stay close enough to help.
Coaching Focus
Adjusting position after every pass
Lines moving together, not in isolation
Communication to stay connected
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce area size
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with three horizontal zones
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Directional game. Teams attack toward goal. When attacking, all players must remain within two zones of the ball. When defending, the team must recover together into the middle zone before stepping again. Play stays live.
Key Condition: Distance between lines matters more than speed.
Coaching Focus
Back line stepping with midfield
Midfield supporting both sides of the ball
Front players checking distance, not drifting
Progression
Remove zone restrictions
Increase pressure by allowing faster transitions
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if scored while the team stays compact (coach judgement on line distances). If the team stretches too far and loses connection, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
Team moving as one unit
Compactness behind the ball
Forward players staying connected to midfield
Progression
Remove scoring incentive
Let the game reward or punish spacing naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally manage distances?
Is the team connected without reminders?

Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players maintain compact line distances in all moments
Technical Detail | Positioning relative to ball and teammates, recovery spacing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Stay connected.” / “Move together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Lines stretching → cue: shorten the team
Praise Language | “Great connection.” / “Good team movement.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, communication, discipline

U13 | Compactness & Balance | Week 1 | Protect the Core
Session Outcomes
Players protect central spaces collectively, forcing play wide while staying compact and balanced.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
One player carries the ball while the partner applies light pressure from different angles. The ball carrier shields, turns away, and plays into space before resetting. Roles switch continuously. Rhythm builds through repeated contact and adjustment, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Balance and strength through contact
Turning away from pressure
Prepare players to protect space and body position
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m with a marked central zone
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. The defending team scores a point by preventing passes through the central zone for 10 seconds. The attacking team scores by breaking lines centrally. Immediate restarts maintain tempo.
Main Rule: Protect the middle first.
Coaching Focus
Body shape to block central lanes
Compact distances around the ball
Patience to delay rather than dive in
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce size of the central zone
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a clearly marked central corridor
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game. Teams attack toward goals. Defending teams earn a point for forcing play wide and regaining outside the central corridor. If the centre is exposed, play continues with no reward. Attacks stay live.
Key Condition: Protect inside, show outside.
Coaching Focus
Nearest player delays centrally
Supporting players screen passing lanes
Team shifts together to protect the core
Progression
Allow central penetration after 5 passes (realism)
Reduce space to increase difficulty
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team regains possession after forcing the opponent wide and scores in the same phase. If teams get split centrally, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
Compactness around central areas
Collective shifting side-to-side
Discipline to protect space before pressing
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game reward or punish spacing naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players instinctively protect central space?
Is the team compact without reminders?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players collectively protect central areas while staying compact
Technical Detail | Body orientation, screening, distance between lines
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Protect the core.” / “Inside first.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Chasing wide too early → cue: hold the middle
Praise Language | “Great discipline.” / “Good central protection.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, awareness, teamwork
Reflection Question | “How did protecting the middle help us?”
Session Design Note | Central constraints exaggerate core protection for clarity
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Compactness & Balance | Week 2 | Defensive Cooperation: Cover & Support
Session Outcomes
Players defend together by covering teammates and offering immediate support to stay compact and protect space.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players face each other. One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and direction changes; the partner mirrors without the ball. On a signal, the leader changes direction sharply and the partner adjusts position to stay connected. Roles switch regularly. Flow stays continuous, leading straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Awareness of partner positioning
Adjusting distance while moving
Prepare players for cooperative defending actions
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. The defending team scores a point by winning the ball with two defenders connected (press + cover). Solo regains do not count. Immediate restarts maintain tempo.
Main Rule: No press without cover.
Coaching Focus
First defender applies pressure
Second defender provides cover angle
Distances small enough to help
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a central channel
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game. When defending, the nearest player presses while the second defender must be positioned to cover the central channel. Regains only count if both roles are visible. If the press is beaten and no cover is present, play continues with no reward.
Key Condition: Pressure and cover work together.
Coaching Focus
Angle of approach by the first defender
Cover defender screens dangerous space
Communication between the two
Progression
Allow third defender to join after first contact
Reduce channel width to increase difficulty
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team regains possession through clear defensive cooperation (press + cover) and scores in the same phase. If defending is disconnected, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
Collective defending rather than individual effort
Supporting the press from the right distance
Staying compact behind the ball
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game reward or punish cooperation naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players naturally provide cover and support?
Is defending connected without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players defend with clear cover and support relationships
Technical Detail | Pressing angle, cover position, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Press with help.” / “I’ve got your cover.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Solo pressing → cue: wait for support
Praise Language | “Great cover.” / “Good teamwork defending.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, discipline, awareness
Reflection Question | “How did covering your teammate help us defend?”
Session Design Note | Constraints reward cooperation over individual defending
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

U13 | Compactness & Balance | Week 2 | Compact Block Game: Move as One
Session Outcomes
Players defend as a compact block, shifting together to protect space and limit gaps between units.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players drive with the ball across the lane (1v0), turn, and return. On the coach’s call, the partner steps in to apply brief pressure (1v1). The ball carrier protects, turns away, or releases quickly. Roles switch immediately. Flow stays continuous and sharp, leading straight into Activation.
Coaching Focus
Protecting the ball under pressure
Quick adjustment of body position
Prepare players for contact and collective movement
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. The defending team must move as a connected block; a point is scored if all defenders shift together and force play backward or sideways. If one defender steps out alone, the point is cancelled. Immediate restarts keep tempo high.
Main Rule: Step together or don’t step.
Coaching Focus
Side-to-side shifting as a unit
Communication to move the block
Compact distances between players
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce area size to increase demands
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with three vertical channels
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game. Defending team earns a point for forcing the attack into wide channels while maintaining a compact block centrally. The block must shift together before pressing wide. If gaps appear centrally, play continues with no reward.
Key Condition: Block moves first, press comes second.
Coaching Focus
Protecting central space
Coordinated movement of back and midfield lines
Patience before stepping in
Progression
Allow attackers to switch play freely
Reduce channel width to increase difficulty
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team regains possession while clearly staying compact as a block and scores in the same phase. If defenders become stretched or disconnected, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
Collective shifting and spacing
Discipline to hold shape
Compactness behind the ball
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game reward or punish spacing naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players move as a unit naturally?
Is compactness maintained without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players defend as a compact block and move together
Technical Detail | Distances between players, shifting angles, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Move as one.” / “Stay compact.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | One player steps alone → cue: wait for the block
Praise Language | “Great block movement.” / “Good team discipline.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, patience, collective effort
Reflection Question | “What helped us stay compact together?”
Session Design Note | Constraints exaggerate block movement for clarity
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Compactness & Balance | Week 3 | Press Triggers from Mid-Block
Session Outcomes
Players recognise clear pressing triggers from a compact mid-block and step together at the right moment.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
Area: 20x20m
Players: All players, one ball each
Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using varied touches and direction changes. On a signal, players slow, scan, and adjust body position before accelerating again. Rhythm alternates between patience and speed, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
Rhythm changes without stopping
Awareness before movement
Prepare players to read cues before acting
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
Area: 30x25m
Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. The defending team stays in a compact mid-block and can only press when a trigger appears: bad touch, back to goal, or sideline receive. Regain on a trigger = 1 point. Pressing without a trigger cancels the point. Immediate restarts keep tempo high.
Main Rule: No trigger, no press.
Coaching Focus
Patience in the mid-block
Recognition of pressing cues
Collective step when the trigger appears
Progression
Remove one neutral
Reduce space to increase realism
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
Area: 40x30m with a marked central zone
Players: 6v6
Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game. Defending team sets in a mid-block. When a clear trigger appears (poor touch, square pass, back to goal), the nearest three step to press while the rest squeeze space behind. Successful regain or forced long pass scores a point.
Key Condition: Trigger decides the moment, not emotion.
Coaching Focus
First presser sets direction
Second and third players close passing lanes
Team squeezes up behind the press
Progression
Reduce time allowed to press
Allow free pressing after initial trigger window
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
Area: 50x40m
Players: 7v7
Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team regains possession from a mid-block press triggered correctly and scores in the same phase. If players press without a trigger and get played through, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
Discipline to hold shape
Clear communication before stepping
Compactness behind the press
Progression
Remove double-goal incentive
Let the game reward or punish timing naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
Area: Half pitch
Players: 7v7 or 8v8
Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
Do players wait for triggers naturally?
Is pressing collective rather than reactive?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players press from a mid-block using clear triggers
Technical Detail | Pressing angle, distance between lines, timing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Hold… now go.” / “Trigger first.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Early pressing → cue: wait for the mistake
Praise Language | “Great timing.” / “Good discipline.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, communication, collective movement
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to press?”
Session Design Note | Constraints exaggerate trigger recognition over constant pressing
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Compactness & Balance | Week 3 | Compactness in Game Scenarios
Session Outcomes
Players stay compact and connected across realistic game scenarios, adjusting distances as the picture changes.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player carries the ball while the partner applies light pressure from different angles. The ball carrier shields, turns away, and plays into space before resetting. Roles switch continuously. Rhythm stays calm and controlled, preparing players for contact and spatial awareness before the game-based work.
Coaching Focus
- Balance and body strength
- Awareness of pressure and space
- Prepare players for compact positioning under stress
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. The defending team scores a point by forcing play into one area and staying compact while shifting together. If defenders stretch or press individually, the point is cancelled. Immediate restarts maintain tempo.
Main Rule: Stay compact while the picture changes.
Coaching Focus
- Collective shifting as play moves
- Adjusting distances side-to-side and front-to-back
- Communication to stay connected
Progression
- Remove one neutral
- Reduce area size to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game with scenario constraints introduced by the coach every 2–3 minutes (e.g. defending a lead, chasing a goal, or protecting central space). Teams must adapt their compactness based on the scenario while play stays live.
Key Condition: Compactness adjusts to the game situation.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when to squeeze or drop
- Maintaining connection between lines
- Clear communication based on the scenario
Progression
- Shorten scenario time to increase challenge
- Remove scenario prompts for realism
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m
- Players: 7v7
- Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with changing game states introduced by the coach (e.g. last 2 minutes, one-goal lead, one-goal deficit). Teams must manage compactness appropriately. Double goals awarded if the team scores while clearly managing compactness to match the scenario.
Coaching Focus
- Game awareness and discipline
- Compactness linked to match context
- Emotional control under pressure
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Let game realism drive decisions
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: Half pitch
- Players: 7v7 or 8v8
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with no constraints. Coach observes application only.
Coaching Focus
- Do players manage compactness naturally in game scenarios?
- Are decisions made without prompts?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players adjust compactness to match real game scenarios
Technical Detail | Distance management, body orientation, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Stay connected.” / “Read the game.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Over-pressing late → cue: protect space first
Praise Language | “Great game management.” / “Good discipline.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, communication, discipline
Reflection Question | “How did the game situation change our spacing?”
Session Design Note | Scenario constraints test true understanding of compactness
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

U13 | Managing Possession | Week 1 | Keep the Ball to Control
Session Outcomes
Players keep possession with patience and connection to control the game and dictate tempo.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using inside–outside touches, rolls, and changes of direction. On a signal, players slow the ball, scan, and adjust position before re-accelerating. Rhythm alternates between calm control and movement, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
- High ball contact with calm rhythm
- Awareness before moving
- Prepare players to control tempo with the ball
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. The team in possession scores a point for completing five connected passes while keeping compact distances. If play becomes rushed or disconnected, the count resets. Immediate restarts maintain flow.
Main Rule: Keep it to control it.
Coaching Focus
- Support angles under and around the ball
- Calm decision-making

- Area: 40x30m
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals

Directional possession game. Teams attack toward goals, but goals only count if the team has shown a controlled possession phase (minimum 5 passes) before penetrating. If possession is rushed, teams must recycle and rebuild.
Key Condition: Possession creates control, not speed.

- Patience to circulate rather than force
- Collective movement to stay connected
Progression

- Players: 7v7

- Let the game reward patience naturally

- Area: Half pitch

Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Managing Possession | Week 1 | Circulate with Purpose
Session Outcomes
Players circulate the ball with intent to move opponents, create better angles, and access the next action.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: B – Turning Under Contact
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
- Equipment: Cones
Description
One player carries while the partner applies light, angled pressure. The ball carrier shields, turns away, and releases to space before resetting. Roles switch continuously. Rhythm stays calm and purposeful, flowing straight into Activation.
Coaching Focus
- Balance and protection under pressure
- Turning to improve the next pass
- Prepare players to keep the ball moving with intent
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting. Teams score a point by circulating through three different players and then playing into a new space (switch, split pass, or bounce to far side). Repeating the same pass pattern does not count. Immediate restarts maintain flow.
Main Rule: Move them, then move the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning before receiving
- Support angles to keep circulation alive
- Purposeful passes, not just safe ones
Progression
- Remove one neutral
- Reduce area size to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m with two wide lanes
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goals. To score, the attack must show a clear circulation phase (minimum 4 passes) that shifts the defence before the final action. If the ball stays on one side, teams must recycle and continue.
Key Condition: Circulation creates the opening.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to play through or around pressure
- Timing of movement to receive between lines
- Recognising when circulation has done its job
Progression

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline
U13 | Managing Possession | Week 2 | Rhythm Game: Speed Up, Slow Down
Session Outcomes
Players recognise when to speed up play to exploit space and when to slow down to regain control.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players face each other. One player leads with the ball using feints, stops, and changes of pace; the partner mirrors movement without the ball. On a signal, the leader accelerates away for 3–4 touches, then slows to protect and reset. Roles switch continuously. Rhythm alternates clearly between fast and calm.
Coaching Focus
- Clear contrast between speed and control
- Smooth deceleration after acceleration
- Prepare players for rhythm changes with the ball
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. Teams score:
- 1 point for completing 4 calm passes in control
- 1 point for a quick penetration within 3 seconds after recognising space
Points only count if the correct rhythm is chosen for the situation (coach judgement). Immediate restarts maintain flow.
Main Rule: Change rhythm on purpose.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning before deciding tempo
- Support adjusting to speed or control
- Calm behaviour after fast actions
Progression
- Remove one neutral
- Reduce space to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m with a central control zone
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game. On regain or build-up, teams choose to:
- Speed up: attack directly within 5 seconds, or
- Slow down: circulate through the control zone before penetrating
If the rhythm does not match the picture (e.g. forcing speed under pressure), defenders are released immediately. Play stays live.
Key Condition: Rhythm creates advantage.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to see pressure and space
- Communication to signal tempo choice
- Smooth transitions between fast and calm
Progression
- Reduce size of the control zone
- Add a recovering defender starting closer to goal
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m
- Players: 7v7
- Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if a team clearly changes rhythm (slow → fast or fast → slow) in the build-up before scoring. If play stays at one tempo, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising moments to accelerate
- Using possession to calm the game
- Staying connected through tempo shifts
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive
- Let the game reward or punish rhythm naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: Half pitch
- Players: 7v7 or 8v8
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Do players manage rhythm without prompts?
- Is tempo control visible in possession?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players control the game by changing tempo intelligently
Technical Detail | First touch direction, body orientation, pass selection
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Change the rhythm.” / “Calm or go.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Playing at one speed → cue: what does the picture need?
Praise Language | “Great tempo change.” / “Good control.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, composure, communication
Reflection Question | “Why did you speed up or slow down there?”

U13 | Managing Possession | Week 2 | Maintain Pressure
Session Outcomes
Players keep possession to sustain pressure on the opponent, preventing counter-attacks and creating repeated attacking moments.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes
- Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players drive with the ball across the lane (1v0), turn, and return. On the coach’s call, the partner steps in to apply brief live pressure (1v1). The ball carrier protects, turns away, or releases and immediately supports the next action. Roles switch continuously, keeping rhythm high before Activation.
Coaching Focus
- Protecting the ball under pressure
- Immediate support after releasing
- Prepare players for repeat actions to sustain pressure
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting the team on the ball. Teams score a point by keeping possession for 8 seconds inside the attacking half after entering it. If possession is lost immediately after entry, no point is awarded. Quick restarts maintain flow.
Main Rule: Don’t rush — keep them pinned in.
Coaching Focus
- Support underneath and around the ball
- Recycling play instead of forcing shots
- Staying compact to win second balls
Progression
- Remove one neutral
- Reduce space to increase pressure
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m divided into build and attack zones
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional practice. Teams attack toward goal. Once the ball enters the attacking zone, the objective is to maintain possession for 6–10 seconds before finishing. If the ball is lost, the defending team can counter immediately. Attacks stay live on rebounds.
Key Condition: Pressure is built by staying on the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Positioning to keep the ball in advanced areas
- Calm decisions after penetration
- Quick reactions to regain and sustain pressure
Progression
- Reduce required possession time
- Allow defenders to start higher
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m
- Players: 7v7
- Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with an incentive: double goals if the team scores after maintaining possession in the attacking half for 10 seconds or more. Direct attacks count as normal goals. Fast restarts maintain realism and intensity.
Coaching Focus
- Keeping the opponent under pressure
- Avoiding rushed shots or forced passes
- Compact positioning to prevent counters
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive
- Let sustained pressure create chances naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: Half pitch
- Players: 7v7 or 8v8
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with normal rules. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Do players naturally keep pressure with the ball?
- Is possession used to control territory?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players use possession to sustain attacking pressure
Technical Detail | Support angle, first touch security, pass choice
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Stay on it.” / “Keep them under pressure.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing shots → cue: one more connection
Praise Language | “Great patience.” / “Good pressure with the ball.”
Behaviours to Reward | Composure, connection, willingness to recycle
Reflection Question | “How did keeping the ball help us attack again?”
Session Design Note | Time-based constraints highlight pressure through possession
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

U13 | Managing Possession | Week 3 | Game Management Scenarios: Control Tempo
Session Outcomes
Players manage the game by controlling tempo based on the scenario — slowing the game to protect control or increasing speed to exploit moments.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: A – Controlled Touch Rhythm
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: All players, one ball each
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players move freely with the ball using varied touches and direction changes. On a signal, players slow the ball, scan, and adjust position relative to others, then re-accelerate into space. Rhythm alternates between calm control and purposeful movement, flowing straight into the Activation.
Coaching Focus
- Calm touches under control
- Scanning before changing speed
- Prepare players to manage tempo intentionally
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with neutrals supporting. The coach calls a scenario every 2–3 minutes:
- Protect a lead: score by completing 6 calm passes
- Chasing a goal: score by penetrating within 4 seconds
Correct tempo choice for the scenario = 1 point (coach judgement). Immediate restarts maintain flow.
Main Rule: Let the situation choose the tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of game context
- Support adjusting to slow or fast play
- Calm decisions under scenario pressure
Progression
- Remove one neutral
- Shorten scenario time windows
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m with a central control zone
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game. Coach introduces scenarios during live play (e.g. last minute, one-goal lead, equal score). Teams must manage tempo accordingly:
- Control: circulate through the control zone
- Attack: play forward quickly within 5 seconds
If tempo choice does not match the scenario, play continues with no reward.
Key Condition: Game context drives tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to see pressure and options
- Communication to signal intent
- Staying compact while controlling rhythm
Progression
- Reduce size of the control zone
- Remove verbal scenario cues for realism
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m
- Players: 7v7
- Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with changing scorelines and time scenarios announced by the coach. Double goals awarded if a team scores after clearly managing tempo to match the scenario. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
- Emotional control under pressure
- Using possession to manage the game
- Adjusting tempo as the picture changes
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Let match flow dictate decisions
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: Half pitch
- Players: 7v7 or 8v8
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game with no constraints. Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Do players manage tempo without prompts?
- Is possession used to control moments in the game?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players manage tempo based on game scenarios
Technical Detail | First touch security, body orientation, pass choice
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Read the moment.” / “Control the game.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Playing fast when leading → cue: slow it down
Praise Language | “Great game management.” / “Good control.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, composure, communication
Reflection Question | “How did the situation affect our tempo choice?”
Session Design Note | Scenario constraints test true possession control
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

Session Outcomes
Players make smart end-game decisions with the ball — choosing when to keep it, when to attack, and when to protect the result.

Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
- Equipment: Cones

One player carries the ball while the partner applies light pressure. The ball carrier shields, turns out, and plays a secure pass before moving to support again. Roles switch continuously. Rhythm stays calm and controlled, flowing straight into the Activation.

- Prepare players for control moments late in games
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals (7v4 in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Possession game with scenario calls from the coach:
- Winning: score by keeping possession for 8 seconds
- Drawing: score by circulating then penetrating
- Losing: score by attacking within 4 seconds
Correct decision for the scenario = 1 point (coach judgement). Immediate restarts maintain flow.
Main Rule: Match the decision to the moment.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of game state
- Calm behaviour when protecting
- Bravery when chasing the game
Progression
- Remove one neutral

Setup
- Area: 40x30m with two target goals
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game with live end-game scenarios introduced every 3–4 minutes (e.g. one-goal lead, one-goal deficit, final minute). Teams must choose to:
- Secure: keep possession and run time
- Attack: play forward quickly to score
Goals only count if the decision matches the scenario.
Key Condition: Decision quality matters more than speed.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning before acting
- Communication between teammates
- Emotional control under pressure
Progression
- Reduce coach prompts
- Let players manage scenarios independently
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m
- Players: 7v7
- Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game with changing scorelines and time scenarios announced by the coach. Double goals awarded if a team scores or protects possession using the correct end-game decision. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
- Game awareness
- Managing risk vs control
- Staying compact while protecting the outcome
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Let match pressure drive decisions
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: Half pitch
- Players: 7v7 or 8v8
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game. Final scenario only (coach sets the score and time once). No further input.
Coaching Focus
- Do players manage the game independently?
- Are decisions calm and purposeful?

Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players make correct possession decisions in end-game moments
Technical Detail | Ball protection, pass security, body orientation
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Know the moment.” / “Protect or attack.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing play when winning → cue: keep it, make them chase
Praise Language | “Great game awareness.” / “Smart decision.”
Behaviours to Reward | Composure, leadership, responsibility
Reflection Question | “What told you to keep it or go for it?”
Session Design Note | Scenario constraints test real game understanding
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

- Area: 25x20m, split into two lanes

Players drive with the ball across the lane (1v0), turn, and return. On the coach’s call, the partner steps in to apply brief live pressure (1v1). The ball carrier protects, escapes, or releases forward, then supports immediately. Roles switch continuously, building sharp reactions before the Activation.

Possession game. The defending team must stay compact (within two passing distances). On regain, they score a point by playing forward within 3 seconds. If the first action is sideways or backward, no point. Immediate restarts keep tempo high.

Progression

- Area: 50x40m
- Players: 7v7
- Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs

Tournament-style match. Double goals awarded if a team regains possession from a compact shape and scores within 8 seconds. If teams over-stretch before the regain, goals count as normal. Fast restarts maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
- Discipline to stay compact
- Speed and clarity of counter-attacks
- Balance between aggression and control
Progression
- Remove double-goal incentive
- Let the game punish poor compactness naturally
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: Half pitch
- Players: Match numbers available
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game. Tournament final. Normal rules. No coach input.
Coaching Focus
- Do players stay compact without reminders?
- Are counters purposeful and connected?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players counter effectively from a compact defensive shape
Technical Detail | Distances between players, first pass forward, support runs

Common Mistakes & Fixes | Stretching before regain → cue: compact first
Praise Language | “Great compact win.” / “Good counter decision.”
Behaviours to Reward | Discipline, urgency, connection
Reflection Question | “How did staying compact help the counter?”

Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation

Description
Possession game with rotating focus calls every 2–3 minutes:
- Compact: score by keeping team distances tight
- Brave: score by playing forward under pressure
- Intelligent: score by switching or securing appropriately
Correct action linked to the call = 1 point (coach judgement). Immediate restarts maintain intensity.
Main Rule: Same identity, different moments.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of game demand
- Staying connected regardless of task
- Confidence to act under pressure
Progression
- Remove one neutral
- Reduce space to increase stress
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game with minimal constraints. Coach introduces pressure moments (e.g. down a goal, pressed high, protecting a lead). Teams must respond using Belco identity, not new rules. Play remains live and game-real.
Key Condition: Identity guides decisions, not instructions.
Coaching Focus
- Compactness behind the ball
- Bravery to play when pressed
- Intelligent decisions based on the picture
Progression
- Remove coach prompts
- Let players manage pressure independently
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m
- Players: 7v7
- Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Tournament-style match. Double goals awarded if a team scores while clearly demonstrating Belco identity under pressure (coach judgement). Fast restarts maintain realism and emotional intensity.
Coaching Focus
- Identity under fatigue and stress
- Collective behaviour over individual moments
- Emotional control in competitive play
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Let the game decide outcomes
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: Half pitch
- Players: Match numbers available
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game. Final competitive match. Normal rules. No coach input.
Coaching Focus
- Do players stay compact, brave, and intelligent without prompts?
- Is identity visible when pressure rises?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players apply Belco identity consistently under pressure
Technical Detail | Decision-making, positioning, execution under stress
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Be us.” / “Trust the identity.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Panic under pressure → cue: reset, connect, play
Praise Language | “That’s Belco.” / “Great decision under pressure.”
Behaviours to Reward | Composure, bravery, teamwork
Reflection Question | “How did we show our identity when it got hard?”
Session Design Note | Reduced constraints test true identity application
Language Note | Belco terms support recognition, not explanation
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

heading: U13 Cycle 9, week 3, session 1

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 9 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1Belco-blue underline

Players compete in tournament conditions while consistently applying Belco identity: compact, brave, intelligent.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: C – Mirror Feints
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players face each other. One leads with feints, stops, and changes of pace; the partner mirrors movement. On a signal, the leader breaks away, combines with a short wall pass, and finishes into a gate before resetting. Roles switch immediately. Flow is continuous and competitive, preparing players for game intensity.

- Sharp body movement and disguise
- Quick transition from control to action

Short tournament rounds (2–3 minutes). Teams score by finishing after any Belco behaviour: connected build-up, brave forward play, or intelligent regain. Coaches rotate teams quickly to keep intensity high.
Main Rule: Compete, but play our way.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate engagement and focus
- Compactness even in small numbers
- Confidence to play forward under pressure

Belco Cup group games. Normal rules. Coaches observe only. Points are awarded for results and identity moments (coach judgement): compact defending, brave play under pressure, intelligent decisions.
Key Condition: Winning matters, but identity matters more.

- Remove scoring incentives

Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)

Final match. No constraints. No coaching. Players manage the game completely.

heading: U13 Cycle 9, week 3, session 2

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 9 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2Belco-blue underline
U13 | Tournament / Game Application | Week 3 | Team Reflection Review
Session Outcomes
Players reflect on Belco identity in game situations, recognising what helped them stay compact, brave, and intelligent — and what to improve next.
Ball Mastery Block (Optional – 5–10 mins)
Rotation Code: D – 1v0 → 1v1 Activation
Setup
- Area: 20x20m
- Players: Pairs, one ball per pair
- Equipment: Cones
Description
Players drive with the ball (1v0), turn, and play a short pass to their partner. On the return pass, the receiver protects the ball briefly before releasing again. On coach signal, the partner applies light pressure (1v1) for one action only. Roles switch continuously. Rhythm is calm and controlled, setting the tone for reflection-based work.
Coaching Focus
- Calm ball control
- Awareness after actions
- Prepare players mentally for review and discussion
Activation / Intro (10–12 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m
- Players: 4v4 + 3 neutrals
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs
Description
Free possession game. Every 2–3 minutes, the coach pauses play briefly (20–30 seconds) to ask a single reflection cue (see Coaching Focus). Play restarts immediately with no correction or instruction.
Main Rule: Play first, reflect briefly, then play again.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising moments of compactness
- Awareness of brave vs safe decisions
- Communication and connection
Progression
- Remove one neutral
- Reduce pause time to quick verbal check-ins only
Technical – Solving the Situation (15–20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m
- Players: 6v6
- Equipment: Balls, cones, bibs, goals
Description
Directional game with self-managed reflection breaks. Every 4–5 minutes, teams huddle for 30 seconds to answer one question provided by the coach (see Coaching Information). Play resumes immediately after discussion.
Key Condition: Players explain football decisions, not results.
Coaching Focus
- Why decisions were made
- How team shape helped or hurt
- Connection between identity and outcomes
Progression
- Shorten reflection time
- Allow one player per team to lead discussion
Situational Game (25–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m
- Players: 7v7
- Equipment: Two goals, balls, bibs
Description
Normal game. No constraints. Coach observes only. At natural breaks (ball out, injury), the coach may ask one neutral question to a team before restart. No answers required aloud — players restart play immediately.
Coaching Focus
- Independent behaviour
- Visible identity without prompts
- Emotional control and leadership
Progression
- No progression — realism and honesty are the goal
Transfer / End Game (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: Half pitch
- Players: Match numbers available
- Equipment: Goals, balls
Description
Free game. Final play of the cycle. No interruptions.
Coaching Focus
- Are lessons applied naturally?
- Is Belco identity visible without reminders?
Coaching Information (U13)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Players recognise and articulate how they applied Belco identity
Technical Detail | Decision-making, spacing, support behaviour
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “What did we do well?” / “What helps us play our way?”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Blaming outcomes → cue: focus on decisions
Praise Language | “Good awareness.” / “Strong honesty.”
Behaviours to Reward | Reflection, leadership, responsibility
