content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Progressing Through Midfield | Week 2 | Build Through Overload: Find the Free Midfielder
Session Outcomes
Progress through midfield by creating local overloads and recognising the free midfielder to play forward under pressure.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central midfield zone
- Players: 5v4 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball.
- Attacking team always has a +1 overload.
- Defenders press realistically to lock one side.
- A point is scored by finding the free midfielder and playing forward into a mini goal.
- Key rule: The free player must receive facing forward or on the half-turn.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning to identify the free player
- Moving the ball to move defenders
- Body shape to play forward on receipt
Progression
- Reduce space
- Limit possession players to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v3 + 1 neutral (neutral plays as midfielder for possession team)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team builds with a midfield overload.
- Defenders press to close central lanes.
- Objective is to:
- Circulate to draw pressure
- Identify the free midfielder
- Play forward through them
- On regain, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Speed of circulation to create overload
- Timing of the pass into the free midfielder
- Support around the receiver after the first forward pass
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Allow defenders to press aggressively from the start
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, central emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Create a midfield overload
- Find the free midfielder
- Progress play forward under control
- Coach allows play to flow to encourage recognition.
Coaching Focus
- Midfield spacing to create overloads
- Recognising when one midfielder is free
- Collective movement after the overload is used
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes whether players naturally look to create and use midfield overloads.
Coaching Focus
- Players scanning before receiving
- Team patience to move the opponent first
- Confidence to play inside under pressure
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently finds and uses the free midfielder to progress
Technical / Tactical Detail | Scanning, overload positioning, pass timing, support after the pass
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Create the overload.” • “Who’s free?” • “Play through us.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing play → “Move it first.” • Free player static → “Adjust your position.”
Praise Language | “Great recognition.” • “You found the free player.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, movement, bravery to play inside
Reflection Question | “How did we create the free midfielder?”
Session Design Note | Overloads give clarity before moving to full balance
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: overload, free player, connect

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Progressing Through Midfield | Week 2 | Create Superiority
Session Outcomes
Create numerical or positional superiority in midfield to progress forward with control under pressure.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central midfield zone
- Players: 5v4 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Possession team maintains a +1 superiority.
- Defenders press to lock central or ball-side space.
- A point is scored by using superiority to play forward into a mini goal.
- Key rule: The superiority must be created through movement or circulation, not fixed positioning.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising where superiority exists
- Movement to create a free player
- Calm decision-making under pressure
Progression
- Reduce space
- Limit possession players to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral plays with the team in possession)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team uses rotations and circulation to create superiority.
- Neutral can appear between lines or wide to stretch defenders.
- Objective is to:
- Draw pressure
- Create a free player
- Play forward through that advantage
- On regain, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Speed of circulation to shift defenders
- Timing of movement to become free
- Support around the ball once superiority is used
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Allow defenders to press aggressively
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, central emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Create midfield superiority (numerical or positional)
- Use it to break a line and progress forward
- Coach allows play to flow to encourage recognition.
Coaching Focus
- Midfield spacing and movement
- Recognising overloads and isolations
- Collective movement after breaking the line
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes whether players naturally look to create and use superiority in midfield.
Coaching Focus
- Players scanning before receiving
- Team patience to move defenders first
- Confidence to play forward through central areas
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently creates and uses superiority to progress through midfield
Technical / Tactical Detail | Movement to create advantage, scanning, pass timing, support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Create the advantage.” • “Who’s free?” • “Use the extra player.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Static superiority → “Move to make it real.” • Rushed pass → “Hold and shift them.”
Praise Language | “Great advantage.” • “You used the extra player well.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, movement, bravery to play inside
Reflection Question | “Where did we create the advantage, and how did we use it?”
Session Design Note | Superiority teaches recognition before equal-number realism
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: superiority, free player, connect

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Progressing Through Midfield | Week 3 | Tempo Game: Calm to Accelerate
Session Outcomes
Control midfield tempo by circulating calmly to draw pressure, then accelerating play at the right moment to progress forward.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central midfield zone
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball under light pressure.
- Defenders shift compactly to block forward lanes.
- Key rule: Team must complete 3 calm passes before they are allowed to accelerate and play forward to score.
- If the forward action is forced early, possession resets.
Coaching Focus
- Calm first touch and pass quality
- Scanning to recognise pressure cues
- Collective patience before accelerating
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Reduce required calm passes to 2
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral plays as central midfielder)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team circulates to move the block.
- Neutral helps control rhythm and reset play.
- Objective is to:
- Circulate calmly to draw pressure
- Recognise the moment to accelerate with a forward pass or carry
- On regain, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when the opponent is set vs stretched
- Speed of the first action after acceleration
- Support arriving immediately after tempo change
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Limit attackers to 2 touches to increase decision speed
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, central emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Circulate in midfield to control tempo
- Then accelerate play to break a line and progress forward
- Coach allows play to flow to encourage natural tempo decisions.
Coaching Focus
- Midfield unit controlling rhythm
- Clear triggers to speed up (free player, defender stepping, open body)
- Collective movement to support acceleration
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes whether players naturally slow the game before accelerating.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising calm vs fast moments
- Team staying connected during circulation
- Confidence to reset if acceleration isn’t on
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team controls midfield tempo and accelerates play at the right moment
Technical / Tactical Detail | Pass quality, scanning, tempo recognition, support timing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Calm first.” • “Move them.” • “Now go.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Rushing forward → “Control it first.” • Over-circulating → “Can we go now?”
Praise Language | “Great tempo change.” • “That acceleration hurt them.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, awareness, bravery, connection
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to speed up?”
Session Design Note | Tempo control builds decision quality before full match realism
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: tempo, control, accelerate
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

heading: U14 Cycle 2, week 3, session 2
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 2, week 3, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Progressing Through Midfield | Week 3 | Through Ball Finishing
Session Outcomes
Break midfield lines with well-timed through balls and finish attacks with coordinated forward runs.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central zone with a marked channel behind defenders
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals beyond the channel
Description
- Possession team circulates to move defenders.
- To score, a pass must be played through the channel to a runner who finishes.
- Key rule: The through ball can only be played when the runner starts on the blind side of the defender.
- Quick resets to repeat timing moments.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning for runner and space
- Weight and timing of the through ball
- Runner’s movement relative to defender
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Reduce channel width
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral acts as linking midfielder)
- Targets: End zone or mini goal behind defenders
Description
- Attacking team circulates in midfield.
- Neutral helps connect and draw pressure.
- Objective is to:
- Fix defenders
- Release a runner with a through ball
- Finish quickly
- Defenders counter on regain.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the runner (not too early)
- Pass weight to lead, not chase
- Supporting runs after the through ball
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Add recovering defender after the pass
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, central emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Break midfield with a through ball
- Create a clear finishing action
- Coach allows play to flow to encourage natural recognition.
Coaching Focus
- Midfielders recognising when defenders step
- Forwards timing runs across or beyond the line
- Collective movement to support the finish
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes whether players naturally look to play and run beyond midfield lines.
Coaching Focus
- Players resisting forced passes
- Runners adjusting movement to defender position
- Calm finishing after the through ball
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently breaks lines with through balls and finishes attacks
Technical / Tactical Detail | Pass weight, timing of runs, body shape, finishing composure
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Fix then slip.” • “Hold… now run.” • “Pass into space.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Early run → “Delay the movement.” • Over-hit pass → “Lead, don’t chase.”
Praise Language | “Perfect timing.” • “That pass split them.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, bravery to run beyond, connection
Reflection Question | “What told you the through ball was on?”
Session Design Note | Clear channels support learning timing before full realism
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: through ball, timing, break lines

heading: U14 Cycle 3, week 1, session 1
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 3, week 1, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Finishing from Positional Play | Week 1 | Positional Attack: Occupy 5 Lanes
Session Outcomes
Create controlled attacks by occupying all five attacking lanes to stretch the defence and arrive in finishing positions together.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m attacking half
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
- Pitch marked into 5 vertical lanes
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball while maintaining occupation of all five lanes.
- Players must rotate after passing but lanes must stay filled.
- A point is scored by playing forward into a mini goal after switching lanes.
- Key rule: No two players can occupy the same lane at the same time.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of lane occupation
- Movement to fill empty lanes quickly
- Patience to keep structure before attacking
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Limit possession players to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, attacking direction
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral plays with attacking team)
- Targets: Full goal or end zone
- Pitch divided into 5 lanes
Description
- Attacking team builds positional attacks.
- Objective is to:
- Occupy all five lanes (2 wide, 2 half-spaces, 1 central)
- Move the ball to stretch the defence
- Finish once the block is unbalanced
- Neutral helps maintain central or half-space connection.
- On regain, defenders counter into a mini goal.
Coaching Focus
- Width from wide players
- Depth and timing of runs into half-spaces
- Central player linking play, not blocking lanes
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Allow defenders to counter immediately on regain
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m, attacking half emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
- Five-lane reference maintained
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- All five lanes are occupied during the attack
- The attack leads to a shot or goal
- Coach allows flow to test recognition under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Team shape in sustained attack
- Players holding width and half-space discipline
- Collective timing of runs into the box
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes whether players naturally occupy lanes in attack.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising empty lanes
- Width and depth maintained during possession
- Attacks finishing with multiple players involved
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently occupies five lanes to create balanced attacks
Technical / Tactical Detail | Width, half-space occupation, timing of runs, spacing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Fill the lanes.” • “Hold your width.” • “Arrive together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Crowding central lane → “Find a free lane.” • Early runs → “Hold until we’re set.”
Praise Language | “Great structure.” • “That shape created the chance.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, discipline, patience, collective movement
Reflection Question | “Which lane created the goal or chance?”
Session Design Note | Clear lane structure builds positional understanding before free attacking play
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: lanes, width, structure
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

heading: U14 Cycle 3, week 1, session 2
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 3, week 1, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Finishing from Positional Play | Week 1 | Create the Final Pass
Session Outcomes
Create and recognise the moment for the final pass through collective positioning, patience, and timing in the attacking phase.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m attacking zone
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
- Pitch divided into three vertical lanes (left–central–right)
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball.
- Defenders stay compact and protect the central lane.
- A point is scored by playing a final pass that breaks a line and leads to a finish in a mini goal.
- Key rule: The final pass can only be played after the defence has shifted at least once.
Coaching Focus
- Patience before forcing the final action
- Scanning to recognise defender movement
- Body shape to disguise and execute the final pass
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Reduce space to increase pressure
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, attacking direction
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral plays with attacking team)
- Targets: Full goal or end zone
Description
- Attacking team builds positional attacks.
- Objective is to:
- Fix defenders with circulation
- Create a passing lane
- Deliver a final pass into a finishing action
- Neutral supports between lines to help create the final pass.
- On regain, defenders counter into a mini goal.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the final pass (not early, not late)
- Movement of attackers to create passing lanes
- Supporting runs beyond the ball after the pass
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Limit attackers to 2 touches in the build-up phase
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m, attacking half emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- The final pass breaks a defensive line
- And leads directly to a shot or goal
- Coach allows play to flow to encourage decision-making.
Coaching Focus
- Team structure before the final pass
- Recognition of defender stepping or over-committing
- Collective timing of runs into the box
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally delay, draw defenders, and then play the final pass.
Coaching Focus
- Players resisting rushed final balls
- Attackers moving to receive between or beyond lines
- Confidence to reset if the final pass isn’t on
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently creates and recognises the moment for the final pass
Technical / Tactical Detail | Pass disguise, timing, movement ahead of the ball, finishing support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Move them first.” • “Wait for the moment.” • “Slip the pass.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forced final ball → “Reset and move them.” • Static attackers → “Arrive, don’t stand.”
Praise Language | “Great patience.” • “That pass unlocked them.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, bravery, collective movement
Reflection Question | “What told you the final pass was on?”
Session Design Note | Final pass quality improves when players learn to wait and recognise cues
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: final pass, patience, timing
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

heading: U14 Cycle 3, week 2, session 1
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 3, week 2, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Finishing from Positional Play | Week 2 | Arrive Late: Second Line Finishing
Session Outcomes
Create goals by timing second-line runs into the box, arriving late to finish after the defence has been fixed.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m attacking zone
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
- Mark a finishing zone at the top of the box area
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball to fix defenders.
- First line players stay high and occupy defenders.
- A point is scored when a second-line runner arrives late into the finishing zone to score.
- Key rule: Second-line players cannot enter the finishing zone until the final pass is played.
Coaching Focus
- Patience to hold position
- Scanning for the right moment to arrive
- Body shape to finish first or second touch
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Reduce finishing zone size
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, attacking direction
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral plays as linking midfielder)
- Targets: Full goal
- Mark a second-line arrival zone outside the box
Description
- Attacking team builds positional attacks.
- First line (forwards/wide players) fix the back line.
- Objective is to:
- Circulate to draw defenders
- Create space at the top of the box
- Finish with a late-arriving midfielder
- Neutral helps connect and reset play.
- On regain, defenders counter into a mini goal.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the late run (not early)
- Awareness of rebound and cut-back areas
- Support runs after the initial shot
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Add recovering defender tracking the second line
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m, attacking half emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- A goal is scored by a second-line runner
- Or a shot is created from a late arrival
- Coach allows flow to test timing under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Attackers fixing defenders in the box
- Midfielders holding patience before arriving
- Collective awareness of finishing zones
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether midfielders naturally arrive late rather than standing in the box.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising space outside the box
- Timing of arrivals after the ball moves wide or backward
- Confidence to shoot from second-line positions
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently creates goals through second-line, late-arrival finishes
Technical / Tactical Detail | Timing of runs, finishing technique, awareness of space, rebounds
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Hold… arrive.” • “Fix them first.” • “Attack the space late.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Early run → “Wait outside.” • Standing in box → “Arrive, don’t camp.”
Praise Language | “Perfect timing.” • “Great late run.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, awareness, bravery to finish, collective movement
Reflection Question | “What told you it was the moment to arrive?”
Session Design Note | Second-line finishing develops patience and collective attacking timing
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: arrive late, second line, timing

heading: U14 Cycle 3, week 2, session 2

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Finishing from Positional Play | Week 2 | Attack the Cut-Back
Session Outcomes
Create and finish chances by attacking cut-back zones with correct timing, spacing, and collective movement.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m attacking zone
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
- Mark a cut-back zone just inside the box (central corridor)
Description
- Possession team circulates to move defenders.
- Attacks must go wide before finishing.
- A point is scored only from a cut-back pass into the marked zone.
- Key rule: Finishers must arrive into the cut-back zone from outside — no standing inside.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when the ball is wide and head is up
- Timing of runs into the cut-back zone
- Body shape to finish first time or controlled touch
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Reduce size of the cut-back zone
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, attacking direction
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral plays with attackers)
- Targets: Full goal
- Mark three finishing lanes: near post, cut-back zone, far post
Description
- Attacking team builds positional attacks.
- Ball must travel wide before a finish.
- Objective is to:
- Fix defenders in the box
- Deliver a cut-back
- Attack different finishing lanes collectively
- Neutral helps circulate and reset play.
- On regain, defenders counter into a mini goal.
Coaching Focus
- Wide player’s decision: cross vs cut-back
- Timing and angle of runs into finishing zones
- Awareness of teammates’ movements
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Add recovering defender tracking the cut-back zone
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m, attacking half emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- The attack goes wide
- A cut-back is played
- The finish comes from a coordinated run
- Coach allows flow to test realism.
Coaching Focus
- Team recognising when to attack wide
- Multiple runners attacking different zones
- Patience to find the cut-back instead of forced crosses
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally look for cut-backs and coordinated finishes.
Coaching Focus
- Players arriving into space, not waiting
- Awareness of high-percentage finishing areas
- Confidence to finish quickly under pressure
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently creates and finishes chances from cut-back situations
Technical / Tactical Detail | Timing of runs, finishing zones, decision-making wide, body shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Get it wide.” • “Arrive together.” • “Attack the cut-back.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Standing in box → “Arrive, don’t wait.” • Early cross → “Head up first.”
Praise Language | “Great timing.” • “That run made the goal.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, collective movement, bravery to finish
Reflection Question | “Which run created the best chance from the cut-back?”
Session Design Note | Cut-back finishing develops coordinated attacking behaviour
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: cut-back, arrive, finishing zones

(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Finishing from Positional Play | Week 3 | Combination to Finish: Wall Pass in Zone 14
Session Outcomes
Create scoring chances by combining in Zone 14 using wall passes to break lines and finish with control.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m attacking zone
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
- Mark Zone 14 centrally outside the box
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball around Zone 14.
- To score, the ball must enter Zone 14 and be played as a wall pass (one-two) before finishing.
- Defenders protect central space and apply controlled pressure.
- Key rule: The player receiving the return pass must face forward.
Coaching Focus
- Positioning between the lines in Zone 14
- Quality and timing of the wall pass
- Body shape to play forward after the combination
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Limit attackers to 2 touches in Zone 14
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, attacking direction
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral operates in Zone 14)
- Targets: Full goal
- Zone 14 clearly marked
Description
- Attacking team builds positional attacks.
- Neutral helps create central combinations.
- Objective is to:
- Find a player between the lines in Zone 14
- Play a wall pass to break the line
- Finish quickly
- Defenders counter into a mini goal on regain.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of movement into Zone 14
- Weight and angle of the set pass
- Supporting runs beyond the wall pass
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Add a recovering defender tracking the return run
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m, attacking half emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
- Zone 14 reference maintained
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- A wall pass is played in Zone 14
- The attack leads to a shot or goal
- Coach allows flow to test recognition under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Players finding pockets between lines
- Speed of combination in tight central areas
- Collective movement to support the finish
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally look for central combinations to finish.
Coaching Focus
- Players choosing combination over forced shots
- Timing and quality of wall passes
- Confidence to play through central pressure
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently creates chances through wall passes in Zone 14
Technical / Tactical Detail | Between-line positioning, pass weight, body shape, finishing support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Find the pocket.” • “Set and go.” • “Play forward.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Back to goal → “Open your body.” • Late support → “Arrive with the pass.”
Praise Language | “Great combination.” • “That wall pass broke them.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, bravery in central areas, quick combinations
Reflection Question | “What made the wall pass possible in that moment?”
Session Design Note | Zone 14 focus builds central creativity before free finishing
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: Zone 14, wall pass, combine

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Finishing from Positional Play | Week 3 | 3rd-Man Timing
Session Outcomes
Create clear finishing chances by timing third-man runs and passes to break lines and arrive in scoring positions.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m attacking zone
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
- Central pocket marked just outside the box
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball.
- Attacks must include a third-man action before finishing.
- Example: Player A plays to B, B sets, third-man C runs to finish.
- Key rule: The third-man cannot start their run until the first pass is played.
Coaching Focus
- Patience before movement
- Scanning to recognise third-man opportunity
- Weight and angle of the set pass
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Reduce space to increase pressure
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, attacking direction
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral plays as linking attacker)
- Targets: Full goal
Description
- Attacking team builds positional attacks.
- Neutral supports combinations but cannot finish.
- Objective is to:
- Fix defenders with the ball
- Play a set pass
- Release a third-man runner to finish
- Defenders counter into a mini goal on regain.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the third-man run (late, explosive)
- Body shape of the set player
- Supporting runs after the shot (rebounds)
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Add a recovering defender tracking the third-man run
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m, attacking half emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- A third-man combination is used
- The action leads to a shot or goal
- Coach allows play to flow to test recognition under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Players resisting early runs
- Recognition of defender stepping out
- Collective movement to support the third-man action
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally use third-man timing to finish rather than forcing play.
Coaching Focus
- Players coordinating movement and passes
- Third-man runs arriving unseen
- Calm finishing after the combination
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently creates chances using well-timed third-man runs
Technical / Tactical Detail | Timing of movement, set-pass quality, finishing composure
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Fix them.” • “Set and go.” • “Arrive late.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Early run → “Wait for the pass.” • Poor set → “Set to play forward.”
Praise Language | “Perfect timing.” • “That third-man made the goal.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, awareness, explosive movement, collective connection
Reflection Question | “What told you to make the third-man run?”
Session Design Note | Third-man timing links midfield play to finishing moments
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: third-man, timing, combine

heading: U14 Cycle 3, week 4, session 1

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 4 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Finishing from Positional Play | Week 4 | Collective Attack Game: From Build-Up to Finish
Session Outcomes
Attack collectively from build-up through to finish, maintaining structure, patience, and coordinated movement in the final third.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, split into build-up zone → attacking zone
- Players: 6v4 (possession team overloaded)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals in attacking zone
Description
- Play starts from the build-up zone.
- Possession team must progress into the attacking zone before finishing.
- Defenders apply controlled pressure and look to counter if they win the ball.
- Key rule: A goal only counts if at least three passes connect build-up to attack.
Coaching Focus
- Team spacing during build-up
- Patience before entering the attacking zone
- Early communication to organise the attack
Progression
- Add a fifth defender
- Reduce space between zones
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x35m, directional
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (neutral plays with attacking team)
- Targets: Full goal
- Zones: Build-up third, final third
Description
- Attacking team builds from the back and progresses forward.
- Neutral helps connect midfield to attack.
- Objective is to:
- Maintain structure through build-up
- Enter the final third under control
- Finish with coordinated movement
- On regain, defenders counter into two mini goals.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of forward runs
- Support underneath the ball
- Decision to penetrate or recycle
Progression
- Remove the neutral (5v5)
- Allow defenders to press freely from the start
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m, near full width
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow; all restarts from the GK.
- Team scores double if they:
- Build from the back
- Progress through midfield
- Finish with at least three players involved in the final action
- Coach allows flow to test full attacking behaviour.
Coaching Focus
- Collective spacing across all phases
- Balance between patience and penetration
- Team arriving together in finishing areas
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether build-up habits connect naturally to final-third actions and finishes.
Coaching Focus
- Players maintaining structure under pressure
- Collective decision-making in attack
- Calm reactions after missed chances
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team attacks collectively from build-up to finish with connection and structure
Technical / Tactical Detail | Spacing, timing of runs, support angles, finishing support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Build together.” • “Be patient.” • “Arrive as a team.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Rushed attack → “Reset and build.” • Isolated finisher → “Support the play.”
Praise Language | “That was a team attack.” • “Great connection from start to finish.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, awareness, collective movement, bravery
Reflection Question | “How did our build-up help create the finish?”
Session Design Note | Full-journey games consolidate positional attacking learning
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: build-up, connect, arrive together
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 4 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Finishing from Positional Play | Week 4 | Rhythm to Purpose
Session Outcomes
Control attacking rhythm to recognise the right moment to play with purpose and finish collectively.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m attacking zone
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball under light pressure.
- Defenders stay compact and delay.
- Key rule: Team must complete 3 controlled passes before they are allowed to play with purpose (penetrating pass, dribble, or shot).
- If the attacking action is forced early, possession restarts.
- Quick resets to repeat rhythm decisions.
Coaching Focus
- Calm ball circulation
- Scanning to read defender movement
- Recognising the moment to speed up
Progression
- Add a fourth defender
- Reduce required passes to 2
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, attacking direction
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral plays with attacking team)
- Targets: Full goal
Description
- Attacking team builds positional attacks.
- Neutral helps maintain rhythm and reset play.
- Objective is to:
- Circulate to move defenders
- Recognise imbalance or free player
- Accelerate with purpose to finish
- Defenders counter into a mini goal on regain.
Coaching Focus
- Tempo control before penetration
- First action after acceleration
- Supporting runs once purpose is shown
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Limit attackers to 2 touches in the build-up phase
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m, attacking half emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Control rhythm in possession
- Then accelerate into a purposeful attacking action
- Create a shot or goal
- Coach allows flow to test decision-making under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Team patience in sustained attacks
- Clear triggers to accelerate (free player, defender stepping, open body)
- Collective movement once the attack speeds up
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally control rhythm before attacking with purpose.
Coaching Focus
- Players avoiding rushed finishes
- Team recognising when to reset vs go
- Collective confidence to accelerate together
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently controls rhythm and attacks with purpose
Technical / Tactical Detail | Tempo control, scanning, timing of acceleration, support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Control it.” • “Move them.” • “Now go.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forced attack → “Reset the rhythm.” • Over-circulating → “Can we go now?”
Praise Language | “Great patience.” • “Perfect moment to go.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, bravery, collective action
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to attack with purpose?”
Session Design Note | Rhythm-to-purpose links all positional attacking behaviours
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: rhythm, purpose, accelerate

heading: U14 Cycle 4, week 1, session 1
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 4, week 1, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Pressing Organisation | Week 1 | Press Triggers: Closed Ball & Return Pass
Session Outcomes
Act collectively to press when the ball is closed or played backwards, staying compact and connected.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, two adjacent rectangles
- Players: 12–14 players split into 3 teams of 4
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals (one at each end)
Description
- One team keeps possession in one rectangle (4v2).
- Defending pair presses; if they win it, they play a quick return pass into the opposite rectangle to swap roles.
- Possession team scores a point for 5 passes.
- Direction is set by mini goals but no finishing yet.
- Key rule: Defenders can only press aggressively once the ball carrier is facing their own goal (closed ball).
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of body shape and opponent cues
- First defender closes with intent, second defender supports
- Quick reaction when the ball is closed
Progression
- Limit possession team to 2 touches
- Add a scoring gate for defenders after regain
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, divided into two halves
- Players: 4v3 in each half (rotate defenders)
- Targets: End zones at each end
Description
- Attacking team builds from one end zone to the other.
- Defending team presses only on two triggers:
- Closed body shape
- Return pass into pressure
- If defenders win it, they attack immediately into the opposite end zone.
- If attackers break through, reset from start zone.
Coaching Focus
- Timing of the press – go together, not alone
- Angle of approach to block forward options
- Playing forward immediately after regain
Progression
- Reduce to 4v4
- Allow free pressing but reward goals only if regain comes from a trigger
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, directional
- Players: 6v6 + GK (or 6v6 with end zones)
- Goals: Full-size or mini goals depending on numbers
Description
- Normal directional game.
- Team out of possession scores double if they win the ball from:
- A closed ball
- A return/back pass
- Play restarts quickly from GK or coach.
- Minimal stoppages to keep rhythm.
Coaching Focus
- Compactness around the ball when pressing
- Clear triggers: who goes, who covers
- Collective reaction on regain or if press is broken
Progression
- Remove double-score incentive
- Play fully normal rules to increase realism
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, max 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal conditions.
- Coach observes only.
- Let players recognise pressing moments naturally.
Coaching Focus
- Spontaneous use of press triggers
- Team staying compact when stepping in
- Collective reaction, not individual chasing
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team recognises closed ball and return pass as moments to press together
Technical / Tactical Detail | Body shape, distance between units, timing of first and second defender
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Wait for the trigger.” • “Go together.” • “Win it and play forward.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Late press → “Recognise it earlier.” • Solo press → “Find your support.”
Praise Language | “Great timing as a unit.” • “That’s collective pressure.”
Behaviours to Reward | Quick reaction, compact support, communication, bravery to step in
Reflection Question | “What told you it was the right moment to press?”
Session Design Note | Early overloads create clarity before moving to balanced, realistic games
Language Note | Use Belco terms: compact, trigger, play forward
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

heading: U14 Cycle 4, week 1, session 2
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 4, week 1, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Pressing Organisation | Week 1 | Angles of Approach
Session Outcomes
Press with correct approach angles to lock play, deny forward options, and win the ball collectively.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 6v3 (rotate defenders every 60–90 seconds)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 4 small gates on the outside
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball inside the area.
- Defenders can only press once the ball travels into a defender’s zone.
- Objective for defenders: win the ball and play through any outside gate.
- Key rule: First defender must curve their run to block the nearest forward gate.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape when approaching the ball
- Curved run to show play one way
- Second defender positions to lock the exit
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure
- Limit possession players to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 4v3 + GK or target player
- Targets: Two mini goals or end zone behind attacking team
Description
- Attacking team builds toward goal/end zone.
- Defending team presses on a forward touch or poor control.
- First defender sets the angle to block central play.
- Supporting defenders squeeze to close passing lanes.
- On regain, defenders attack immediately.
Coaching Focus
- Angle of approach to force play wide or back
- Distance and timing between first and second defender
- Pressing to trap, not to tackle alone
Progression
- Move to 4v4
- Remove trigger restriction — defenders must self-recognise moments
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x35m, directional
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow.
- Team scores double if the regain comes from:
- Forcing play wide with an angled press
- Winning the ball after locking the sideline
- Quick restarts to maintain intensity.
Coaching Focus
- Compactness around the ball
- Clear pressing roles: steer, cover, lock
- Collective movement as the ball travels
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Play fully normal rules for realism
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, max 7v7
- Full width, realistic length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes pressing behaviour without stopping play.
Coaching Focus
- Natural use of curved runs
- Team pressing as a unit
- Players adjusting angles based on teammate positions
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team uses correct angles to control where the opponent can play
Technical / Tactical Detail | Curved approach, body shape, spacing between pressers
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Show them where to go.” • “Angle first, speed second.” • “Lock it together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Straight run → “Curve your approach.” • Over-pressing → “Delay and lock.”
Praise Language | “Great angle.” • “You forced the mistake together.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, compact support, patience, bravery to step in
Reflection Question | “How did your angle help your teammate press?”
Session Design Note | Pressure increases as players learn to self-manage angles
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: compact, lock, force wide
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

heading: U14 Cycle 4, week 2, session 1
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 4, week 2, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Pressing Organisation | Week 2 | Compact Front Line: Nearest Three Go
Session Outcomes
Press as a compact front three, with the nearest three players acting together to lock play and force regains.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 28x22m central grid
- Players: 6v3 (rotate defenders every 60–90 seconds)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball freely.
- Defending team scores by winning the ball and finishing into either mini goal.
- Key rule: Only the nearest three defenders are allowed to press; the furthest defender must recover and screen.
- Direction is set by mini goals but no build-up pattern is coached.
Coaching Focus
- Distance between the pressing three
- Body shape to block central options
- First reaction when the ball moves
Progression
- Reduce area size
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral always with attacking team)
- Targets: End zone behind defending team
Description
- Attacking team builds forward toward the end zone.
- Defending team presses only with the nearest three players.
- The nearest player steps, the other two support and cover.
- On regain, defenders attack immediately into the end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Compact triangle shape of the pressing three
- Clear roles: step, cover, screen
- Staying connected while moving forward
Progression
- Remove the neutral player (4v4)
- Allow counter-press immediately after loss
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m, directional
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if the ball is won by:
- A coordinated press from the nearest three
- Forcing a turnover in the attacking half
- Coach allows play to continue to build rhythm.
Coaching Focus
- Front-line compactness
- Timing of the first step forward
- Collective squeeze behind the press
Progression
- Remove double-score incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Pressing Organisation | Week 2 | Cover and Balance
Session Outcomes
Support the press with correct cover and balance so the team stays compact and protected behind the ball.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m
- Players: 5v3 inside, 2 support players outside (rotate roles)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Inside team keeps possession.
- Three defenders press; outside players act as bounce options.
- Defenders score by winning the ball and finishing into either mini goal.
- Key rule: Only one defender presses the ball; the other two must provide cover and balance.
Coaching Focus
- Distance and angle of covering players
- Body shape to protect central space
- Reading when to step and when to hold
Progression
- Remove one outside support player
- Reduce space to increase pressure
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team builds toward the end zone.
- Defending team presses with one player stepping to the ball.
- Nearest teammate provides cover; third defender provides balance by protecting the far side.
- On regain, defenders attack immediately.
Coaching Focus
- Supporting angles behind the press
- Staying connected while shifting across
- Protecting the space behind the pressing player
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Allow immediate counter-press on loss
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 50x40m, directional
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they regain the ball while:
- One player presses
- One covers
- One balances
- Coach lets play run to build realism and rhythm.
Coaching Focus
- Compactness behind the press
- Team shifting as the ball moves
- Collective reaction if the press is bypassed
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe natural use of cover and balance behind pressing actions.
Coaching Focus
- Players holding discipline behind the ball
- Recovery runs after press is broken
- Team staying compact, not stretched
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently supports the press with cover and balance
Technical / Tactical Detail | Cover angles, spacing, body shape, shifting together
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “One press, one cover, one balance.” • “Protect the middle.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | All chase → “Hold your balance.” • Flat line → “Stagger your cover.”
Praise Language | “Great cover.” • “You protected the team there.”
Behaviours to Reward | Discipline, communication, awareness, collective movement
Reflection Question | “Where did you need to be when your teammate pressed?”
Session Design Note | Early structure supports understanding before full freedom
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: cover, balance, compact

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Pressing Organisation | Week 3 | Team Press: Forward + Midfield Unit Connection
Session Outcomes
Connect the forward and midfield units to press as one compact block, denying central access and forcing regains together.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x22m central grid
- Players: 6v4 inside (forwards + midfielders as defenders)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Possession team circulates inside the grid.
- Four defenders (2 forwards + 2 midfielders) work as a unit.
- Defenders score by winning the ball and finishing into either mini goal.
- Key rule: Front two can only press if midfield two are connected within 8–10m behind.
Coaching Focus
- Vertical distance between lines
- Midfield stepping in behind the press
- Body shape to protect central space
Progression
- Reduce grid length to increase compactness
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 5v4 (attacking team vs forward + midfield unit)
- Targets: End zone behind defending team
Description
- Attacking team builds toward the end zone.
- Defending unit presses only when:
- Forward press is matched by midfield squeeze
- Forwards set the direction; midfield locks space behind.
- On regain, defenders attack immediately into the end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Timing between forward press and midfield step
- Compact distances front-to-mid
- Blocking central passes before tackling
Progression
- Move to 5v5
- Allow free pressing decisions without restriction
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they regain the ball with:
- Forward press + midfield squeeze connected
- Coach allows flow to maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
- Unit connection (not individual pressing)
- Midfield reading forward cues
- Collective reaction if the first press is bypassed
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether forward and midfield units stay connected naturally.
Coaching Focus
- Natural line connection
- Compact team shape when pressing
- Recovery and re-compact if press is broken
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Forwards and midfield press together as one connected unit
Technical / Tactical Detail | Vertical compactness, timing of squeeze, body shape to block centre
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Press together.” • “Squeeze behind.” • “Protect the middle.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Front presses alone → “Midfield, step with them.” • Gap appears → “Close the line.”
Praise Language | “That’s unit pressure.” • “Great connection between lines.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, collective movement, discipline, bravery
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time for midfield to step?”
Session Design Note | Structure first, then realism as players self-organise
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: compact, connect, squeeze

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Pressing Organisation | Week 3 | Win It High
Session Outcomes
Win the ball in the attacking half through collective pressing and immediate forward intent.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m, positioned in the attacking half
- Players: 5v3 inside the grid, 2 target players outside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Possession team circulates inside the grid.
- Three defenders press to regain.
- On regain, defenders score by playing forward into either mini goal within 5 seconds.
- Key rule: Defenders can only score if the regain happens inside the grid (high regain).
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction on loss
- Speed and intent of first press
- Supporting angles around the ball
Progression
- Reduce grid size
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x30m, directional, positioned high up the pitch
- Players: 4v3 + GK (defenders pressing high)
- Targets: Full goal or end zone behind attacking team
Description
- Attacking team starts from GK and builds out.
- Defending team sets a high press.
- Press is triggered on:
- Poor first touch
- Backward or sideways pass
- On regain, defenders attack immediately to score.
- If attackers break the press, play continues briefly before reset.
Coaching Focus
- Aggressive first step forward
- Compact distances between pressing players
- Playing forward immediately after regain
Progression
- Move to 4v4
- Remove set triggers and allow players to self-recognise moments
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, attacking half emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal directional game.
- Team scores double if:
- The ball is won in the attacking half
- The shot comes within 8 seconds of the regain
- Quick restarts to maintain pressing rhythm.
Coaching Focus
- Front-to-back compactness
- Collective squeeze behind the press
- Mentality to attack immediately after winning it high
Progression
- Remove time constraint
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, realistic length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe natural high-press behaviours and decision making.
Coaching Focus
- Recognition of high regain moments
- Team confidence to step in high
- Reaction if the high press is broken
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently looks to regain and attack in the opponent’s half
Technical / Tactical Detail | First step speed, compact spacing, forward passing after regain
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it high.” • “Go together.” • “Attack straight away.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Hesitation → “Step in now.” • Spread press → “Squeeze closer.”
Praise Language | “Great high regain.” • “That’s brave pressing.”
Behaviours to Reward | Aggression, collective movement, quick reactions, bravery
Reflection Question | “What told you it was on to win it high?”
Session Design Note | Space reduced progressively to increase realism and pressure
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: win it high, compact, attack fast

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 4 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Pressing Organisation | Week 4 | Pressing Game: Regain in 5 Seconds
Session Outcomes
React collectively on loss to regain the ball within five seconds while staying compact and connected.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 5v5 inside (rotate pairs if numbers higher)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Normal possession inside the grid.
- When possession changes, the team that lost the ball has 5 seconds to win it back.
- If they succeed, they score by finishing into either mini goal.
- If not, play continues with normal possession.
- Key rule: All players must react forward together on loss.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction on loss
- Sprint distance toward the ball
- Compactness around the regain zone
Progression
- Reduce grid size
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral always with team in possession)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team builds toward the end zone.
- On loss of possession, the attacking team has 5 seconds to counter-press and regain.
- If they regain in time, they attack immediately.
- If not, defending team can counter freely.
Coaching Focus
- First step speed on loss
- Nearest three pressing together
- Cover and balance behind the press
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Reduce regain window to 4 seconds
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- They regain possession within 5 seconds of losing it
- The regain occurs in the attacking half
- Coach allows play to flow to keep intensity high.
Coaching Focus
- Collective reaction to loss
- Front-to-back compactness during counter-press
- Decision to press or recover if regain isn’t on
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes natural counter-press behaviour.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising regain moments without countdown prompts
- Team staying compact after loss
- Recovery shape if regain fails
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team reacts instantly to loss and regains possession quickly
Technical / Tactical Detail | Sprint reactions, compact distances, cover behind press
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Five seconds.” • “Go now.” • “Together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Delay on loss → “React instantly.” • Spread press → “Squeeze in.”
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” • “That’s aggressive together.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, communication, compact support, bravery
Reflection Question | “What helped us win it back quickly?”
Session Design Note | Time constraint sharpens collective reaction and intent
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: regain, compact, react

- The attack comes from a pressing win

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Defending the Middle Third | Week 1 | Compact Mid-Block: Protect Central Zone
Session Outcomes
Defend the middle third as a compact unit, protecting the central zone and forcing play wide or backwards.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central channel (marked middle third)
- Players: 5v4 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on wide edges
Description
- Possession team circulates inside the central channel.
- Defending team sets a mid-block and aims to protect the central zone.
- Defenders score by winning the ball and playing into either wide mini goal.
- Key rule: Defenders cannot press beyond the channel lines—shape first, pressure second.
Coaching Focus
- Compact distances horizontally and vertically
- Body shape to block central passing lanes
- Patience: delay rather than dive in
Progression
- Reduce channel width
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with attacking team)
- Targets: End zones behind defending team
Description
- Attacking team builds through the middle third toward the end zone.
- Defending team holds a compact mid-block.
- Press is triggered only when:
- Ball enters central lane under pressure
- Attacker receives facing their own goal
- On regain, defenders attack immediately into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Screen passes into the central zone
- Staggered positioning (cover and balance)
- Collective shifting as the ball moves
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow immediate counter-press after regain
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, middle-third emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Win the ball in the middle third
- Force the opponent wide before regaining
- Coach lets play run to maintain rhythm.
Coaching Focus
- Team compactness between lines
- Midfield screen protecting the centre
- Decision to step or hold as a unit
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether the team naturally protects the central zone in a mid-block.
Coaching Focus
- Natural compact mid-block behaviour
- Communication between lines
- Recovery and re-compact if the block is broken
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team stays compact and consistently protects the central zone in the middle third
Technical / Tactical Detail | Distances between lines, body shape to screen, collective shifting
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Protect the middle.” • “Stay compact.” • “Force them wide.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Stepping out alone → “Hold the line.” • Gaps inside → “Close the centre.”
Praise Language | “Great discipline.” • “You protected the team there.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, communication, compact support, awareness
Reflection Question | “What helped us stop the pass into the middle?”
Session Design Note | Structure first to teach compactness before adding full freedom
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: compact, screen, protect central

(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Defending the Middle Third | Week 2 | Control Depth: Track Runners
Session Outcomes
Control space behind the line by recognising and tracking runners while maintaining a compact mid-block.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central channel
- Players: 5v4 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned behind defenders
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball in the middle third.
- Attacking players can make timed forward runs beyond the defensive line.
- Defenders score by winning the ball or intercepting a forward pass.
- Key rule: Goals count double if a defender tracks a run and prevents penetration.
Coaching Focus
- Awareness of space behind the line
- Scanning for runners off the ball
- Timing of drop vs step
Progression
- Reduce channel length to increase pressure
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with attacking team)
- Targets: End zone behind defending team
Description
- Attacking team looks to play through or run beyond the defensive line.
- Defenders hold a compact shape and track runners when the ball carrier has time.
- If defenders win the ball, they counter into the opposite end zone.
- Key condition: Defenders cannot step out unless cover is in place behind.
Coaching Focus
- Body shape to see ball and runner
- Communication between defenders
- Protecting depth first, then pressing
Progression
- Remove the neutral (4v4)
- Allow free pressing once depth is controlled
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, middle-third emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Prevent a forward run through tracking
- Win the ball after delaying penetration
- Coach allows play to flow to encourage realism.
Coaching Focus
- Back line and midfield connection
- Collective drop when the ball has time
- Decision to track or pass runners on
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally control depth and track runners.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising danger early
- Team dropping together
- Recovery runs after penetration attempts
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team controls depth and consistently tracks forward runs
Technical / Tactical Detail | Body orientation, scanning, timing of drop, unit connection
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “See ball and runner.” • “Protect the space.” • “Drop together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Ball watching → “Check your shoulder.” • Late drop → “Read it earlier.”
Praise Language | “Great tracking.” • “You protected the line.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, communication, recovery effort, discipline
Reflection Question | “What told you a runner was coming?”
Session Design Note | Early structure supports reading cues before full freedom
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: depth, track, compact

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Defending the Middle Third | Week 2 | Move as a Unit
Session Outcomes
Defend the middle third by moving together as one unit, staying compact while shifting to protect central space.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central channel
- Players: 5v4 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned wide
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball inside the channel.
- Defending team sets a mid-block.
- Defenders score by winning the ball and playing into either wide mini goal.
- Key rule: Defenders must move together side-to-side; no individual pressing outside the unit.
Coaching Focus
- Side-to-side shifting as a group
- Distances between teammates
- Body shape to block central lanes
Progression
- Reduce channel width
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with attacking team)
- Targets: End zones behind defending team
Description
- Attacking team builds through the middle third.
- Defending team shifts as a compact block, pressing only when the unit is connected.
- If one defender steps, the others must squeeze and cover.
- On regain, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Collective movement on ball travel
- Timing of step vs shift
- Communication to stay connected
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow free pressing once unit movement is consistent
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, middle-third emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Regain the ball while staying compact as a unit
- Force play wide before winning possession
- Coach allows play to run to encourage self-organisation.
Coaching Focus
- Team shifting together
- Compact distances horizontally and vertically
- Collective reaction when the ball switches
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether the team naturally moves together when defending.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising when to shift as a unit
- Communication across the line
- Quick re-compact after movement
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently defends by shifting and pressing together as one unit
Technical / Tactical Detail | Horizontal compactness, timing of shifts, body shape, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Move together.” • “Stay connected.” • “Shift as one.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Stepping alone → “Wait for the unit.” • Gaps opening → “Squeeze across.”
Praise Language | “Great unit movement.” • “That’s compact defending.”
Behaviours to Reward | Communication, discipline, awareness, collective effort
Reflection Question | “What helped us stay connected as the ball moved?”
Session Design Note | Clear structure early supports collective movement before full freedom
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: compact, connected, unit
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Defending the Middle Third | Week 3 | Delay and Regain: Defend Forward When Possible
Session Outcomes
Delay the opponent in the middle third, then defend forward together to regain when the moment is right.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central channel
- Players: 5v4 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned behind the possession team
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball in the channel.
- Defending team sets a compact mid-block.
- Defenders score by winning the ball and playing forward into either mini goal.
- Key rule: Defenders must delay first; pressing is only allowed once the ball carrier is slowed or facing backwards.
Coaching Focus
- Slowing the ball carrier
- Body shape to stay goal-side and balanced
- Collective decision of when to step in
Progression
- Reduce channel length
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with attacking team)
- Targets: End zone behind defending team
Description
- Attacking team builds through the middle third.
- Defending team delays play by holding shape and screening passes.
- Press is triggered when:
- The attacker takes a heavy touch
- The ball is received facing backwards
- On regain, defenders attack immediately into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Patience before pressing
- Angle and timing of the first step forward
- Support and cover behind the press
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow players to self-recognise press moments
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, middle-third emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Delay the opponent
- Then regain the ball by defending forward in the middle third
- Coach allows play to run to encourage realism.
Coaching Focus
- Collective patience in the block
- Clear press triggers
- Compact support when stepping in
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally delay before pressing forward.
Coaching Focus
- Recognition of when to hold vs step
- Team pressing together after delay
- Quick re-compact if regain fails
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team delays effectively and regains by defending forward at the right moment
Technical / Tactical Detail | Body shape, timing of press, cover and balance, compactness
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Slow them.” • “Wait… now go.” • “Forward together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Diving in early → “Delay first.” • Late support → “Step together.”
Praise Language | “Great patience.” • “Perfect moment to step in.”
Behaviours to Reward | Discipline, awareness, communication, collective action
Reflection Question | “What told you it was the right moment to press forward?”
Session Design Note | Teaching delay first creates smarter, more effective regains
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: delay, regain, defend forward
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)
