content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Defending the Middle Third | Week 3 | Counter Shape
Session Outcomes
Recover quickly into a compact counter shape after losing the ball, protecting the centre and preventing forward penetration.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m central channel
- Players: 5v5 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned centrally behind each team
Description
- Free possession inside the channel.
- When possession is lost, the team that lost the ball must recover into a compact defensive shape before pressing.
- Defending team scores by winning the ball and finishing into the central mini goal.
- Key rule: No immediate pressing allowed for 3 seconds after loss — recovery first.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate recovery runs toward goal side
- Protecting central space first
- Communication to organise shape
Progression
- Reduce recovery time to 2 seconds
- Reduce channel width to increase pressure
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with attacking team)
- Targets: End zones behind defending team
Description
- Attacking team builds through the middle third.
- On loss, defending team must recover into counter shape before engaging.
- Once shape is set, defenders can step in to delay and regain.
- On regain, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Speed and angle of recovery runs
- Line distances once recovered
- Clear roles: recover, screen, then press
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow pressing immediately once shape is recognisable
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, middle-third emphasis
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Recover into counter shape within 5 seconds
- Then regain the ball in the middle third
- Coach allows play to flow to encourage realism.
Coaching Focus
- Collective sprint to recover
- Compact block before pressing
- Decision to delay or step once organised
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe how quickly and consistently teams recover into shape after losing possession.
Coaching Focus
- Natural recovery habits
- Team protecting the middle first
- Re-organisation after transitions
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently recovers into a compact counter shape after loss
Technical / Tactical Detail | Recovery runs, body orientation, spacing, screening central lanes
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Recover first.” • “Protect the middle.” • “Then step.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Chasing immediately → “Get goal-side.” • Central gaps → “Close the middle.”
Praise Language | “Great recovery.” • “That shape protected us.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, communication, discipline, collective effort
Reflection Question | “What did you do first when we lost the ball?”
Session Design Note | Delaying the press teaches players to value shape before aggression
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: counter shape, recover, compact
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Attack | Week 1 | Reaction After Regain: First Pass Forward
Session Outcomes
React immediately after regaining the ball by playing the first pass forward to exploit disorganisation.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 5v5 inside (rotate if numbers higher)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite ends
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On regain, the winning team has 5 seconds to score in either mini goal.
- Key rule: First action after regain must be forward (pass or carry).
- If the first action is sideways or backwards, possession resets to the other team.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction on regain
- Body shape to play forward first
- Supporting movement ahead of the ball
Progression
- Reduce grid size
- Reduce scoring window to 4 seconds
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x30m, directional
- Players: 4v3 (defending team regains and attacks)
- Targets: End zone or mini goal behind attacking team
Description
- Attacking team circulates the ball.
- Defending team presses to regain.
- On regain, defenders must find a forward pass into the end zone or goal.
- Supporting players must move ahead of the ball immediately.
- Reset quickly to repeat transition moments.
Coaching Focus
- Quality and direction of first pass
- Timing of forward support runs
- Decision: pass, carry, or finish
Progression
- Add recovering defender after 3 seconds
- Move to 4v4
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- The first pass after regain is forward
- The attack leads to a shot within 8 seconds
- Coach allows flow to maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Collective reaction after regain
- Forward options created quickly
- Balance between speed and control
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes whether players naturally look forward first after regain.
Coaching Focus
- Players scanning forward on regain
- Teammates offering forward options early
- Team maintaining compact support behind the attack
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently plays forward immediately after regaining possession
Technical / Tactical Detail | Body shape on regain, first-pass quality, support timing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it, play forward.” • “See forward first.” • “Support the pass.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Backwards pass → “Forward option first.” • No support → “Run ahead of the ball.”
Praise Language | “Great first pass.” • “That reaction hurt them.”
Behaviours to Reward | Speed of reaction, bravery to play forward, support runs
Reflection Question | “What did you see that made you play forward?”
Session Design Note | Time limits sharpen forward intent immediately after regain
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: regain, forward, support
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Attack | Week 1 | Support to Counter
Session Outcomes
Provide immediate, connected support after regain to counter quickly and attack with numbers and clarity.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 4v4 inside, 2 floaters on the outside (play with team in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On regain, the team that wins the ball has 6 seconds to attack and score.
- Outside floaters can be used only after the regain.
- Key rule: At least one supporting run must go beyond the ball before a shot.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction to support the ball
- Angles and distances of first support
- Forward movement ahead of the ball
Progression
- Remove one floater
- Reduce time window to 5 seconds
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x30m, directional
- Players: 4v3 (defenders regain and counter)
- Targets: End zone or mini goal behind attacking team
Description
- Attacking team circulates possession.
- Defending team presses to regain.
- On regain, defenders must:
- One player support underneath
- One player run beyond
- Attack continues until shot or ball goes dead.
- Quick reset to repeat counter moments.
Coaching Focus
- First support angle (in front or underneath)
- Timing of forward runs
- Decision-making: pass, carry, or finish
Progression
- Add a recovering defender after 3 seconds
- Move to 4v4
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- The counter-attack includes at least two supporting runs
- A shot occurs within 8 seconds of regain
- Coach allows play to run to maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Numbers forward on the counter
- Spacing to stretch the opponent
- Balance between speed and support
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes whether players naturally support counters with forward runs.
Coaching Focus
- Players reacting together after regain
- Supporting angles appearing early
- Team staying connected if counter slows
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently supports the counter with forward and underneath options
Technical / Tactical Detail | Support angles, timing of runs, spacing, decision speed
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Support it.” • “Run beyond.” • “Arrive together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Ball carrier isolated → “Get close to help.” • Flat support → “One under, one beyond.”
Praise Language | “Great support.” • “That counter had numbers.”
Behaviours to Reward | Reaction speed, forward running, connection, bravery
Reflection Question | “Where did we need support to make the counter work?”
Session Design Note | Repetition of short, sharp counters builds instinctive support habits
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: counter, support, arrive together
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 6, week 2, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Attack | Week 2 | Break with Speed: Exploit Space
Session Outcomes
Exploit space immediately after regain by breaking forward with speed and collective support.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m, split into two horizontal zones
- Players: 4v4 inside, 2 neutral floaters (play with team in possession)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals at opposite ends
Description
- Free possession in the central zone.
- On regain, the winning team must break into the far zone and attack within 6 seconds.
- Neutrals can only be used after the regain.
- Key rule: First action after regain must be forward into space (pass or carry).
Coaching Focus
- Immediate recognition of space
- Speed of first movement after regain
- Supporting runs ahead of the ball
Progression
- Remove one neutral
- Reduce time window to 5 seconds
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v3 (defending team regains and attacks)
- Targets: End zone or mini goal behind attacking team
Description
- Attacking team circulates possession.
- Defending team presses to regain.
- On regain, defenders must break forward quickly:
- Ball carrier drives into space or plays forward early
- At least one teammate runs beyond
- Attack continues until shot, goal, or ball goes dead.
- Quick reset to repeat.
Coaching Focus
- Carry vs pass decision at speed
- Timing of the run beyond the ball
- Distance between ball carrier and support
Progression
- Add a recovering defender after 3 seconds
- Move to 4v4
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- The counter-attack starts with a forward break into space
- A shot occurs within 8 seconds of regain
- Coach allows play to run to maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Collective sprint forward on regain
- Width and depth to stretch the opponent
- Decision to continue the break or secure possession
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally attack space with speed after regain.
Coaching Focus
- Players scanning for space on regain
- Supporting runs matching the speed of the break
- Team staying connected if the break slows
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently breaks forward at speed to exploit space after regain
Technical / Tactical Detail | First action speed, carry vs pass choice, timing of forward runs
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Break fast.” • “Attack the space.” • “Go with the ball.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Slow first action → “Explode forward.” • No runs → “Go beyond the ball.”
Praise Language | “Great speed.” • “That break hurt them.”
Behaviours to Reward | Acceleration, forward intent, support runs, bravery
Reflection Question | “What space did you see when we won it?”
Session Design Note | Time limits reinforce speed and forward intent in transition
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: break, space, speed
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Attack | Week 2 | Compact Recovery if Lost
Session Outcomes
React immediately after a lost counter-attack to recover compactly and protect the centre before pressing again.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 5v5 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite ends
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On regain, the winning team attacks a mini goal.
- If the attack breaks down or possession is lost, the attacking team must recover into a compact shape before re-pressing.
- Key rule: No pressing allowed for 3 seconds after losing the counter — recovery first.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate recovery reaction
- Sprinting goal-side
- Compact distances around the ball
Progression
- Reduce recovery window to 2 seconds
- Reduce grid size to increase pressure
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with attacking team)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team builds and looks to counter on regain.
- If the counter fails, that team must recover into a compact defensive shape before engaging.
- Defending team can attack freely if recovery is slow.
- On regain after recovery, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Recovery runs toward the middle
- Communication to organise shape
- Decision: recover first, then press
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow pressing immediately once shape is clearly compact
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Lose the ball while attacking
- Recover into compact shape
- Then regain possession within 8 seconds
- Coach lets play run to maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
- Collective sprint to recover
- Protecting the centre first
- Clear transition from recovery to press
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe how often teams recover compactly after failed attacks.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising danger immediately after loss
- Team compactness during recovery
- Communication to re-organise quickly
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently recovers compactly after losing possession in attack
Technical / Tactical Detail | Recovery runs, central protection, spacing, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Recover first.” • “Protect the middle.” • “Then go again.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Chasing immediately → “Get goal-side.” • Spread recovery → “Squeeze in.”
Praise Language | “Great recovery.” • “You protected us there.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, discipline, communication, collective effort
Reflection Question | “What did we do first when the counter broke down?”
Session Design Note | Teaching recovery habits protects the team during aggressive attacking transitions
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: recover, compact, re-press

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Attack | Week 3 | Counter-Attack Game: Win → Play → Support
Session Outcomes
Counter-attack with clarity by winning the ball, playing forward immediately, and supporting the attack with connected runs.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 4v4 inside, 2 outside targets on end lines
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On regain, the winning team must:
- Win the ball
- Play a forward pass within 3 seconds
- Support with at least one forward run
- Finish into either mini goal.
- Outside targets can only be used after regain.
- Key rule: No goal if there is no supporting run beyond the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction on regain
- First forward decision
- Early supporting movement ahead of the ball
Progression
- Remove outside targets
- Reduce time to play forward to 2 seconds
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v3 (defenders regain and counter)
- Targets: End zone or mini goal behind attacking team
Description
- Attacking team circulates possession.
- Defending team presses to regain.
- On regain, defenders must:
- Play forward quickly
- Create a 2v1 or 3v2 through support runs
- Attack continues until shot or ball goes dead.
- Quick resets to repeat counter moments.
Coaching Focus
- Speed of first pass or carry
- Angle and timing of support runs
- Decision-making at speed (pass vs carry)
Progression
- Add a recovering defender after 3 seconds
- Move to 4v4
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- The counter includes win → play → support
- A shot occurs within 8 seconds of regain
- Coach allows play to flow to maintain tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Collective sprint forward on regain
- Spacing to stretch the opponent
- Balance between speed and connection
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes how often regains lead to connected counter-attacks.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising counter moments
- Support arriving at the right time
- Team staying connected if the counter slows
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team counters effectively by winning it, playing forward, and supporting together
Technical / Tactical Detail | First-pass direction, support angles, timing of runs, decision speed
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it.” • “Play forward.” • “Support the ball.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Isolated ball carrier → “Get numbers forward.” • Late support → “Run early.”
Praise Language | “Great counter.” • “That was connected.”
Behaviours to Reward | Reaction speed, forward intent, support runs, bravery
Reflection Question | “Who supported the counter and why did it work?”
Session Design Note | Clear sequencing builds understanding before full realism
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: counter, forward, support

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Attack | Week 3 | Finishing the Break
Session Outcomes
Turn counter-attacks into goals by finishing quickly and collectively before the opponent recovers.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 4v4 inside, 2 target players positioned near goals
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On regain, the winning team has 6 seconds to attack and finish.
- Target players can only be used after the regain to help create finishing options.
- Key rule: A shot must be taken within the time window.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate recognition of finishing opportunity
- First touch and body shape toward goal
- Supporting runs into scoring areas
Progression
- Remove target players
- Reduce finishing window to 5 seconds
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v3 + GK
- Targets: Full-size goal
Description
- Attacking team circulates possession.
- Defending team presses to regain.
- On regain, defenders counter with a numerical advantage.
- Focus is on creating a shot quickly through:
- Early pass
- Direct carry
- One supporting run beyond
- Reset quickly to repeat multiple finishing moments.
Coaching Focus
- Decision speed near goal
- Choosing the right finishing option
- Timing of runs into the box
Progression
- Add a recovering defender after 3 seconds
- Move to 4v4
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- They finish within 8 seconds of regain
- The attack includes at least two players arriving in the box
- Coach allows play to flow to maintain realism and tempo.
Coaching Focus
- Collective sprint to finish
- Width and depth to open finishing lanes
- Decision to shoot vs square pass
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes how often counter-attacks end with a shot or goal.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising when to finish quickly
- Support arriving in scoring positions
- Calmness and clarity in the final action
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently converts counter-attacks into shots and goals
Technical / Tactical Detail | First touch toward goal, shot selection, timing of box entries
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Finish it.” • “Attack the goal.” • “Arrive to score.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Overplaying → “Shoot earlier.” • No runners → “Attack the box.”
Praise Language | “Great finish.” • “That break had purpose.”
Behaviours to Reward | Bravery to shoot, forward runs, composure, collective effort
Reflection Question | “What made it the right moment to finish?”
Session Design Note | Time pressure sharpens decision-making and finishing intent
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: break, finish, arrive

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Defence | Week 1 | Reaction After Loss: Nearest Three Press
Session Outcomes
React immediately after losing possession, with the nearest three players pressing together to delay, disrupt, or regain.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 5v5 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- When possession is lost, the team that lost the ball must react instantly.
- Key rule: Only the nearest three players are allowed to press for the first 5 seconds.
- If they win it back, they score in either mini goal.
- If not, play continues normally.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction on loss
- Identifying who the nearest three are
- Compact distances between the pressing players
Progression
- Reduce grid size
- Reduce reaction window to 4 seconds
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with team in possession)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team builds possession.
- On loss, the attacking team must counter-press with the nearest three.
- Roles:
- One presses the ball
- One covers inside
- One balances behind
- On regain, attackers immediately play forward into the end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Speed of first step after loss
- Clear roles within the nearest three
- Supporting angles rather than chasing
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow the fourth player to join after 3 seconds
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- They regain the ball within 6 seconds of losing it
- The regain comes from the nearest three pressing together
- Coach allows play to run to maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
- Collective sprint toward the ball
- Compact support behind the press
- Decision to press or recover if regain isn’t on
Progression
- Remove scoring incentives
- Fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether the nearest three naturally react together after loss.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising pressing responsibility instantly
- Team compactness during the counter-press
- Recovery shape if the press is broken
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Nearest three players react immediately and press together after loss
Technical / Tactical Detail | First-step speed, compact spacing, cover and balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Lost it—go.” • “Nearest three.” • “Together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Ball watching → “React now.” • Everyone pressing → “Three go, rest recover.”
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” • “That’s three together.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, communication, compact support, bravery
Reflection Question | “How did you know you were one of the nearest three?”
Session Design Note | Clear roles early help players recognise counter-press responsibility
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: nearest three, react, compact

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Defence | Week 1 | Delay Counter
Session Outcomes
Delay the opponent’s counter-attack immediately after loss, buying time for teammates to recover and regain compact shape.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 5v5 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite ends
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On loss of possession, the team that lost the ball must delay the counter.
- Defending team scores by forcing the counter-attacking team to play backwards or sideways within 5 seconds.
- Key rule: First defender must slow the ball carrier, not tackle.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate reaction on loss
- Body shape to stay goal-side
- Slowing the ball carrier
Progression
- Reduce grid size
- Add a 5-second countdown for the counter team to attack
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v3 (defending team delays the counter)
- Targets: End zone or mini goal behind defenders
Description
- Attacking team starts with the ball and looks to counter quickly.
- Defending team reacts to loss and focuses on delaying:
- First defender slows
- Second defender covers inside
- Third defender balances behind
- If defenders delay successfully for 5 seconds, play resets.
- If attackers break through, they finish.
Coaching Focus
- Angle of approach to block forward space
- Distance to control speed without diving in
- Communication between defenders
Progression
- Move to 4v4
- Allow tackling after the first 3 seconds of delay
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Lose the ball
- Successfully delay the counter
- Recover shape before the opponent attacks the goal
- Coach allows play to run to maintain realism.
Coaching Focus
- Collective reaction to counter threat
- Protecting the central channel
- Decision to delay or press once numbers recover
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally delay counters after losing the ball.
Coaching Focus
- Players slowing the ball rather than chasing
- Teammates recovering behind the delay
- Team re-forming compact shape
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently delays counters to allow recovery
Technical / Tactical Detail | Body shape, approach angle, spacing, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Slow them.” • “Stay goal-side.” • “Buy time.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Diving in → “Delay, don’t tackle.” • Straight run → “Show them wide.”
Praise Language | “Great delay.” • “You bought us time.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, awareness, recovery effort, communication
Reflection Question | “How did delaying help the team recover?”
Session Design Note | Teaching delay first prevents chaotic defending in transition
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: delay, recover, compact
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

heading: U14 Cycle 7, week 2, session 1

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Defence | Week 2 | Counter-Press Triggers: Closed or Return Ball
Session Outcomes
Recognise clear counter-press triggers (closed body shape or return pass) and react together to regain or disrupt immediately.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 5v5 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on opposite sides
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- When possession is lost, the team that lost the ball can counter-press only on two triggers:
- Ball receiver facing their own goal (closed)
- Return/backward pass into pressure
- If counter-pressing team regains, they score in either mini goal.
- If no trigger appears within 5 seconds, team must recover shape.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning for body shape and pass direction
- First defender’s timing on the trigger
- Supporting reactions from nearest teammates
Progression
- Reduce grid size
- Limit possession to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with team in possession)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team circulates possession.
- On loss, the defending team counter-presses only if a trigger appears.
- Roles on trigger:
- First defender presses the ball
- Second defender blocks forward pass
- Third defender provides cover
- On regain, immediate forward play into the end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Patience until the trigger appears
- Angle of approach to lock the ball
- Compact distances between the pressing players
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow the 4th defender to join after 3 seconds
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Counter-press from a closed ball or return pass
- Regain possession within 6 seconds
- Coach allows play to run to test recognition under realism.
Coaching Focus
- Team recognising triggers together
- Collective jump on the moment
- Decision to counter-press or recover if trigger isn’t there
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes whether players naturally wait for and attack triggers.
Coaching Focus
- Players resisting forced presses
- Clear collective reaction on triggers
- Recovery shape when counter-press is not on
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently recognises and reacts to counter-press triggers
Technical / Tactical Detail | Body shape cues, press timing, cover and balance
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Wait for the trigger.” • “Closed—go.” • “Return—step.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing press → “No trigger, recover.” • Late support → “Jump together.”
Praise Language | “Great trigger recognition.” • “That was smart pressure.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, collective reaction, communication
Reflection Question | “What told you the counter-press was on?”
Session Design Note | Clear triggers prevent chaotic pressing in transition moments
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: trigger, counter-press, recover

(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Defence | Week 2 | Compact Behind
Session Outcomes
Recover quickly behind the ball after loss, forming a compact shape to protect central space before pressing again.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 5v5 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned centrally
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On loss of possession, the team that lost the ball must recover behind the ball before any pressing is allowed.
- Defending team scores by forcing the opponent to play backwards or by winning the ball after recovery.
- Key rule: All players must be goal-side of the ball within 4 seconds before pressing.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate sprint recovery
- Central protection first
- Compact distances behind the ball
Progression
- Reduce recovery time to 3 seconds
- Reduce grid size to increase pressure
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with team in possession)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team builds forward.
- On loss, the attacking team must recover into a compact shape behind the ball before engaging.
- Defending team can attack freely if recovery is slow or disorganised.
- Once compact, defenders can step to delay or regain.
- On regain, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Recovery angles toward the centre
- Line distances once recovered
- Clear communication to organise shape
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow pressing immediately once compact shape is clearly formed
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Lose the ball
- Recover compactly behind it
- Then delay or regain possession in the middle third
- Coach allows play to flow to test realism.
Coaching Focus
- Collective sprint recovery
- Protecting central lanes
- Decision to hold or step once compact
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether teams naturally recover compactly behind the ball after loss.
Coaching Focus
- Players prioritising recovery over chasing
- Team compactness during transition
- Communication to organise quickly
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently recovers compactly behind the ball after loss
Technical / Tactical Detail | Recovery runs, central protection, line distances, communication
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Get behind it.” • “Protect the middle.” • “Then step.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Chasing forward → “Recover first.” • Central gaps → “Close inside.”
Praise Language | “Great recovery.” • “That shape protected us.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, discipline, awareness, collective effort
Reflection Question | “What did we protect first when we lost the ball?”
Session Design Note | Teaching compact recovery reduces vulnerability in transition moments
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: compact, recover, protect

(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Defence | Week 3 | Compact Recovery: Regain Shape Fast
Session Outcomes
Recover immediately after loss to regain compact team shape quickly and protect the central zone.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid
- Players: 5v5 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned centrally
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On loss of possession, the team that lost the ball must recover into compact shape within 4 seconds.
- No pressing is allowed until the team is compact and goal-side.
- Defending team scores by winning the ball after recovery and finishing into a mini goal.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate sprint recovery on loss
- Getting goal-side first
- Compact distances between teammates
Progression
- Reduce recovery time to 3 seconds
- Reduce grid size to increase pressure
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with team in possession)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team builds forward.
- On loss, the attacking team must recover into compact shape quickly.
- Once shape is set, defenders can step to delay or regain.
- If recovery is slow or stretched, attacking team can progress freely.
- On regain, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Speed and angle of recovery runs
- Central protection first, then pressure
- Communication to organise shape quickly
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow pressing immediately once compactness is clearly visible
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup

- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Lose the ball
- Recover into compact shape within 5 seconds
- Then delay or regain possession
- Coach allows play to run to test realism.
Coaching Focus
- Collective sprint recovery
- Protecting the central channel
- Decision to hold shape or step forward once organised
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules

Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe how quickly teams regain shape after losing possession.
Coaching Focus
- Players prioritising recovery over chasing
- Team compactness under transition pressure
- Communication and leadership during recovery

Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently regains compact defensive shape immediately after loss
Technical / Tactical Detail | Recovery runs, spacing, central protection, communication

Common Mistakes & Fixes | Chasing ball → “Recover inside.” • Stretched shape → “Squeeze together.”
Praise Language | “Great recovery.” • “That shape protected us.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, discipline, awareness, collective effort

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Transition to Defence | Week 3 | Protect the Middle
Session Outcomes
Immediately protect the central space after loss by recovering inside and denying forward penetration through the middle.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 25x20m central grid with a marked central lane
- Players: 5v5 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned centrally
Description
- Free possession inside the grid.
- On loss of possession, the team that lost the ball must recover to protect the central lane.
- Defending team scores by:
- Blocking a forward pass through the middle
- Or winning the ball after protecting the centre
- Key rule: No pressing allowed outside the central lane for the first 3 seconds after loss.
Coaching Focus
- Immediate recovery runs toward the middle
- Body shape to block central passing lines
- Communication to organise protection quickly
Progression
- Reduce grid size
- Reduce protection window to 2 seconds
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral (neutral with team in possession)
- Targets: End zones behind each team
Description
- Attacking team builds forward.
- On loss, the defending team must recover centrally first.
- Once the middle is protected, defenders can shift to delay or regain.
- If attackers play through the central zone before recovery, they score a point.
- On regain, defenders counter into the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Prioritising central space over ball chasing
- Staggered positions to screen passes
- Collective movement as the ball shifts wide
Progression
- Remove neutral (4v4)
- Allow pressing immediately once central compactness is clear
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, directional
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if they:
- Lose the ball
- Protect the middle within 4 seconds
- Then delay or regain possession
- Coach allows play to run to test decision-making.
Coaching Focus
- Team recovering inside as a priority
- Compact distances between lines
- Decision to hold the middle or step wide together
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally recover centrally after loss.
Coaching Focus
- Players protecting the centre before engaging
- Team staying narrow and compact
- Communication across units during recovery
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently protects the middle immediately after losing possession
Technical / Tactical Detail | Recovery runs inside, screening body shape, compact spacing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Inside first.” • “Protect the middle.” • “Compact now.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Chasing wide → “Get inside.” • Central gaps → “Close the middle.”
Praise Language | “Great protection.” • “You closed the centre.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, discipline, communication, collective effort
Reflection Question | “What space did we protect first after losing the ball?”
Session Design Note | Central protection reduces danger and buys time to reorganise
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: protect middle, recover, compact

heading: U14 Cycle 8, week 1, session 1
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 8, week 1, session 1
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Switch of Play & Width Management | Week 1 | Recognise When to Switch: Use Pivot
Session Outcomes
Recognise pressure and switch play through the pivot to access free space on the opposite side.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m, split into three vertical lanes (wide–central–wide)
- Players: 5v3 inside (one defender starts passive)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned wide at opposite ends
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball.
- Central lane contains a designated pivot who plays one- or two-touch.
- When pressure builds on one side, possession team must find the pivot to switch play.
- Defenders score by winning the ball and finishing into a mini goal.
- Key rule: Goals count only if the attack uses the pivot to switch lanes.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning before receiving
- Body shape to play across the pitch
- Speed and accuracy of the pivot pass
Progression
- Activate all defenders
- Limit pivot to one touch
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional, three vertical zones
- Players: 4v4 + 1 pivot (neutral, plays with possession team)
- Targets: End zones or mini goals wide
Description
- Attacking team builds in one wide zone.
- Defenders apply pressure to overload the ball side.
- Attacking team must recognise pressure, find the pivot, and switch to the far side.
- On regain, defenders counter toward the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when the ball side is locked
- Timing and angle of the pass into the pivot
- Movement to receive on the far side after the switch
Progression
- Remove the neutral (pivot becomes fixed role)
- Allow defenders to jump the pivot to increase realism
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, full width
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- They switch play through a central pivot
- The switch leads to penetration or a shot
- Coach allows flow to encourage recognition in real time.
Coaching Focus
- Team shape to create a central pivot option
- Patience to switch rather than force play
- Width held on the far side to receive the switch
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally use the pivot to switch when under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising pressure cues
- Pivot positioning and availability
- Team maintaining width away from the ball
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team recognises pressure and switches play effectively through the pivot
Technical / Tactical Detail | Scanning, body shape, pass weight, pivot positioning, width timing
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Can we switch?” • “Find the pivot.” • “Change the picture.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing ball-side play → “Reset through the pivot.” • Static far side → “Hold your width.”
Praise Language | “Great recognition.” • “That switch opened the game.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, movement off the ball, bravery to switch
Reflection Question | “What told you it was time to switch?”
Session Design Note | Central pivot provides clarity before moving to full free play
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: pivot, switch, width
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent
(Light-blue separator line above; Segoe UI 8 pt grey text)

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Switch of Play & Width Management | Week 1 | Open Ball Scan
Session Outcomes
Recognise the open ball early through scanning and body shape, allowing quicker forward or switching decisions.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m, three vertical lanes (wide–central–wide)
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned wide
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball freely.
- Defenders apply light-to-moderate pressure.
- Players score by finding an open-side teammate who receives on the open ball and plays forward or across.
- Key rule: Receiver must scan before the ball arrives; first touch must face away from pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning before receiving
- Body shape to receive on the open side
- Speed of decision after first touch
Progression
- Increase defender pressure
- Limit possession players to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional, three vertical zones
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral pivot
- Targets: End zones or mini goals wide
Description
- Attacking team builds through one side.
- Defenders shift to overload the ball side.
- Attacking players must scan early and receive on the open ball to:
- Play forward
- Or switch through the pivot
- On regain, defenders counter toward the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Scanning timing (before the pass)
- First touch away from pressure
- Recognising forward vs switch options
Progression
- Remove the neutral
- Allow defenders to jump passing lanes earlier
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, full width
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- A player receives on the open ball
- And the next action breaks a line or switches play
- Coach allows flow to encourage natural recognition.
Coaching Focus
- Team shape to create open-ball moments
- Speed of collective movement after the open ball
- Width held on the far side
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally scan and receive on the open ball.
Coaching Focus
- Players checking shoulders consistently
- First touch setting up next action
- Team recognising and using open-ball moments
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently recognises and uses open-ball moments through scanning
Technical / Tactical Detail | Scanning frequency, body shape, first touch direction, decision speed
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Scan early.” • “Open body.” • “Play what you see.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Closed body → “Open up.” • Late scan → “Check before it comes.”
Praise Language | “Great scan.” • “That open touch changed the picture.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, bravery to receive, quick decisions, movement
Reflection Question | “What did you see before the ball arrived?”
Session Design Note | Reduced pressure early supports scanning habits before realism
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: open ball, scan, switch

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Switch of Play & Width Management | Week 2 | Width and Depth: Stretch to Control
Session Outcomes
Use width and depth together to stretch the opponent, control space, and create clearer passing options.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m, three vertical lanes (wide–central–wide)
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned wide at opposite ends
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball.
- Wide lanes must be occupied when in possession.
- Defenders work to stay compact centrally.
- Key rule: A point is scored only if the ball is played from one wide lane to the other through the central zone.
- Emphasis on stretching the pitch before penetrating.
Coaching Focus
- Holding width away from the ball
- Creating depth with forward and backward options
- Patience to move the opponent before attacking
Progression
- Activate all defenders
- Limit central players to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional, three vertical zones
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral pivot
- Targets: End zones or mini goals on wide sides
Description
- Attacking team builds with width and depth:
- One player high
- One player underneath
- Width maintained on both sides
- Defenders stay compact and shift together.
- Attacking team must stretch the block before penetrating or switching.
- On regain, defenders counter toward the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Distance between wide players
- Timing of depth runs (when to stay, when to go)
- Central player’s role in connecting both sides
Progression
- Remove neutral (pivot becomes fixed role)
- Allow defenders to press higher to increase realism
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, full width
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- They stretch the opponent with width and depth
- Then penetrate or switch successfully
- Coach allows play to flow to encourage recognition.
Coaching Focus
- Team shape in possession
- Width held even when ball is central
- Depth options supporting the ball carrier
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally use width and depth to control the game.
Coaching Focus
- Players staying wide and deep at the right moments
- Team recognising when the opponent is stretched
- Collective patience before attacking
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently uses width and depth to stretch and control the opponent
Technical / Tactical Detail | Spacing, timing of depth runs, central connections, decision speed
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Stretch them.” • “Hold the width.” • “Give depth.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Everyone coming short → “Keep one high.” • Narrow shape → “Use the width.”
Praise Language | “Great spacing.” • “You opened the pitch.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, movement off the ball, bravery
Reflection Question | “How did our width help us control the game?”
Session Design Note | Clear positional roles help players understand spacing before full freedom
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: width, depth, stretch

heading: U14 Cycle 8, week 2, session 2
headingPath: U14 sessions > U14 Cycle 8, week 2, session 2
content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Switch of Play & Width Management | Week 2 | Circulate to Create
Session Outcomes
Circulate the ball with purpose to move the opponent, create free players, and open space to penetrate or switch.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m, three vertical lanes (wide–central–wide)
- Players: 5v3 inside
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned wide
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball across the lanes.
- Defenders stay compact and shift together.
- Key rule: A point is scored only after the ball has moved through all three lanes before an attempt on goal.
- Encourage patience and tempo changes to move defenders.
Coaching Focus
- Quick but controlled circulation
- Body shape to play one- and two-touch
- Recognising when the opponent is stretched
Progression
- Activate all defenders
- Limit possession players to 2 touches
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional, three vertical zones
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral pivot (plays with possession team)
- Targets: End zones or mini goals wide
Description
- Attacking team circulates to draw pressure on one side.
- Objective is to move the block, then find:
- A free player between lines
- Or a switch to the far side
- Pivot supports circulation and resets when play is locked.
- On regain, defenders counter toward the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Speed of circulation vs speed of penetration
- Timing of the pass that breaks the line
- Movement off the ball to create the free player
Progression
- Remove the neutral (pivot becomes fixed role)
- Allow defenders to jump passing lanes earlier
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, full width
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- They circulate the ball from one side to the other
- Then penetrate or switch effectively
- Coach allows flow to encourage real-time recognition.
Coaching Focus
- Team patience in possession
- Width held while circulating
- Decision to continue circulation or attack the moment
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally circulate to create space rather than forcing play.
Coaching Focus
- Players recognising when the opponent is set
- Team willingness to reset and move the ball again
- Collective understanding of when to speed up or slow down
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team circulates the ball with intent to create space and free players
Technical / Tactical Detail | Pass speed, body shape, off-ball movement, tempo control
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Move them.” • “Be patient.” • “Create the free man.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing play → “Circulate again.” • Static off-ball → “Move to help.”
Praise Language | “Great patience.” • “You moved them first.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, movement, bravery to reset
Reflection Question | “How did moving the ball help create space?”
Session Design Note | Circulation habits build control before penetration
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: circulate, create, free player

Use circulation and side-to-side movement to change tempo, break defensive lines, and progress with control.

- Recognising when the opponent is shifting late

- Attacking team circulates to draw pressure to one side.

- Forward pass

Coaching Focus

- Remove scoring incentive

Setup

Element | Detail

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 8 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 2
(Segoe UI 9 pt grey text, Belco-blue underline)
U14 | Switch of Play & Width Management | Week 3 | Final Third Entry
Session Outcomes
Enter the final third with control by using width, timing, and the right moment to penetrate after circulation or a switch.
Activation / Intro (10–15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x20m, divided into two zones (middle third → final third)
- Players: 5v3 in the middle zone
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals in the final-third zone
Description
- Possession team circulates the ball in the middle zone.
- To enter the final third, the ball must be played forward with intent (pass or carry).
- Once in the final third, attackers have 5 seconds to finish.
- Key rule: Entry pass must be played when the receiver is facing forward or arriving on the move.
Coaching Focus
- Patience before the entry
- Timing of the forward pass or carry
- Supporting runs ahead of the ball
Progression
- Reduce space between zones
- Add a recovering defender once the ball enters the final third
Technical / Small-Group (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 40x30m, directional, three vertical lanes
- Players: 4v4 + 1 neutral pivot (plays with possession team)
- Targets: End zone or mini goal in the final third
Description
- Attacking team circulates to move the block.
- Objective is to recognise the right moment to enter the final third through:
- A split pass
- A switch followed by a forward action
- A carry into space
- Pivot helps reset play if the entry is blocked.
- On regain, defenders counter toward the opposite end zone.
Coaching Focus
- Recognising when the opponent is unbalanced
- Quality and weight of the entry pass
- Movement of players ahead of the ball to receive between or beyond lines
Progression
- Remove the neutral (pivot becomes fixed role)
- Limit attackers to 2 touches in the middle third
Tactical / Group Game (20–30 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, full width
- Players: 7v7 + GK
- Goals: Normal goals
Description
- Normal game flow with quick restarts.
- Team scores double if:
- They enter the final third under control (not forced)
- The entry leads to a shot or clear chance
- Coach allows play to flow to test recognition under pressure.
Coaching Focus
- Team shape before entry (width and depth)
- Decision to enter vs recycle
- Collective movement to support the attack once inside
Progression
- Remove scoring incentive
- Play fully normal rules
Transfer / End Game (15–25 mins)
Setup
- Directional game, maximum 7v7
- Full width, appropriate length
- Normal goals and rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Observe whether players naturally choose the right moments to enter the final third.
Coaching Focus
- Players resisting forced entries
- Team recognising space and timing
- Balance between patience and penetration
Coaching Information (U14)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Team consistently enters the final third with control and collective support
Technical / Tactical Detail | Pass weight, timing of runs, spacing before entry, decision-making
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Wait… now go.” • “Enter with purpose.” • “Support the entry.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forced pass → “Recycle and move them.” • Static runs → “Arrive on the move.”
Praise Language | “Great timing.” • “That was a smart entry.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, movement off the ball, bravery to reset
Reflection Question | “What told you it was the right moment to enter?”
Session Design Note | Final-third entry comes from control, not speed alone
Language Note | Reinforce Belco terms: entry, circulate, support
