BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
U15 | Positional Play in Progression | Week 1 | Maintain Triangles
Session Outcomes
Maintain clear triangles around the ball to support progression and control under pressure.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, rectangular, directional
- Players: 5v3 + GK (possession team overload)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on end line
Description
- Play starts with GK. Possession team scores by progressing into either mini goal.
- Defending team presses to win and counter into a central end zone.
- Overload supports early success in forming and holding triangles around the ball.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Ball always has two clear supporting angles
- Distances allow quick one- or two-touch play
- Team shifts together to keep triangles alive as the ball moves
Individual Cues
- Move early to complete the triangle
- Adjust position to stay open to the ball
- Play secure if support is late
Progression
- Reduce space to increase tempo and pressure on triangle quality
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, full width
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (soft overload)
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter on regain
Description
- Possession team attacks the big goal; opposition attacks mini goals.
- Neutral supports the team in possession, helping maintain triangles between lines.
- Focus on keeping triangles connected while progressing forward.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Triangles stay staggered, not flat
- Support adjusts as the ball moves forward
- Team remains compact to protect the ball and next pass
Individual Cues
- Scan to see where the triangle will form next
- Don’t stand on the same line as the ball
- Take responsibility to reposition after passing
Progression
- Remove the neutral to move toward equality
- Add a 6-second rule to attack after breaking a line
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team progresses through at least two clear triangles before scoring.
- Game demands real-time maintenance of triangles under match pressure.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team maintains triangle support as play speeds up
- Compact shape allows triangles to reform quickly
- Progression happens through connection, not isolation
Individual Cues
- Receive side-on to keep options open
- Reposition immediately after releasing the ball
- Stay disciplined to protect triangle structure
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players naturally forming and maintaining triangles under pressure
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Maintain triangles to support progression and control under pressure
Technical / Tactical Detail | Support angles, spacing, body shape, timing of movement, compactness
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Find the triangle.” • “Compact to attack.” • “Control the game.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Flat support → “Change your line.” • Ball isolated → “Where’s the third man?”
Praise Language | “Good angles.” • “Well connected.” • “Calm progression.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, movement, communication, collective responsibility
Reflection Question | “What helped us keep the triangle alive under pressure?”
Session Design Note | Overloads early support clarity; realism increases toward the end
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition

U15 | Positional Play in Progression | Week 1 | Structure in Possession – Create Overload
Session Outcomes
Create and use overloads in possession to progress forward with control and collective support.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, rectangular, directional
- Players: 5v3 + GK (possession team overload)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on end line
Description
- Play starts with GK. Possession team scores by playing into either mini goal.
- Defending team presses to win and counter into a central end zone.
- Overload supports early recognition of spare player and calm circulation to create advantage.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team positions to give the ball a clear spare option
- Distances are short to allow quick support around the overload
- Ball moves toward the overload side to fix defenders
Individual Cues
- Scan early to find the free player
- Adjust position to stay outside defenders’ cover shadows
- Play forward when the overload is secured
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure and speed of decision-making
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, split into two vertical corridors
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (soft overload)
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain
Description
- Possession team attacks the big goal; opposition attacks mini goals.
- Neutral helps create overloads in the ball area.
- Team must recognise where the overload is and use it to progress.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team shifts together to support the overload
- Players behind the ball stay connected to protect transition
- Overload is used to move forward, not just keep possession
Individual Cues
- Move early to join or support the overload
- Receive side-on to play the next action
- Don’t force play away from the overload
Progression
- Remove the neutral to move toward equality
- Add a 6-second rule to attack after breaking pressure
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team creates and uses an overload in possession before scoring.
- Game demands collective recognition and timing under match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team stays compact to allow overloads to form naturally
- Ball is circulated to draw defenders into one area
- Progression happens once numerical advantage is clear
Individual Cues
- Take responsibility to join the overload
- Communicate to organise support
- Recover quickly if the overload breaks down
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism and free decision-making
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players creating and using overloads naturally under pressure
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Create and use overloads to progress play with control
Technical / Tactical Detail | Spacing, support angles, body shape, timing of movement, compactness
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Create the overload.” • “Compact to attack.” • “Control the game.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing play away from overload → “Where’s the spare?” • Team too spread → “Reconnect first.”
Praise Language | “Good support.” • “Well recognised.” • “Calm progression.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, movement, communication, collective responsibility
Reflection Question | “How did we recognise where the overload was?”

Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
U15 | Positional Play in Progression | Week 2 | Third-Man Runs
Session Outcomes
Use third-man runs to progress play through pressure with timing, connection, and control.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, rectangular, directional
- Players: 5v3 + GK (possession team overload)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on end line
Description
- Play starts with GK. Possession team scores by playing into either mini goal.
- Defending team presses to win and counters into a central end zone.
- Overload supports early recognition of the third player arriving beyond pressure.
- Focus on short connections to trigger a third-man action.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Ball-near players stay connected to invite pressure
- Third player positions beyond the press, ready to move on the pass
- Team stays compact so the third-man option is reachable
Individual Cues
- Pass and move immediately to create space
- Time the run to arrive as the ball is released
- Scan to see where the next pass will be played
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure and speed of timing
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (soft overload)
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain
Description
- Possession team attacks the big goal; opposition attacks mini goals.
- Neutral supports the team in possession, helping create third-man options between lines.
- Focus on linking two short passes to release the third player forward.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- First pass draws pressure; second sets the third-man release
- Team stays staggered, not flat, to allow forward movement
- Tempo increases only once the third man is released
Individual Cues
- Set the ball with intent, not just safety
- Move off the ball to create the third-man lane
- Play forward quickly once the run is made
Progression
- Remove the neutral to move toward equality
- Add a 6-second rule to attack after the third-man release
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team progresses through a clear third-man run before scoring.
- Game demands recognition, timing, and trust under match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team maintains triangles to support third-man actions
- Compact shape allows quick support after the release
- Progression comes from connection, not individual dribbling
Individual Cues
- Receive side-on to play the next pass quickly
- Commit to the run once the trigger pass is played
- Recover quickly if the third-man action breaks down
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism and free decision-making
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players naturally using third-man runs to progress under pressure
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Use third-man runs to progress play through pressure
Technical / Tactical Detail | Timing of movement, support angles, body shape, tempo change, compactness
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Set it, then go.” • “Find the third.” • “Compact to attack.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Early run → “Wait for the pass.” • Flat support → “Change your line.”
Praise Language | “Good timing.” • “Well connected.” • “Brave movement.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, unselfish runs, communication, collective responsibility
Reflection Question | “What told you the third-man run was on?”
Session Design Note | Overloads early support clarity; realism increases toward the end
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
U15 | Positional Play in Progression | Week 2 | Break Lines Through Cooperation

Break opposition lines through coordinated movement, timing, and support between players.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, rectangular, directional
- Players: 5v3 + GK (possession team overload)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on end line
Description
- Play starts with GK. Possession team scores by playing through either mini goal.
- Defending team presses to win and counters into a central end zone.
- Overload supports early success in combining short passes and movement to break the first line.
- Focus on two-player cooperation creating space for the next action.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Ball-near players stay close to support combinations
- Team shape stays compact so breaking actions are connected
- Forward movement is timed off the pass, not before it
Individual Cues
- Pass with intent to draw a defender
- Move immediately after passing to support or break
- Scan to recognise when a line can be played through
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure and decision speed
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional

- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain
Description
- Possession team attacks the big goal; opposition attacks mini goals.
- Neutral supports combinations between lines.
- Emphasis on wall passes, third-player support, and coordinated movement to break pressure.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- First action attracts pressure; second action breaks the line
- Team stays staggered to offer options ahead of the ball
- Tempo increases only once the line is broken
Individual Cues
- Set the ball cleanly for the next player
- Time movement to arrive as the pass is played
- Play forward quickly once space is created
Progression
- Remove the neutral to move toward equality
- Add a 6-second rule to attack after breaking a line
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team breaks a line through a clear cooperative action before scoring.
- Game demands timing, trust, and coordination under match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team maintains compact spacing to support combinations
- Breaking actions are followed by immediate support
- Progression comes from cooperation, not isolated actions
Individual Cues
- Receive side-on to play the next action
- Commit to movement once the trigger pass is played
- Recover quickly if the combination breaks down
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players breaking lines through cooperation and timing under pressure
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Break opposition lines through coordinated actions and support
Technical / Tactical Detail | Passing weight, timing of movement, support angles, compactness
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Connect to break.” • “Play together.” • “Compact to attack.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forcing individual play → “Who’s with you?” • Early runs → “Wait for the pass.”
Praise Language | “Good connection.” • “Well timed.” • “Brave combination.”
Behaviours to Reward | Unselfish movement, communication, trust, collective responsibility
Reflection Question | “How did working together help us break the line?”
Session Design Note | Overloads early support clarity; realism increases toward the end
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 2 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1

Recognise when to slow the game and when to speed it up to progress with control as a team.

- Area: 30x25m, rectangular, directional
- Players: 5v3 + GK (possession team overload)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals on end line
Description
- Play starts with GK. Possession team scores by playing into either mini goal.
- Defending team presses to win and counters into a central end zone.
- Possession team circulates calmly, then accelerates once pressure commits.
- Overload supports early recognition of rhythm changes without rushing.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team stays compact to allow calm circulation
- Ball speed moves the press side to side
- Tempo change happens together, not individually
Individual Cues
- First touch sets the speed of play
- Scan to recognise when defenders step or hold
- Play brave when the moment to speed up is clear
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure and tempo demands
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, split into two horizontal zones
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (soft overload)
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain
Description
- Possession team attacks the big goal; opposition attacks mini goals.

- Remove scoring condition to increase realism

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
U15 | Final Third Decision-Making | Week 1 | Final Third Entries – Timing to Support
Session Outcomes
Enter the final third with correct timing of runs and support to maintain control and create clear chances.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m, directional, final-third orientation
- Players: 5v3 + GK (attacking team overload)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned inside an end zone
Description
- Play starts with GK. Attacking team progresses into the end zone and finishes in either mini goal.
- Defending team presses and counters into a central target zone on regain.
- Overload supports early success in recognising when to enter the final third and when to hold.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Ball carrier has at least two supporting options before entry
- Team stays compact behind the ball to allow late support
- Entry happens when support is moving, not standing
Individual Cues
- Delay your run to arrive as the ball arrives
- Scan to see defenders’ body shape before entering
- If support is late, secure and wait
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure and decision speed near the box
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, split into middle and final-third zones
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (attacking support)
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain
Description
- Attacking team must enter the final third from the middle zone before scoring.
- Neutral supports timing and decision-making around the entry moment.
- Focus on coordinated movement: one carries, others support and arrive.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team recognises when defenders are retreating vs stepping
- Support players time movement to arrive after the entry pass
- Compact shape protects against counter after entry
Individual Cues
- Make the run after the pass, not before
- Arrive side-on to play or finish
- Recover quickly if the entry breaks down
Progression
- Remove the neutral to move toward equality
- Remove zone restriction once timing improves
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, full width
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team enters the final third with at least two supporting runs before finishing.
- Game demands timing, patience, and collective awareness under match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team waits for support before forcing entry
- Runs are staggered: one supports, one arrives, one balances
- Compactness behind the attack secures second balls
Individual Cues
- Choose when to carry vs when to release
- Commit to the run once the entry is made
- Stay composed in the final action
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players timing support runs and entering the final third with control

Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Enter the final third with correct timing and collective support
Technical / Tactical Detail | Timing of runs, spacing, body shape on entry, compact support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Wait, then go.” • “Arrive together.” • “Compact to attack.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Early runs → “Hold, then arrive.” • Forced entry → “Is support ready?”
Praise Language | “Good timing.” • “Well supported.” • “Calm entry.”
Behaviours to Reward | Patience, awareness, communication, intelligent movement

- Play starts with GK. Attacking team progresses into the end zone and scores in either mini goal.

- Overload supports early success in seeing multiple options before acting.
- Emphasis on pause, scan, decide before the final action.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Ball carrier always has support underneath and beside
- Team stays compact to allow short decisions near goal
- Movement creates choices, not rushed actions
Individual Cues
- Scan before receiving in the final third

- Choose pass, carry, or finish based on space—not habit

- Players must recognise whether to pass, combine, carry, or finish based on defender behaviour.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team creates multiple options around the ball
- Support adjusts as defenders step or retreat
- Decisions are backed by movement, not watching
Individual Cues
- Delay slightly to see the defender’s reaction
- Commit fully once the decision is made
- Stay calm if the first option isn’t on
Progression
- Remove the neutral to move toward equality
- Add a 6-second rule to finish after entering the final third

- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the correct decision is recognised and executed in the final third (pass, carry, or finish).
- Coach reinforces recognition, not just outcome.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team supports the decision with runs and balance
- Compactness behind the ball protects against transition
- Attacks stay connected through the final action
Individual Cues
- Receive side-on to see options
- Take responsibility to decide
- React immediately after the action—shot, pass, or loss
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players recognising space, pressure, and support to make good final-third decisions
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Recognise and execute the correct decision in the final third
Technical / Tactical Detail | Scanning, defender cues, timing of action, support positioning
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Scan first.” • “Choose the moment.” • “Commit to the decision.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Rushed action → “What did you see?” • Forcing shots → “Is there a better option?”
Praise Language | “Good decision.” • “Calm choice.” • “Well supported.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, composure, accountability, intelligent bravery

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
U15 | Final Third Decision-Making | Week 2 | Cut-Back or Cross? – Read the Picture
Session Outcomes
Recognise whether to cut back or cross based on defender position, support timing, and box balance.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m, directional, wide-channel emphasis
- Players: 5v3 + GK (attacking team overload)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals positioned inside a central end zone
Description
- Play starts with GK. Attacking team progresses wide and finishes in either mini goal.
- Defending team presses and counters into a central target zone on regain.
- Overload supports early success in arriving with support and reading the picture before the final pass.
- Encourage attackers to reach the wide area with runners arriving, not waiting.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Ball goes wide with at least two supporting runs inside
- Team stays compact behind the attack to allow cut-backs
- Decision (cross or cut-back) is made after defenders commit
Individual Cues
- Scan the box before the final action
- If defenders drop, cut back; if they hold central, cross early
- Deliver with accuracy, not power
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure and speed of decision
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, split into wide channels and central zone
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (attacking support)
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain
Description
- Attacking team must use a wide channel before finishing.
- Neutral supports timing and box balance.
- Players must decide: cut-back to arriving support or cross based on defender shape and numbers in the box.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Runners arrive in different lines (front post, penalty spot, edge)
- One player secures balance behind the ball
- Team commits together once the wide player shapes to deliver
Individual Cues
- Delay slightly to see defender movement
- Attack the box with purpose, not hope
- Finish first time when the picture is clear
Progression
- Remove the neutral to move toward equality
- Remove channel markings to increase realism
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, full width
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the correct decision (cut-back or cross) is made based on the picture in the box.
- Coach reinforces decision quality, not just goals.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team recognises when the box is set vs empty
- Support runs are timed, not rushed
- Compactness behind the attack protects second balls
Individual Cues
- Receive wide with body shape to see inside
- Choose cut-back or cross based on defender distance
- React immediately after delivery for rebounds
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players reading the picture and choosing cut-back or cross under match pressure
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Choose the correct final action wide based on defender shape and support
Technical / Tactical Detail | Scanning, delivery type, timing of runs, box balance, compact support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Read the picture.” • “Arrive to finish.” • “Compact to attack.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Blind crossing → “What did you see?” • Early runs → “Arrive, don’t wait.”
Praise Language | “Good picture.” • “Right choice.” • “Well supported.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, composure, unselfish runs, intelligent bravery
Reflection Question | “What told you to cut back or cross in that moment?”
Session Design Note | Overloads early support clarity; realism increases toward the end
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 3 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
U15 | Final Third Decision-Making | Week 3 | Combination to Finish – Quality over Rush
Session Outcomes
Use combinations in the final third to create high-quality finishing moments without rushing the action.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 35x25m, directional, final-third orientation
- Players: 5v3 + GK (attacking team overload)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals inside an end zone
Description
- Play starts with GK. Attacking team progresses into the end zone and scores in either mini goal.
- Defending team presses and counters into a central target zone on regain.
- Overload supports early success in short combinations near goal.
- Emphasis on connect first, finish second — no rushed shots.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Ball carrier always has close support for a combination
- Team stays compact to allow short passes and rebounds
- Finishing action happens after defenders are moved, not immediately
Individual Cues
- Play the simple pass to set the next action
- Delay the finish if the picture isn’t clear
- Scan before shooting to see support and space
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure and demand quicker but calmer decisions
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, split into middle and final-third zones
- Players: 5v5 + 1 neutral (attacking support)
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain
Description
- Attacking team must enter the final third before scoring.
- Neutral supports wall passes, third-man actions, and short combinations.
- Focus on two- and three-player combinations to create a clear finishing moment.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team recognises when defenders are set vs stretched
- Support players arrive around the ball, not ahead of it
- Combination speed increases only once the space is created
Individual Cues
- Set the ball cleanly for the next player
- Move after passing to stay involved
- Choose the finish once balance is broken
Progression
- Remove the neutral to move toward equality
- Add a 6-second rule to finish after entering the final third
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m, full width
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team uses a clear combination (wall pass, third-man, or lay-off) before finishing.
- Coach reinforces quality of the action, not speed.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team stays connected to allow combinations near goal
- One player sets, one supports, one finishes
- Compactness behind the attack secures second balls
Individual Cues
- Receive side-on to combine quickly
- Don’t force the shot — trust the next pass
- React immediately after the finish or loss
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
- Coach observes only.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players choosing combinations over rushed finishes under pressure

Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Create quality finishing moments through combinations
Technical / Tactical Detail | Short passing, timing of movement, finishing composure, compact support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Connect first.” • “Quality over rush.” • “Arrive together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Early shots → “Is there one more pass?” • Isolated finisher → “Where’s your support?”
Praise Language | “Good patience.” • “Well combined.” • “Calm finish.”
Behaviours to Reward | Unselfish play, composure, awareness, collective responsibility

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
U15 | High Press & Counter-Press | Week 1 | Press Triggers – Closed and Return Balls
Session Outcomes
Recognise closed body shape and return passes as press triggers and react collectively to win the ball or lock play in.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (build-up team) vs 3 pressers
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Play starts with the build-up team keeping possession and trying to score in either mini goal.
- Pressing team looks to win the ball and score immediately.
- Press triggers are closed body shape and return pass.
- Overload supports early recognition and coordinated pressing actions.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest player presses; others lock nearby options
- Team steps in together on the trigger, not one by one
- Distances stay short to prevent escape passes
Individual Cues
- Sprint on the trigger, don’t jog
- Angle the run to block the forward pass
- Stay balanced to react if the ball escapes
Progression
- Reduce space to increase speed and intensity of pressing decisions
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-press on loss
Description
- Build-up team attacks the big goal; pressing team attacks mini goals.
- Pressing team looks to jump on return balls and closed touches.
- If possession is lost, immediate 3-second counter-press is required.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- First presser goes hard; second and third lock the exit routes
- Team stays compact to trap the ball side
- If press fails, drop together and reset shape
Individual Cues
- Read the receiver’s body shape
- Be aggressive but controlled on the approach
- React instantly after losing the ball
Progression
- Add a 5-second rule to score after regaining possession
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the ball is won following a press trigger (closed body or return pass).
- Game demands recognition, timing, and collective commitment.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team squeezes up when the opponent plays backward
- Press is led by the ball-nearest player, supported immediately
- Compactness behind the press protects against long balls
Individual Cues
- Scan off the ball to see potential triggers
- Commit fully once the press is on
- Recover quickly if the opponent escapes
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players recognising press triggers and reacting together under match pressure
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Recognise press triggers and press collectively to regain possession
Technical / Tactical Detail | Pressing angles, distance control, body shape recognition, compactness
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Trigger—go.” • “Lock it in.” • “Compact to press.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Solo pressing → “Go together.” • Late reaction → “See the trigger.”
Praise Language | “Good jump.” • “Well locked.” • “Connected press.”
Behaviours to Reward | Aggression, communication, collective effort, intelligent timing
Reflection Question | “What told us it was time to press?”
Session Design Note | Overloads early support trigger recognition; realism increases toward the end
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
U15 | High Press & Counter-Press | Week 1 | Compact to Attack
Session Outcomes
Stay compact when pressing so the team can regain the ball and attack immediately with numbers and control.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, rectangular, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 pressers + 1 support presser (4 pressing)
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- Pressing team presses to win the ball and attacks the opposite mini goal immediately.
- Focus is on pressing together and staying close enough to attack quickly after regain.
- Overload supports early success in compact pressing and fast attacking reactions.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Pressing unit stays tight side-to-side and front-to-back
- Nearest player presses; others lock short options
- Team squeezes forward as the ball travels
Individual Cues
- Sprint to press, don’t drift
- Angle run to force play inside the compact block
- React forward immediately after the regain
Progression
- Reduce area to increase pressure and pressing speed
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate attack on regain; 3-second counter-press on loss
Description
- One team builds to the big goal; the other presses high.
- When the pressing team wins the ball, they must attack quickly while still compact.
- If the ball is lost after regain, immediate counter-press is required.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team stays close to the ball during the press to support the attack
- First pass after regain is forward or secure, not hopeful
- Players behind the ball step in to support the attack
Individual Cues
- Be ready to attack the moment the ball is won
- Choose the best first action, not the fastest
- Recover instantly if the attack breaks down
Progression
- Add a 5-second rule to score after regaining possession
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team wins the ball while compact and attacks immediately with support.
- Game demands balance between aggressive pressing and controlled attacking shape.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team squeezes up together when pressing
- Compact shape allows multiple attackers to arrive after regain
- If the first attack isn’t on, secure and build again
Individual Cues
- Scan early after regain to see support
- Don’t drift wide or deep alone
- Stay brave but connected
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for teams staying compact when pressing and attacking quickly with numbers after regain
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Press compactly and attack immediately after regaining possession
Technical / Tactical Detail | Pressing distances, support angles, first action after regain, compact shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Compact to attack.” • “Win it, then go.” • “Stay connected.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Team stretched → “Squeeze up.” • Rushed long ball → “Who’s with you?”
Praise Language | “Good squeeze.” • “Well supported.” • “Connected attack.”
Behaviours to Reward | Aggression, togetherness, quick reactions, intelligent decisions
Reflection Question | “How did staying compact help us attack after the regain?”
Session Design Note | Overloads early support compact pressing; realism increases toward the end
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

Common Mistakes & Fixes | Solo pressing → “Wait for the three.” • Late support → “Squeeze up.”
Praise Language | “Good trigger.” • “Well synced.” • “Connected press.”

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
U15 | High Press & Counter-Press | Week 2 | Line Coordination
Session Outcomes
Coordinate defensive lines to press, squeeze space, and protect the team shape collectively.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v4 (possession team) vs 4 pressers
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- Pressing team works as two connected lines (front + support).
- Focus is on stepping, holding, and squeezing together rather than individual pressing.
- Overload supports early success in line movement and spacing.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Lines move up and back together
- Space between lines stays short and compact
- Press starts from the front but is supported immediately behind
Individual Cues
- Scan to stay connected to your line
- Step when the line steps—don’t hesitate
- Hold position if the line holds
Progression
- Reduce area to increase speed of line movement and pressure
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-press on loss
Description
- One team builds toward the big goal; the other presses in coordinated lines.
- Focus on the front line pressing while the second line squeezes space behind.
- If the press is broken, team drops together and resets shape.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Front line sets the press direction
- Second line steps in to close gaps and intercept
- Team stays compact side-to-side and front-to-back
Individual Cues
- Adjust position constantly to stay connected
- Communicate when stepping or holding
- React instantly if the ball breaks the line
Progression
- Add a 5-second rule to score after regaining possession
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup

- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the ball is won through clear line coordination (front press + supporting squeeze).

Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Lines step and drop together
- Compact shape prevents passes between lines
- Regain leads to immediate, supported attack
Individual Cues
- Stay disciplined to the line role
- Don’t chase alone—trust the structure
- Recover quickly if the opponent escapes

- Remove scoring condition to increase realism

- Players: 7v7 including GKs

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 4 | WEEK 3 | SESSION 1
U15 | High Press & Counter-Press | Week 3 | Counter-Press to Regain
Session Outcomes
React immediately after loss to counter-press collectively and regain the ball in the attacking half.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 counter-pressers
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- When possession is lost, the team that lost the ball has 3 seconds to counter-press and win it back.
- If the counter-press succeeds, immediate attack to score.
- Overload supports early success in reacting together on loss.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest players react instantly on loss
- Team squeezes toward the ball to close space
- Counter-press is aggressive but organised
Individual Cues
- Sprint on loss—no complaints or pauses
- Angle run to block forward escape
- Stay balanced to react if the ball pops out
Progression
- Reduce area to increase intensity and reaction speed
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition: Mandatory 3-second counter-press on loss
Description
- One team attacks the big goal; the other defends and counters to mini goals.
- When the attacking team loses the ball, they must counter-press immediately.
- Focus on regaining before the opponent can turn or play forward.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest three react first; rest squeeze behind
- Team stays compact to limit passing options
- If regain isn’t possible, drop together and reset
Individual Cues
- Read where the ball will go next
- Be aggressive but controlled in the challenge
- Communicate to organise pressure
Progression
- Add a 5-second rule to score after a successful counter-press regain

Setup

- Players: 6v6 + GK

- All restarts from GK.

Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team squeezes up immediately on loss
- Passing lanes forward are blocked first
- Regain leads to quick, supported attack
Individual Cues
- React first, think second
- Commit fully once the counter-press is on
- Recover quickly if the opponent escapes

- Remove scoring condition to increase realism

- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules

- Free play with minimal intervention.

- Observation only
- Look for immediate reactions after loss and collective effort to regain possession

- If not, play continues normally.
- Overload supports early success in reacting quickly and acting together.

Collective Cues
- Nearest players go immediately on loss
- Team squeezes toward the ball to remove space
- First aim is to stop forward play, second is to regain

- One team attacks the big goal; the other defends and counters.
- When the attacking team loses the ball, they must counter-press for five seconds.
- Success is defined as either winning the ball or forcing the opponent to play backwards or wide.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest three react first; rest squeeze in behind
- Team stays compact to block central exits
- If regain doesn’t happen, drop together and reset
Individual Cues

- Be aggressive but controlled in duels

- Add a 5-second rule to score after a successful regain

Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team squeezes up immediately on loss
- Forward passing lanes are blocked first
- Compact shape allows multiple players to engage
Individual Cues
- First step is forward
- Commit fully once the counter-press is on
- Recover quickly if the opponent escapes

- Remove scoring condition to increase realism

- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs

- Observation only
- Look for immediate reactions on loss and collective effort in the first five seconds

- Compactness allows second actions if the first forward pass fails

- Be brave to play forward under pressure

- React instantly after regain or loss

- Remove scoring condition to increase realism

- Compact distances allow regain and quick attack

Control pressing tempo with discipline — knowing when to go aggressively and when to hold shape to protect the team.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 pressers
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.

- Focus on not over-pressing — recognising when to go and when to reset.
- Overload supports early clarity in tempo control.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Press starts together and ends together
- If the ball escapes, team drops quickly into compact shape
- Distances stay short so the team can press again
Individual Cues
- Sprint when pressing; recover quickly when beaten
- Don’t chase alone after the press is broken
- Stay switched on for the next trigger

- Reduce space to increase tempo changes and recovery demands

Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional

- One team builds to the big goal; the other presses.
- Pressing team must decide: go hard on triggers or hold shape when the picture isn’t right.
- Emphasis on repeating presses with discipline rather than one long chase.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team recognises when the press is on vs when to recover
- Lines stay connected during both pressing and recovery
- Shape is restored quickly after failed press
Individual Cues
- Read teammate movement before stepping
- Communicate “go” or “hold” clearly
- Stay patient — pressing is a team action

- Add a 5-second rule to score after regain

Setup
- Area: 55x40m

- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.

Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team controls press tempo across the game
- Aggression is balanced with structure
- Compactness allows repeated pressing moments
Individual Cues
- Don’t force the press when the team isn’t set
- Recover your position quickly after effort
- Stay focused even after failed presses

- Remove scoring condition to fully normalise behaviour

- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs

Description

- Observation only

- Defenders stay close side-to-side and front-to-back
- Central space is protected first; wide areas are shown
- Team shifts together as the ball moves
Individual Cues
- Stay goal-side and body-shape inside
- Don’t step out alone—wait for the line
- Communicate to organise the block
Progression
- Reduce space to increase pressure and speed of ball movement
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 wide mini goals
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 5 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
U15 | Defending Compactness & Depth | Week 2 | Depth Control – Read the Line
Session Outcomes
Control defensive depth by reading when to step, hold, or drop as a unit to protect space behind and in front.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v4 (attacking team) vs 4 defenders
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals placed behind the defensive line
Description
- Attacking team keeps possession and tries to play through or behind the defensive line to score in mini goals.
- Defending team works as a unit to control depth — stepping when pressure is on, dropping when the ball is free.
- Balanced numbers support early clarity in recognising when the line should move.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Line steps up when the ball is closed and pressure is applied
- Line drops together when the ball is open and forward threat exists
- Distances between defenders stay tight as the line moves
Individual Cues
- Scan constantly to see ball, opponent, and teammates
- Match your movement to the line, not the ball alone
- Communicate early: “step” or “drop”
Progression
- Reduce space behind the line to punish late or disconnected movement
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals placed behind the defensive line
- Transition: Immediate counter-attack on regain
Description
- Attacking team builds toward the big goal and looks to play through or over the defensive line.
- Defending team must read cues to control depth — stepping together to compress space or dropping together to protect behind.
- On regain, defenders counter to reinforce value of correct depth control.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Pressure on the ball dictates the line height
- Line stays connected to the midfield unit
- Team adjusts depth together, not one defender at a time
Individual Cues
- Delay the attacker to allow the line to organise
- Don’t drop early if pressure is on the ball
- Recover quickly if the line is broken
Progression
- Add a time limit for attackers to increase stress on defensive decisions
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Defending team scores double if they successfully control depth to stop a through ball or force play backwards.
- Game demands constant reading of ball pressure, opponent movement, and teammate positioning.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Line moves up and back together
- Depth is controlled to protect both space in front and behind
- Compactness is maintained while adjusting depth
Individual Cues
- Stay switched on to changes in ball pressure
- Trust the line and resist dropping alone
- Communicate clearly and early
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for defenders reading cues and controlling depth as a connected unit under match tempo

Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Control defensive depth by reading ball pressure and team position

Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Read the line.” • “Step or drop—together.” • “Protect space.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | One player dropping → “Hold with the line.” • Stepping without pressure → “Is the ball closed?”
Praise Language | “Good line.” • “Well timed step.” • “Connected drop.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, communication, discipline, collective responsibility
Reflection Question | “What told us to step or drop as a line?”

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 1
U15 | Transition Control (Both Directions) | Week 1 | Win → Play → Organise
Session Outcomes
After regaining the ball, play the first action with clarity and organise the team shape immediately to control the next phase.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 defenders
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals at each end
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- Defending team presses to win the ball.
- On regain, defenders must complete one controlled pass before attacking a mini goal.
- Focus is on win → secure → organise, not instant chaos.
- Overload supports early clarity in transition behaviour.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest players secure the ball on regain
- Team shape reforms quickly behind the first pass
- Support angles appear immediately around the ball
Individual Cues
- First action after regain is calm and purposeful
- Scan quickly before passing
- Recover position immediately after the pass
Progression
- Limit touches on the first pass to increase tempo without losing control
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition Rule: Mandatory secure pass before attacking after regain
Description
- One team attacks the big goal; the other defends.
- When defenders regain, they must play one pass to organise shape before attacking.
- If possession is lost again, immediate counter-press is required.
- Practice links regain, decision, and team organisation.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- First pass connects the team, not isolates the ball
- Players behind the ball step in to support
- Team shape becomes compact immediately after regain
Individual Cues
- Don’t force the first forward ball
- Protect the ball if support isn’t ready
- React instantly if the pass breaks down
Progression
- Remove the mandatory pass and observe if behaviours remain
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team regains, secures possession, and attacks with organised support.
- Game tests transition control at match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team stabilises shape immediately after regain
- Forward attack is supported underneath and behind
- Compactness allows recovery if the attack breaks down
Individual Cues
- First touch after regain protects the ball
- Choose clarity over speed
- Recover shape quickly after the action
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players securing the first action and organising shape in both transition directions
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Control the transition by securing possession and organising team shape
Technical / Tactical Detail | First pass after regain, support angles, compact recovery shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Win it.” • “Secure it.” • “Organise quickly.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forced forward ball → “Connect first.” • No support → “Get underneath.”
Praise Language | “Good first action.” • “Well organised.” • “Calm transition.”
Behaviours to Reward | Composure, awareness, teamwork, intelligent decision-making
Reflection Question | “What helped us control the moment after winning the ball?”
Session Design Note | Early structure supports clarity; realism increases toward the end
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 1 | SESSION 2
U15 | Transition Control (Both Directions) | Week 1 | Balance Attack Risk
Session Outcomes
Balance attacking risk after regain by choosing when to go forward and when to secure and reorganise.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 defenders
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals at each end
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- Defenders press to win the ball.
- On regain, defenders choose: play forward immediately or secure and reset before attacking.
- Overload supports early success in recognising risk vs control.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team reacts together on regain
- Support appears underneath the ball quickly
- Shape is rebuilt before high-risk actions
Individual Cues
- Scan immediately after winning the ball
- If the ball is open and supported, go forward
- If isolated, secure and wait for help
Progression
- Limit touches on the first action to increase decision speed
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition Rule: Choice-based — forward within 3 seconds or secure one pass first
Description
- One team attacks the big goal; the other defends.
- On regain, the defending team must read the picture and choose the right level of risk.
- If the forward option isn’t clear, they secure possession and organise before attacking.
- Immediate counter-press if possession is lost again.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team reads the picture together after regain
- Forward actions are supported immediately
- Compact shape protects the team if risk is taken
Individual Cues
- Don’t force the first forward ball
- Protect the ball if pressure is immediate
- React quickly if the decision fails
Progression
- Remove the choice rule and observe natural decisions
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team shows good risk balance after regain (secure when needed, attack when on).
- Game demands calm judgement under match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team stabilises before committing numbers forward
- Attacks are supported from underneath
- Compactness allows recovery after risky actions
Individual Cues
- Choose the best option, not the fastest
- Communicate intention to teammates
- Recover shape quickly after the action
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players balancing risk and control naturally in transition moments
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Balance attacking risk and control after regain
Technical / Tactical Detail | First decision after regain, support angles, compact recovery shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Read the picture.” • “Secure or go.” • “Control the game.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Forced forward play → “Who’s with you?” • Over-safe play → “Is it on?”
Praise Language | “Good decision.” • “Well balanced.” • “Calm choice.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, composure, communication, intelligent risk
Reflection Question | “What helped you decide whether to go forward or secure the ball?”
Session Design Note | Choice-based rules develop decision-making under realistic pressure
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
U15 | Transition Control (Both Directions) | Week 2 | Transition to Defence
Session Outcomes
React immediately after losing possession to delay, recover shape, and protect the central zone.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 defenders
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals at each end
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- When possession is lost, the team that lost the ball must immediately transition to defence.
- Objective is to delay the first pass forward and protect central space.
- Overload supports early success in recognising defensive transition roles.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest players apply immediate pressure to delay
- Team collapses toward the ball to protect the middle
- Shape reforms quickly behind the first defender
Individual Cues
- First reaction is defensive, not complaining
- Angle recovery run to block forward pass
- Communicate to organise who presses and who covers
Progression
- Reduce space to increase speed and stress of defensive transition
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition Rule: Mandatory 3-second defensive reaction on loss
Description
- One team attacks the big goal.
- On losing the ball, they must immediately delay and recover into a compact defensive shape.
- If the opponent breaks pressure, defending team drops together and protects the central lane.
- Practice links reaction, recovery runs, and shape.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- First defender delays; others recover inside
- Central space is protected before wide areas
- Team regains compactness quickly
Individual Cues
- Sprint to recover goal-side
- Don’t chase the ball past teammates
- Communicate early to organise recovery
Progression
- Add a 5-second rule for attackers to score after regain to increase pressure
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Defending team scores double if they delay the attack and force play wide within the first five seconds after loss.
- Game demands fast reaction, discipline, and collective recovery under match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Immediate squeeze toward the ball on loss
- Lines recover together to protect central space
- Delay buys time for shape to recover
Individual Cues
- First step is defensive
- Choose recovery run over risky tackle
- Stay disciplined once shape is restored
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for immediate defensive reactions, recovery runs, and compact team shape after loss
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Transition quickly to defence to delay and protect central space
Technical / Tactical Detail | Reaction speed, recovery runs, delaying, compact shape
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Lose it—defend.” • “Protect the middle.” • “Recover together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Slow reaction → “First step now.” • Chasing alone → “Recover inside.”
Praise Language | “Great reaction.” • “Good delay.” • “Well recovered.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, discipline, communication, collective responsibility
Reflection Question | “What helped us stop the opponent playing forward after we lost the ball?”
Session Design Note | Clear transition rules sharpen defensive reaction habits
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 6 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
U15 | Transition Control (Both Directions) | Week 2 | Counter-Press Organisation
Session Outcomes
Organise immediately after loss to counter-press together, block forward options, and regain or stabilise play.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 counter-pressers
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals at each end
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- On loss, the team that lost the ball must counter-press for 3 seconds.
- Aim is to organise pressure: one presses, others block exits.
- Overload supports early success in structure, not chaos.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest player applies pressure; others close short options
- Team squeezes toward the ball to reduce space
- First aim is to stop forward play, then regain
Individual Cues
- React immediately on loss
- Angle run to block the forward pass
- Stay balanced to adjust if the ball escapes
Progression
- Reduce space to increase speed and accuracy of organisation
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition Rule: Mandatory 3-second counter-press on loss
Description
- One team attacks the big goal; the other defends.
- When the attacking team loses the ball, they must counter-press with organisation.
- Success is defined as regaining the ball or forcing the opponent to play backwards or wide.
- If the counter-press fails, team drops quickly into compact shape.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest three organise the counter-press
- Passing lanes forward are blocked first
- Team stays compact to allow a second action
Individual Cues
- Read where the opponent’s first touch is going
- Communicate who presses and who covers
- Don’t over-commit if isolated
Progression
- Add a 5-second rule to score after a successful counter-press regain
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team regains possession through an organised counter-press.
- Game demands quick organisation, discipline, and collective decision-making.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team squeezes immediately on loss
- Counter-press has clear roles: pressure, cover, balance
- If regain isn’t on, team drops together and resets
Individual Cues
- First step is toward the ball
- Commit once the counter-press is on
- Recover position quickly if beaten
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for organised counter-pressing actions and quick recovery into shape
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Counter-press with organisation to regain or stabilise play
Technical / Tactical Detail | Reaction speed, pressing angles, blocking lanes, compact distances
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “Lose it—organise.” • “Block first.” • “Go together.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | All chase → “Roles.” • Late reaction → “First step now.”
Praise Language | “Well organised.” • “Good squeeze.” • “Connected press.”
Behaviours to Reward | Urgency, communication, discipline, collective effort
Reflection Question | “How did organising the counter-press help us control the transition?”
Session Design Note | Clear time rules sharpen organisation before intensity
Language Note | Belco Framework vocabulary used to support recognition
Belconnen United FC – Compact • Brave • Intelligent

- Players: 7v7 including GKs

Description

Element | Detail

Common Mistakes & Fixes | Chasing the ball → “Recover inside.” • Pressing too early → “Get shape first.”
Praise Language | “Great recovery.” • “Well organised.” • “Good discipline.”

U15 | Transition Control (Both Directions) | Week 4 | Rhythm Game

Control game rhythm through smart decisions in transition — knowing when to speed up, slow down, or reset shape.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 defenders

- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.

Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team reacts together on regain
- Support appears quickly underneath the ball
- Shape is organised before forcing tempo
Individual Cues
- Scan immediately after winning or losing the ball
- Speed up only if the picture is clear
- Slow the game if support is not set
Progression
- Limit touches on the first action to increase tempo awareness
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals

Description
- One team attacks the big goal; the other defends.
- On regain, the coach calls the rhythm:
- Fast = immediate forward attack
- Set = secure pass and organise shape before attacking
- Practice teaches players to adapt tempo without losing control.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team responds to rhythm cue together
- Forward actions are supported immediately
- Compact shape protects the team if tempo increases
Individual Cues
- Adjust speed of play to the moment
- Communicate intention early
- Recover quickly if the rhythm choice breaks down

Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the team shows clear rhythm control after a transition (fast when on, calm when not).
- Game challenges emotional control and decision-making under match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team recognises moments to accelerate or pause
- Shape stays connected through tempo changes
- Compactness allows repeated rhythm changes
Individual Cues
- Don’t rush because of pressure
- Be brave to slow the game when needed
- Recover shape quickly after actions
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to normalise rhythm behaviour
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players managing tempo naturally in both attacking and defensive transitions

Element | Detail

Behaviours to Reward | Composure, awareness, communication, intelligent risk

- Speed: attack quickly if the ball is open and support is ready
- Slow: secure one pass and organise shape if the picture is unclear
- Overload supports early success in recognising tempo cues.

- Team reacts together on regain

- One team attacks the big goal; the other defends.

- “Go” = immediate forward action
- “Set” = secure pass and reorganise before attacking

Collective Cues
- Team agrees the tempo quickly and acts together

- Team stabilises before accelerating
- Tempo changes happen together, not individually

BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 1
U15 | Game Management & Tempo | Week 2 | Situational Pressing
Session Outcomes
Recognise game situations and choose when to press aggressively and when to hold shape to control the game.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 pressers
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals at each end
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- Pressing team adjusts intensity based on coach-called scenarios (e.g. “protect the lead”, “need a goal”).
- Focus is on recognising when to press, not pressing all the time.
- Overload supports early clarity in pressing decisions.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team presses together when the moment is right
- Shape stays compact when holding
- Lines move up or drop together based on the situation
Individual Cues
- Scan to recognise the situation
- Don’t press alone — wait for the group
- Communicate intention clearly (“go” or “hold”)
Progression
- Reduce space to increase speed of pressing decisions
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition Rule: Coach calls scenario before each restart
Description
- One team builds to the big goal; the other defends.
- Based on the scenario, defending team decides whether to press high or stay compact and delay.
- On regain, defenders counter to reinforce value of correct pressing choice.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team adjusts pressing height together
- Pressing intensity matches the game situation
- Compactness is maintained even when pressing high
Individual Cues
- Recognise triggers within the situation
- Stay disciplined to the team plan
- Recover quickly if the press is broken
Progression
- Remove coach calls and allow players to recognise situations themselves
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Coach introduces live scenarios during play (scoreline, time remaining).
- Teams must decide when to press aggressively and when to manage space and tempo.
- Focus is on decision-making, not constant intensity.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team recognises pressing moments together
- Lines move up or drop as one unit
- Regains are followed by controlled play
Individual Cues
- Read the moment before committing
- Communicate to organise the press
- Stay calm under pressure and fatigue
Progression
- Remove scenarios and allow the game to flow freely
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players adjusting pressing behaviour based on game moments

Element | Detail

Technical / Tactical Detail | Pressing height, trigger recognition, compactness, communication

Common Mistakes & Fixes | Pressing blindly → “What’s the situation?” • Team split → “Move together.”
Praise Language | “Smart decision.” • “Good control.” • “Well organised.”

content:
BELCONNEN UNITED FC – SESSION PLAN | CYCLE 7 | WEEK 2 | SESSION 2
U15 | Game Management & Tempo | Week 2 | Recognise Triggers
Session Outcomes
Recognise key game triggers and respond collectively to press, hold, or reset tempo.
Activation – Uvodni dio / Game Activation (15 mins)
Setup
- Area: 30x25m, directional
- Players: 5v3 (possession team) vs 3 defenders
- Equipment: Balls, cones, 2 mini goals at each end
Description
- Possession team keeps the ball and scores in either mini goal.
- Defenders press to regain.
- Coach highlights common triggers during play (closed body shape, poor touch, return pass).
- On each trigger, defenders must react together — press if on, hold if not.
- Overload supports early clarity in recognising triggers without chaos.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team reacts together when a trigger appears
- Distance between players stays compact on the trigger
- If the trigger isn’t clear, team holds shape
Individual Cues
- Scan constantly for body shape and ball speed
- Commit once the trigger is recognised
- Don’t guess — wait for the cue
Progression
- Reduce space to increase speed and realism of trigger recognition
Situational Practice – Solving the Situation (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 45x30m, directional
- Players: 5v5
- Targets: Big goal vs 2 mini goals
- Transition Rule: Press only allowed on a clear trigger
Description
- One team builds to the big goal; the other defends.
- Defending team may only press aggressively when a clear trigger appears.
- If no trigger is present, team stays compact and delays.
- On regain, defenders counter to reinforce correct decision-making.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Nearest players identify the trigger first
- Team squeezes together on the press
- If press fails, drop and reconnect immediately
Individual Cues
- Recognise poor touches or closed receiving shape
- Angle runs to block forward options
- Communicate the trigger clearly (“go”)
Progression
- Remove restriction and observe natural trigger recognition
Situational Game – Collective Execution (25 mins)
Setup
- Area: 55x40m
- Players: 6v6 + GK
- Goals: Full-size goals with GKs
Description
- All restarts from GK.
- Condition: Goals count double if the ball is won following a recognised trigger (poor touch, closed body, return pass).
- Game demands awareness, patience, and shared understanding under match tempo.
Coaching Focus
Collective Cues
- Team recognises triggers at the same moment
- Press is aggressive but controlled
- Compactness behind the press protects the team
Individual Cues
- Stay switched on off the ball
- Commit fully when the trigger appears
- Recover quickly if the opponent escapes
Progression
- Remove scoring condition to increase realism
Free Play – Transfer Game (20 mins)
Setup
- Area: 60x45m
- Players: 7v7 including GKs
- Directional game with normal rules
Description
- Free play with minimal intervention.
Coaching Focus
- Observation only
- Look for players recognising triggers naturally and responding together
Coaching Information (U15)
Element | Detail
Objective / Outcomes | Recognise game triggers and respond collectively
Technical / Tactical Detail | Body shape reading, ball speed, press timing, compact support
Coaching Messages (Belco Voice) | “See the trigger.” • “Go together.” • “Hold if it’s not on.”
Common Mistakes & Fixes | Guessing the press → “Wait for the cue.” • Solo pressing → “Where’s your support?”
Praise Language | “Good read.” • “Well timed.” • “Connected reaction.”
Behaviours to Reward | Awareness, patience, communication, intelligent aggression
Reflection Question | “What told us it was the right moment to press?”
Session Design Note | Trigger-based rules sharpen shared decision-making
